full.text: ἀ- (before a vowel, ἀν-) negative, as in ἄ-γνωστος, ἄ-δικος.
ἀ-, ἁ- copulative, indicating community and fellowship, as in ἁ-πλοῦς, ἀ-κολουθέω, ἀ-δελφός.
An intensive force (LS, s. α), as in ἀ-τενίζω is sometimes assumed (but see Boisacq, see word).
Ἀαρών (Heb. אַהֲרוֹן), indecl. (in FlJ, -ῶνος), Aaron (Exo.4:14, al.): Luk.1:5, Act.7:40, Heb.5:4, 7:11, 9:4.

Ἀβαδδών (Heb. אֲבַדּוֹן, destruction; LXX, ἀπώλεια, only in Wisdom Lit., of the place of the ruined dead: Job.26:6, 28:22, 31:12, Psa.88:12, Pro.15:11), indecl.; in NT, Abaddon, the angel of the Abyss: Rev.9:11.
αβαρής, -ές (βαρός), without weight; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word) not burdensome: 2Co.11:9.
Ἀββά (T, -ᾶ), indecl. (Aram, אַבָּא, emphatic form of אַב = Heb. אָב, father), used in the phrase Ἀ. ὁ παήρ, Abba, Father (see Swete on Mk, I.with): Mrk.14:36, Rom.8:15, Gal.4:6.
Ἄβελ, (WH, Ἅ-), ὁ, indecl. (הֶבֶל), Abel (Gen.4:2-10): Heb.11:4, 12:24; αἵμα Ἄ., Mat.23:35, Luk.11:51.
Son of Rehoboam (3Ki.14:1): Mat.1:7.
A priest of the line of Eleazar (1Ch.24:3, 10): Luk.1:5.
Ἀβιάθαρ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. אֶבְיָתָר), Abiathar (1Ki.21:1): Mrk.2:26.
Ἀβειληνή (T, Rec. Ἀβι-), -ῆς, ἡ, (sc. χώρα), Abilene, a district in the Anti-Lebanon: Luk.3:1.
Ἀβιούδ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. אֲבִיָּהוּד), Abiud, Abihud: Mat.1:13.
Ἀβραάμ (Heb. אַבְרָהָם), ὁ, indecl. (in FlJ, Ἄβραμος, -ου; MM, VGT, see word), Abraham (Gen.17:5 al.): Mat.1:1, 2 al.
in cl., boundless, bottomless (e.g. ἄ. πέλαγος, ἄ. πλοῦτος Æsch.).

[In LXX (for תְּהוֹם, Gen.1:2, al.; exc. Isa.44:27, Job.41:22 for צוּלָה, מְצוּלָה, Job.36:16 for רַחַב) and] NT, as subst. (MM, VGT, see word), ἡ ἄ. (sc. χώρα), the abyss; (a) of the sea (Gen.1:2); (b) of the underworld, as the abode of the dead: Rom.10:7 (a paraphrase of Deu.30:13 LXX); as the abode of demons, Luk.8:31, Rev.9:1, 2, 11, 11:7, 17:8, 20:1, 3 (Cremer, 2).
Ἄγαβος, -ου, ὁ Agabus: Act.11:28, 21:10.
ἀγαθοεργέω, -ῶ, to do good, show kindness: 1 Ti 6:18 (Cremer, 8). ἀγαθουργέω, -ῶ, contracted form (rare, see WH, App., 145) of ἀγαθοερ- (which see), to do good: Act.14:17.

ἀγαθὀ-ποιέω, -ῶ (= cl. ἀγαθὸν ποιεῖν, εὐεργετεῖν), [in LXX: Num.10:32, Jdg.17:13 A, Zep.1:12 (יטב hi.), Tob.12:13 B, 1Ma.11:33, 2Ma.1:2 ;] to do good; (a) univ.: 1Pe.2:15, 20, 3:6, 17, 3Jo.11; (b) for another's benefit: Mrk.3:4 (T, ἀγαθὸν ποιῆσαι), Luk.6:9; (with) accusative of person(s), Luk.6:33, 35 (Cremer, 8).
ἀγαθοποιία, -ας, ἡ (ἀγαθοποιός), well-doing: 1Pe.4:19.
ἀγαθοποιός, -όν = cl. ἀγαθουργός, [in LXX, of a woman who deals pleasantly in order to corrupt, Sir.42:14 ;] doing well, acting rightly (Plut.): 1Pe.2:14 (Cremer, 8; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀγαθός, -ή, -όν, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] in general, good, in physical and in moral sense, used of persons, things, acts, conditions, etc., applied to that which is regarded as "perfect in its kind, so as to produce pleasure and satisfaction, . . . that which, in itself good, is also at once for the good and the advantage of him who comes in contact with it" (Cremer, 3): γῆ, Luk.8:8; δένδρον, Mat.7:18; καρδία, Luk.8:15; δόσις, Jas.1:17; μέρις, Luk.10:42; ἔργον (frequently in Pl.), Php.1:6; ἐ...

ἀγαθωσύνη (on the termination, see: ἁγιότης, and cf . WH, App., 152; MM, VGT, see word), -ης, ἡ (ἀγαθός), [in LXX for טוֹבָה, טוֹב, טוּב, only in Heb. bks. ;] goodness (representing "the kindlier, as δικαιοσύνη, the sterner element in the ideal character," AE, Eph., 5:9; on its relation to χρηστότης, see Tr., Syn., §lxiii): Rom.15:14, Gal.5:22, 2Th.1:11.

ἀγαλλίασις, -εως, ἡ (ἀγαλλιάω), [in LXX (most frequently in Pss. and often coupled with εὐφροσύνη, as Ps 44(45):15) chiefly for גִּיל ;] exultation, exuberant joy: Luk.1:44, Act.2:46, Heb.1:9, Ju 24; χαρὰ καὶ ἀ., Luk.1:14 (Cremer, 592).

ἀγαλλιάω, -ῶ, Hellenistic form of cl. ἀγάλλω, to glorify, mid. -ομαι, to exult in; [in LXX (most frequently in Pss.) chiefly for גיל, רנן pi. ;] to exult, rejoice greatly: before ἐπί, with dative, Luk.1:47; with dative mod., 1Pe.1:8, Rev.19:7. Mid., with same sense: Mat.5:12, Luk.10:21, Act.2:26, 16:34, 1Pe.4:13; before ἵνα, Jhn.8:56; ἐν, Jhn.5:35 (1 aor. pass. perh. as mid.; but see Mozley, Psalter, 5), 1Pe.1:6 (Cremer, 590).
ἄ-γαμος, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.16:9 ;] unmarried: 1Co.7:8, 32; fem. (= cl. ἄνανδρος), ib. 11, 34.

ἀγανακτέω, -ῶ (ἄγαν, much, ἄχομαι, to grieve), [in LXX: Wis.5:22, 12:27, Da TH Bel 28, 4Ma.4:21 ;] to be indignant: Mat.21:15, 26:8, Mrk.10:14, 14:4; before περί, Mat.20:24, Mrk.10:41; before ὅτι, Luk.13:14 (see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀγανάκτησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀγανακτέω), [in LXX: Est.18:3 א 1 ;] indignation: 2Co.7:11 (see MM, VGT, see word).
Of human affection, to men: τ. πλησίον, Mat.5:43; τ. ἐχθρούς, ib. 44; to Christ, Jhn.8:42; to God, Mat.22:37; with accusative of thing
Of divine love;

subs.text: God's love: to men, Rom.8:37; to Christ, Jhn.3:35;

full.text: Of men's love:

subs.text: to one another, Jhn.13:35;
to God, 1Jn.2:5.

full.text: Of divine love;

subs.text: God's love: to men, Rom.5:8; to Christ, Jhn.17:26;
Christ's love to men: Rom.8:35.

full.text: In pl., love feasts: Ju 12 (DB, iii, 157). SYN.: φιλία. ἀ., signifying properly (see: ἀγαπάω) love which chooses its object, is taken over from LXX, where its connotation is more general, into NT, and there used exclusively to express that spiritual bond of love between God and man and between man and man, in Christ, which is characteristic of Christianity. It is thusdistinct from φιλία, friendship (Jas.4:4 only), στοργή, natural affection (in NT only in compounds, see: ἄστοργος) and ἔρως, sexual love, which is not used in NT, its place being taken by ἐπιθυμία. (Cf. ἀγαπάω; and see Abbott, Essays, 70f.; DB, vol. i., 555; Cremer, 13, 593; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀγαπητός, -ή, -όν (ἀγαπάω), [in LXX chiefly for יָתִיד, יָדִיד ;] beloved (see M, Pr., 221); (a) by God: of Christ, Mat.3:17; of men, Rom.1:7; (b) by Christians, of one another: 1Co.4:14; frequently as form of address, ib. 10:14; opposite to ἐχθρός, Rom.11:28 (see AR, Eph., 229; Cremer, 17; MM, VGT, see word).
Ἅγαρ (Rec. Ἄ-), ἡ, indecl. (in FlJ, Ἀγάρα, -ης, Heb. הָגָר), Hagar (Gen.16): Gal.4:24,25.

ἀγγαρεύω (from the Persian; cf. Vg. angiare, and the Heb. אִגֶרֶת; on the orthogr., see Bl., §6, 1; M, Pr., 46), to impress into public service, employ a courier; hence, to compel to perform a service (prob. common in the vernac.; cf. Deiss., BS, 86f., MM, Exp., iv; VGT, see word): Mat.5:41, 27:32, Mrk.15:21.
ἀγγεῖον, -ου, τό (ἄγγος), [in LXX chiefly for כְּלִי ;] a vessel (see MM, VGT, see word): Mat.25:4.
ἀγγελία, -ας, ἡ (ἄγγελος), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁמוּעָה ;] a message: 1Jo.1:5, 3:11 (Cremer, 18; MM, VGT, see word).
a messenger, one sent: Mat.11:10, Jas.22:25.

As in LXX, in the special sense of angel, a spiritual, heavenly being, attendant upon God and employed as his messenger to men, to make known his purposes, as Luk.1:11, or to execute them, as Mat.4:6. The ἄ. in Rev.1:20-2:1, al., is variously understood as (1) a messenger or delegate, (2) a bishop or ruler, (3) a guardian angel, (4) the prevailing spirit of each church, i.e. the Church itself. (Cf. Swete, Ap)., in l.; DB, iv, 991; Thayer, see word; Cremer, 18; MM, VGT, see word)
ἄγε, prop. imperat. of ἄγω, come! used as adv. and addressed, like φέρε, to one or more persons: Jas.4:13, 5:1.
ἀγέλη, -ης, ἡ (ἄγω), [in LXX chiefly for עֵ֫דֶר ;] a herd: Mat.8:30-32, Mrk.5:11, 13, Luk.8:32,33.
ἀγενεαλόγητος, -ον (γενεαλογέω), without genealogy, i.e. without recorded pedigree (cf. Neh.7:64): Heb.7:3 (Cremer, 152; MM, VGT, see word).
unborn (Plat.);
of no family, ignoble, base (opposite to ἀγαθός, Soph., Fr., 105): opposite to εὐγενής, 1Co.1:28 (for exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word).
to dedicate, separate, set apart for God; of things: Mat.23:17,19, 2Ti.2:21; of persons: Christ, Jhn.10:36, 17:19.

to purify, make conformable in character to such dedication: forensically, to free from guilt, 1Co.6:11, Eph.5:26, Heb.2:11, 10:10, 14, 29, 13:12; internally, by actual sanctification of life, Jhn.17:17, 19, Act.20:32, 26:18, Rom.15:16, 1Co.1:2, 7:14, 1Th.5:23, Rev.22:11; of a nonbeliever influenced by marriage with a Christian, 1Co.7:14.
In the intermediate sense of ceremonial or levitical purification:

subs.text: of things, 2Ti.2:21;
of persons, Heb.9:13.

full.text: to treat as holy: Mat.6:9, Luk.11:2, 1Pe.3:16 (Cremer, 53, 602; MM, VGT, see word).
consecration;

sanctification: so strictly in Rom.6:19,22 (but see Meyer), 1Co.1:30, 1Th.4:3,7, 2Th.2:13, Heb.12:14, 1Pe.1:2. Elsewhere it perhaps (Ellic.; but see Milligan, Th., 48) inclines to the resultant state: 1Th.4:4, 1Ti.2:15 (Cremer, 55, 602).
Its highest application is to God himself, in his purity, majesty, and glory: Luk.1:49, Jhn.17:11, Rev.4:8. Hence

subs.text: of things and places which have a claim to reverence as sacred to God, e.g. the Temple: Mat.24:15, Heb.9:1;
of persons employed by him, as angels: 1Th.3:13; prophets, Luk.1:70; apostles, Eph.3:5.

full.text: Applied to persons as separated to God's service:

subs.text: of Christ, Mrk.1:24, Jhn.6:69, Act.4:30;
of Christians, Act.9:13, Rom.1:7, Heb.6:10, Rev.5:8.

full.text: In the moral sense of sharing God's purity: Mrk.6:20, Jhn.17:11, Act.3:14, Rev.3:7.

Of pure, clean sacrifices and offerings: 1Co.7:14, Eph.1:4. SYN.: ἁγνός, pure, both in ceremonial and spiritual sense; ἱερός (sacer), sacred, that which is inviolable because of its (external) relation to God; ὅσιος (sanctus as opposite to nefas), that which is based on everlasting ordinances of right. (Cf. Tr., Syn., §lxxxviii; DB, ii, 399 f.; Cremer, 34, 594-601; MM, VGT, see word)
Its highest application is to God himself, in his purity, majesty, and glory: Luk.1:49, Jhn.17:11, Rev.4:8. Hence

subs.text: of things and places which have a claim to reverence as sacred to God, e.g. the Temple: Mat.24:15, Heb.9:1;
of persons employed by him, as angels: 1Th.3:13; prophets, Luk.1:70; apostles, Eph.3:5.

full.text: Applied to persons as separated to God's service:

subs.text: of Christ, Mrk.1:24, Jhn.6:69, Act.4:30;
of Christians, Act.9:13, Rom.1:7, Heb.6:10, Rev.5:8.

full.text: In the moral sense of sharing God's purity: Mrk.6:20, Jhn.17:11, Act.3:14, Rev.3:7.

Of pure, clean sacrifices and offerings: 1Co.7:14, Eph.1:4. SYN.: ἁγνός, pure, both in ceremonial and spiritual sense; ἱερός (sacer), sacred, that which is inviolable because of its (external) relation to God; ὅσιος (sanctus as opposite to nefas), that which is based on everlasting ordinances of right. (Cf. Tr., Syn., §lxxxviii; DB, ii, 399 f.; Cremer, 34, 594-601; MM, VGT, see word)
ἁγιότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἅγιος), [in LXX: 2Ma.15:2 ;] sanctity, holiness, regarded, properly, as an abstract quality (see next word, and cf. Lft. Notes, 49; MM, VGT, see word): 2Co.1:12, Heb.12:10.

ἁγιωσύνη (cf. ἀγαθωσύνη), -ης, ἡ (ἅγιος), [in LXX: Psa.29 (30):4 96(97):12 (קֹדֶשׁ), 95(96):6 (עֹז), 144(145):5 (הוֹד), 2Ma.3:12 ;] holiness, the state in man resulting from ἁγιασμός, which see: Rom.1:4, 2Co.7:1, 1Th.3:13 (Cremer, 52; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀγκάλη,-ης, ἡ (ἄγκος, a bend), [in LXX for אַצִּיל, חֵיק ;] the bent arm: Luk.2:28 (cf. ἐναγκαλίζομαι).
ἄγκιστρον, -ου, τό (ἄγκος, a bend), [in LXX חַכָּה, etc. ;] a fish-hook: Mat.17:27.
ἄγκυρα, -ας, ἡ (ἄγκος, a bend), [in Sm.: Jer.52:18 ;] an anchor: Act.27:29, 30 40 ; figuratively (MM, VGT, see word), Heb.6:19.
ἄγναφος, -ον (= ἄγναπτος, γνάπτω, late form of κνάπτω, to card wool), uncarded, undressed, i.e. new (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.92:6, Mrk.2:21.
ἁγνεία (WH, ἁγνία), [in LXX for טָהֳרָה, נָזִיר, נֶזֶר, etc. ;] purity: 1Ti.4:12, 5:2 (Cremer, 58. For exx. of ceremonial use in π., see MM, Exp., iv).

ἁγνίζω (> ἁγνός), [in LXX always ceremonially, chiefly for קָדַשׁ ;] to purify, cleanse from defilement; (a) ceremonially: Jhn.11:55, Act.21:24, 26 24:18; (b) morally: Jas.4:8, 1Pe.1:22, 1Jn.3:3. SYN.: καθαρίζω, which see (and see: ἁγνός).
ἁγνισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (> ἁγνίζω), [in LXX: Num.6:5 (נֵזֶר) 8:7 19:17 (חַטָּאָה), etc. ;] purification: in ceremonial sense, Act.21:26 (LXX).

to be ignorant, not to know: absol., Ι Ti 1:13, Heb.5:2; with accusative, Act.13:27 17:23, Rom.10:3, 2Co.2:11; ἐν οἷς, 2Pe.2:12; before ὅτι, Rom.2:4 6:3 7:1, 1Co.14:38; οὐ θέλω ὑμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν, a Pauline phrase: with accusative, Rom.11:25; before ὑπέρ, 2Co.1:8; περί, 1Co.12:1, 1Th.4:13. ὅτι, Rom.1:13, 1Co.10:1 (for similar usage in π., V. MM, VGT, see word). Pass.: 1Co.14:38, 2Co.6:9, Gal.1:22.
not to understand: with accusative, Mrk.9:32, Luk.9:45.
ἀγνόημα, -τός, τό (ἀγνοέω), [in LXX: Gen.43:12 (מִשְׁגֶּה), Tob.3:3, Jdth.5:20, Sir.23:2 51:19, 1Ma.13:39 ;] a sin of ignorance (so in π.; see MM, VGT, see word): Heb.9:7.
ἄγνοια, -ας, ἡ (ἀγνοέω), [in LXX chiefly for אָשָׁם, שְׁגָגָה ;] ignorance : Act.3:17 17:30, Eph.4:18 (with sense of wilful blindness; cf. MM, VGT, see word), 1Pe.1:14.
free from ceremonial defilement, in a condition prepared for worship (for exx. of pagan usage, V. MM, VGT, see word).
holy, sacred, venerable (II Mac, l.with).
As in OT (cl.), pure, chaste, undefiled, guiltless;
ἁγνότης, -τητος, ἡ (ἁγνός), purity, chastity (cf. ἁγιότης): 2Co.6:6 11:3.
ἁγνῶς (ἁγνός), adv., purely, with pure motives: Php.1:17.
ἀγνωσία, -ας, ἡ (γινώσκω), [in LXX: Job.35:16 (בִּבְלִי־דַּעַת), Wis.13:1, 3Ma.5:27 ;] ignorance (opposite to γνῶσις): 1Co.15:34, 1Pe.2:15 (see Hort in l.; MM, VGT, see word).
ἄγνωστος, -ον (γινώσκω), [in LXX: Wis.11:18 18:3, 2Ma.1:19 2:7 (Cremer, 157) ;] unknown: Act.17:23 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
an assembly (Hom., Xen., al.).

a place of assembly, a public place or forum, a market-place (Hom., Thuc, al.; LXX): Mat.11:16 20:2 23:7, Mrk.6:56 (cf . MM, VGT, see word, ἀγυιά) 7:4 (Bl., §46, 7) 12:38, Luk.7:32 (Bl., l.with) 11:43 20:46, Act.16:19 17:7 (Cremer, 59; MM, VGT, see word).
to frequent the ἀγορά (Hdt., al.).
to buy in the market, purchase (Xen., al.; LXX; in π. very common in deeds of sale, see MM, VGT, see word): absol., Mat.21:12, Mrk.11:15; with accusative of thing

subs.text: , Mat.13:44, 46, al.; before παρά, with genitive of person

full.text: frequenting the ἀγορά , a lounger in the ἀγορά (Xen., al.): Act.17:5.
In late writers (Strab., al.), proper to the ἀγορά : ἀγοραῖοι (sc. ἥμεραι) ἀγονται (cf. Lat. conventus agere), court-days are kept, Act.19:38 (for exx. of both usages, see MM, VGT, see word).
the chase, a hunting or catching: Luk.5:4.
that which is taken, a catch: of fish, Luk.5:9.
ἀγραυλέω, -ῶ (ἀγραυλος, dwelling in the field; ἀγρός, αὐλή), to live in the fields: Luk.2:8.
to dwell in the field , (New Testament)
ἀγρεύω (ἄγρα), [in LXX: Job.10:16, Pro.5:22 6:25,26, Hos.5:2 (לקח ni., שׁחט, etc.) ;] to catch or take by hunting or fishing; metaphorically, Mrk.12:13.
of the wild olive (Anth.).
As subst., the wild olive: Rom.11:17, 24 (CGT, in 1.; MM, VGT, see word).
living in fields, wild: μέλι. Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6.
savage, fierce: Ju 13. (Cf. usage in π. of a malignant wound; MM, VGT, see word)
Ἀγρίππας, -α (Bl., §7, 2), ὁ Agrippa (II): Act.25:13, 22, 23, 24, 26 26:1, 2, 19, 27, 28, 32. (For Agrippa I, see: Ἡρῴδης, 3.)
a field: Mat.6:28, al.
the country: Mrk.15:21, al.; pl., country places, farms: Mrk.5:14 6:36 56, Luk.8:34 9:12.
= χωρίον a piece of ground: Mrk.10:29, Act.4:27, al. (On the occurrence of this word as compared with χώρα, χωρίον see MM, VGT, see word)

ἀγρυπνέω, -ῶ (ἄγρυπνος, seeking sleep ; ἀγρεύω, ὕπνος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁקד ;] to be sleepless, wakeful (Theogn., Xen., al.); metaphorically (LXX) = cl. ἐγρήγορα, to be watchful, vigilant: Mrk.13:33, Luk.21:36, Eph.6:18, Heb.13:17. SYN.: γρηγορέω, which see; νήφω, associated with γ. in 1Pe.5:8, expressing a wariness which results from self-control, a condition of moral, not merely mental alertness (see M, Th., I, 5:6).
ἀγρυπνία, -ας, ἡ (see supr.), [in LXX: Si .9, 2Ma.2:26 ;] sleeplessness, watching: 2Co.6:5 11:27. (Plat., Hdt.; for exx. in π., see MM, VGT, see word)
to lead, bring, carry: with ace, before ἐπί, εἰς, ἕως, πρός and simple dative; metaphorically, to lead, guide, impel: Jhn.10:16 Rom.2:4, Heb.2:10, 2Ti.3:6, al.
to spend or keep a day: Luk.24:21, Act.19:38
Intrans., to go: subjunc., ἄγωμεν, Mat.2:46, al. (Cramer, 61; MM, VGT, see word).
a carrying away.
a leading, guiding; metaphorically, training; hence, from the expression ἀ. τοῦ βίου, absol., conduct, way of life: 2Ti.3:10 (Cremer, 61; MM, VGT, see word).
a gathering, esp. for games.
a place of assembly.
a contest, struggle, trial; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word),

subs.text: of the Christian life as a contest and struggle: Php.1:30, 1Th.2:2, 1Ti.6:12, 2Ti.4:7 Heb.12:1;
solicitude, anxiety: Col.2:1.

full.text: a contest, wrestling (Eur., Xen.).
Of the mind, great fear, agony, anguish (Dem., Arist.): Luk.22:44 (cf . Field, Notes, 77 f. ; Abbott, Essays, 101 f. ; MM, VGT, see word).
to contend for a prize: 1Co.9:25,
to fight, struggle, strive: Jhn.18:36; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word). Col.1:29 4:12, 1Ti.4:10 6:12, 2Ti.4:7; with inf. (Field, Notes, 66), Luk.13:24 (Cremer, 609)
Ἀδάμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָדָם), Adam: Luk.3:38, Rom.5:14, 1Co.15:22,45, 1Ti.2:13,14 Ju 14. Christ ὁ ἔσχατος Ἀ., 1Co.15:45.
ἀδάπανος, -ον (δαπάνη), without expense, free of charge: 1Co.9:18.
Ἀδδεί (Rec. Ἀδδί), ὁ, Addei: Luk.3:28.
ἀδελφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀδελφός), [in LXX for אָהוֹת ;] a sister: Mat.19:29, al.; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word), of a member of the Christian community: Rom.16:1, 1Co.7:15 Jas.21:15, al.
lit. of a brother (Gen.4:2, al.).
Of a neighbour (Lev.19:17).
Of a member of the same nation (Exo.2:14, Deu.15:3). In NT in each of these senses (1. Mat.1:2, al.;
Rom.9:3) and also,

of a fellow-Christian: 1Co.1:1, Act.9:30. This usage finds illustration in π., where ἀ. is used of members of a pagan religious community (M, Th., I, 1:4; MM, VGT, see word). The ἀδελφοὶ τ. Κυρίου (Mat.12:46-49 13:55 28:10, Mrk.3:31-34, Luk.8:19-21, Jhn.2:12 7:3, 5 10 20:17, Act.1:14, 1Co.9:5) may have been sons of Joseph and Mary (Mayor, Ja., Intr. viff.; DB, i, 320ff.) or of Joseph by a former marriage (Lft., Gal., 252ff.; DCG, i, 232ff.), but the view of Jerome, which makes ἀ. equivalent to ἀνεψιός, is inconsistent with Greek usage. (Cremer, 66.)
abstract, brotherhood, brotherly affection (LXX).
Concrete, the brotherhood, the Christian community: 1Pe.2:17 5:9 (MM, VGT, see word).
unseen, unobserved, not manifest (Ps, l.with): Luk.11:44.
uncertain, indistinct: 1Co.14:8.
ἀδηλότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἄδηλος), uncertainty: 1Ti.6:17.
ἀδήλως (ἄδηλος), adv. uncertainly: of direction, 1Co.9:26.

ἀδημονέω, -ῶ (on the derivation, see MM, VGT, see word), [in Aq.: Job.18:20; Sm.: Psa.60 (61):3 115:2 (116:11), Ecc.7:17(16), Eze.3:15 ;] to be troubled, distressed (MM, l.with): Mat.26:37, Mrk.14:33, Php.2:26.
in Hom., Hades (Pluto), the god of the underworld.

the abode of Hades, the underivorld; in NT, the abode of departed spirits, Hades: ἐν τ. ᾅ., Luk.16:23; εἰς ᾅ., Act.2:27,31; πύλαι ᾅδου, Mat.16:18; κλεῖς τοῦ ᾅ., Rev.1:18; metaphorically, ἕως ᾅ., Mat.11:23, Luk.10:15; personified, Rev.6:8 20:13,14 (Cremer, 67, 610; MM, VGT, see word).
not to be parted, mixed, undistinguishable (cf. Pr, l.with, and see Lft., Ignat. Eph., §3).
without uncertainty (Hort, in l., but see MM, VGT, see word): Jas.3:17.
ἀδιάλειπτος, -ον (διαλείπω), unremitting, incessant: Rom.9:2, 2Ti.1:3. (For exx., see MM, VGT, see word)
ἀδιαλείπτως (see supr.), adv., [in LXX: 1Ma.12:11, II Mac .4, 3Ma.6:33 ;] unremittingly, incessantly: Rom.1:9, 1Th.1:3 2:13 5:17.
incorruption, uprightness , (New Testament)

etymology: from a derivative of a compound of (G0001) (G1311)

full.text: intrans., to be ἄδικος, do wrong, act wickedly or criminally: Act.25:11, 1Co.6:8, 2Co.7:12, Col.3:25, Rev.22:11; to do hurt, Rev.9:19.
Trans.
ἀδίκημα, -τος, τό (ἀδικέω), [in LXX for עָוֹן, פֶּשַׁע, etc. ;] a wrong, injury, misdeed (MM, VGT, see word): Act.18:14 24:20, Rev.18:5.
injustice: Luk.18:6, Rom.9:14.

unrighteousness, iniquity: Jhn.7:18, Act.8:23, Rom.1:18, 29 2:8 6:13, 2Ti.2:19, 1Jn.1:9 5:17; opposite to ἀλήθεια, 1Co.13:6, 2Th.2:12; to δικαιοσύνη ], Rom.3:5; ἀπάτη τῆς ἀ., 2Th.2:10; μισθὸς ἀδικίας, Act.1:18, 2Pe.2:13,15; ἐγράται τῆς ἀ., Luk.13:27; μαμωνᾶς τῆς ἀ., Luk.16:9; κόσμος τῆς ἀ., Jas.3:6; οἰκονόμος τῆς ἀ., Luk.16:8.
= ἀδίκημα, an unrighteous act: ironically, a favour, 2Co.12:13; pl., Heb.8:12 (Cremer, 261; MM, VGT, see word).
unjust: Rom.3:5, Heb.6:10.
unrighteous, wicked: Luk.16:11 18:11, Act.24:15, 1Co.6:1,9 1Pe.3:18; opposite to δίκαιος, Mat.5:45; to εὐσεβής, 2Pe.2:9; to πιστός, Luk.16:10 (Cremer, 200).
ἀδίκως, adv. (ἄδικος), [in LXX for שׁקר, עול, etc. ;] unjustly, undeservedly: 1Pe.2:19.
of things (prop. of metals: LXX, ll. with), not standing the test, rejected: γῆ, Heb.6:8.
Of persons, rejected after testing, reprobate: Rom.1:28, 1Co.9:27, 2Co.13:5-7, 2Ti.3:8, Tit.1:16 (Cremer, 212).
guileless (Pind., Thuc).

Of liquids (Æsch., Eur., and late prose writers), genuine, pure (in π. and in MGr. of wine, also of corn: MM, VGT, see word; Milligan, NTD, 77): of milk, metaphorically, 1Pe.2:2. SYN.: ἀκέραιος (which see), ἄκακος, ἁπλοῦς.
Ἁδραμυντηνός (T, Rec. Ἀδραμυττηνός), -ή,-όν, of Adramyttium, a seaport of Mysia: Act.27:2.
Ἁδρίας (T, Rec. Ἀδρίας), -ου, ὁ, the Adriatic sea, in later Greek usage extended to take in all the waters between Greece and Italy: Act.27:27.
thickness, vigour.
abundance, bounty: 2Co.8:20.
Abbott-Smith omits this number to be unable (cl., Philo; π., see MM, VGT, see word).
In LXX and NT (Kennedy, Sources, 124; Hatch, Essays, 4; Field, Notes, 46 f.), to be impossible: Mat.17:20, Luk.1:37.
of persons, unable, powerless: Act.14:8; figuratively Rom.15:1 (MM, VGT, see word).
Of things, impossible: Mat.19:26, Mrk.10:27, Luk.18:27, Rom.8:3, Heb.6:4,18 10:4 11:6.

ᾄδω (Attic form of Ion. and poët. ἀείδω), [in LXX chiefly for שִׁיר ;] to sing, (a) intrans., with dative (MM, VGT, see word), of praise to God: Eph.5:19, Col.3:16 ; (b) trans., with cogn. accusative: ᾠδὴν, Rev.5:9 14:3 15:3.
of continuous time, unceasingly, perpetually: Act.7:51, 2 Co.4.11; 6:10, Tit.1:12, Heb.3:10.
Of successive occurrences, on every occasion (MM, VGT, see word): 1Pe.3:15, 2Pe.1:12.

etymology: from an obsolete primary noun (apparently meaning continued duration);

full.text: ἀετός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX for נֶשֶׁר ;] an eagle: Rev.4:7 8:13 (Rec. ἀγγέλου) 12:14. Where carrion is referred to, ἀ. is probably a vulture (cf. Job.39:30, Pro.30:17): Mat.24:28, Luk.17:37 (MM, VGT, see word).

ἄζυμος, -ον (ζύμη), [in LXX for מָצָּה; τὰ ἄ. (sc. λάγανα, cakes) = הַמָצָּה ;] unleavened: ἡ ἑορτὴ τῶν ἄ. (הַג הַמָצָּה), the paschal feast (also called τὰ ἄ., Mrk.14:1), Luk.22:1; ἡμέρα τῶν ἄ., Mat.26:17, Mrk.14:12, Luk.22:7; ἡμέραι, Act.12:3 20:6. Fig., of Christians, free from corruption: 1Co.5:7; exhorted to keep festival, ἐν ἀζύμοις (sc. ἄρτοις, λάγανοις, or, indefinitely, "unleavened elements"), ib. 8 (Cremer, 724).
Ἀζώρ, ὁ, indecl., Azor: Mat.1:13,14.
Ἄζωτος, -ου, ἡ (Heb., אַשְׁדּוֹד), Azotus, a Philistine city: Act.8:40.

ἀήρ, ἀέρος, ὁ, [in LXX: 2Ki.22:12 (= Psa.17(18):11 שׁחק), Wi .8 ;] in Hom., Hes., the lower air which surrounds the earth, as opposite to the purer αἰθήρ of the higher regions; generally, air (MM, VGT, see word): Act.22:23, 1Th.4:17, Rev.9:2 16:17; of the air as the realm of demons, Eph.2:2; ἀ. δέρειν, of striving to no purpose, 1Co.9:26; εἰς ἀ. λαλεῖν, of speaking without effect, not being understood, 1Co.14:9.
ἀθανασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀ-θάνατος, undying; V. MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Wis.3:4 4:1 8:13,17 15:3, 4Ma.14:5 16:13 ;] immortality: 1Co.15:53,54 1Ti.6:16 (cf. Cremer, 285 f.).

ἀ-θέμιτος (late form of ἀθέμιστος, LS, MM, VGT, see word), -ον (θέμις, custom, right), [in LXX: 2Ma.6:5 7:1 10:34, 3Ma.5:20 ;] (a) of persons, lawless (III Mac, l.with); (b) of things, lawless, unlawful: Act.10:28, 1Pe.4:3. SYN.: see: ἄθεσμος.
in cl. (a) slighting or denyiyig the gods (Plat.; cf. MM, VGT, see word); (b) godless, ungodly (Pind.); (with) abandoned by the gods (Soph.)
In the NT (cf. Lft. on Ign. ad Trail, §3), of the heathen, without God, not knowing God (Cremer, 281): Eph.2:12.

ἄθεσμος, -ον (θεσμός, law, custom), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:12 6:26 ;] lawless, esp. of those who violate the law of nature and conscience (cf. MM, VGT, see word): 2Pe.2:7 3:17. SYN.: ἀθέμιτος, ἄνομος, κακός, πονηρός, φαῦλος (see Tr., Syn., §lxxiv; DCG, ii, 821b).
to set aside, disregard (in Gramm., to reject as spurious): διαθήκην , Gal.3:16; ἐντολήν , Mrk.7:9; νόμον . Heb.10:28; πίστιν , 1Ti.5:12.
to nullify, make void: Luk.7:30 (see Field, Notes, 59), 1Co.1:19, Gal.2:21.
to reject: Mrk.6:26 (Field, op. cit., 30), Lk 10:16, Jhn.12:48, I Th 48, Ju 8 (for exx. in π., see MM, VGT, see word).

ἀθέτησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀθετέω), [in LXX, usually of unfaithful, rebellious action: 1Ki.24:12 (פֶּשַׁע), Jer.12:1 (בֶּגֶד), Da TH 9:7 (מַעַל), 2Ma.14:28 ;] a disannulling, setting aside: Heb.7:18 9:26 (for similar usage in π., see Deiss., BS, 228 f.; MM, VGT, see word).
Ἀθῆναι, -ῶν, αἱ (plural because consisting of several parts), Athens: Act.17:15,16 18:1, 1Th.3:1.
Athenian, of or from Athens , (Iliad by Homer), etc
ἀθλέω, -ῶ (in cl. also ἀθλεύω, ἆθλος, a contest, in war or in sport), to contend in games, wrestle, combat: 2Ti.2:5.
ἄθλησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀθλέω), a contest, combat, esp. of athletes; figuratively, a struggle: Heb.10:32 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀθυμέω, -ῶ (ἄ-θυμος, without heart), [in LXX for חרה, etc. ;] to be disheartened: Col.3:21.
unpunished (MM, VGT, see word).
innocent: Mat.27:4 (WH, R, mg., δίκαιον) 27:24.
αἴγειος (WH, -γιος), -α, -ον (αἶξ, a goat), [in LXX for עֵז ;] of a goat: Heb.11:37 (MM, VGT, see word).

αἰγιαλός, -οῦ, ὁ (on the derivation, see Boisacq, see word), [in LXX: Jdg.5:17 (חוֹף), Sir.24:14 ;] the sea-shore, beach (cf. Field, Notes, 146; DCG, i, 175 f.; MM, VGT, see word): Mat.13:2,48 Jhn.21:4, Act.21:5 27:39,40.
Αἰγύπτιος, -α, ον, Egyptian: Act.7:22,24,28 21:38, Heb.11:29.

Αἴγυπτος, ου, ἡ, Egypt: Mat.2:13-15,19, Act.2:10 7:9-39, Heb.3:16 11:26,27; γῆ Αἴ., Act.7:40 13:17, Heb.8:9, Ju 5 (cf. Exo.5:12, al.); ἡ Αἴ., Act.7:11; figuratively, of Jerusalem as hostile to God, Rev.11:8.

SYN.: αἰώνιος, also frequently in Inscr. (Deiss., BS, 363 J. The etymological distinction between the meanings of the two words seems not to be retained in late Greek (see Thayer, see word, αἰώνιος; cf. Cremer, 79, 611).

αἰδώς (-όος), -οῦς, ἡ, [in LXX: 3Ma.1:19 4:5 ;] a sense of shame, modesty: 1Ti.2:9 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: αἰσχύνη (see Thayer, 14; Tr., Syn., §xix; Cremer, 611 f.; CGT on I Ti, l.with).
.Αἰθίοψ, -οπος, ὁ (αἴθω, to burn, ὤψ, face; i.e. swarthy), [in LXX for כּוּשׁ ;] Ethiopian: Act.8:27.

In special senses: (a) of generation, origin, kinship (cl.): Jhn.1:13 (see MM, VGT, see word); (b) as in OT (AR on Eph, l.with), in the phrase σάρξ καὶ αἷ. (αἷ. κ. σ.), to indicate human nature as opposite to God and created spirits: Mat.16:17, 1Co.15:50, Gal.1:16, Eph.6:12, Heb.2:14; (with) of things in colour resembling blood: Act.2:19,20, Rev.6:12 14:18-20. (d) of bloodshed, a bloody death (cl.): Mat.23:30,35 27:4,6,8,24,25, Luk.11:50,51 13:1, Act.1:19 5:28 18:6 20:26 22:20, Heb.12:4, Rev.6:10 17:6 18:24 19:2 ; αἷ. ἐκχέειν (Deiss., LAE, 428; MM, VGT, see word, αἷ.), Rom.3:15, Rev.16:6; (e) of sacrificial blood, as an expiation: Heb.9:7,12,13,18-22,25 10:4 11:28 13:11; of the blood of Christ, Mat.26:28, Mrk.14:24, Luk.22:20, Jhn.6:53,54,56, Act.20:28, Rom.3:25 5:9, 1Co.10:16 11:25,27, Eph.1:7 2:13, Col.1:20, Heb.9:12,14 10:19,29 12:24 13:20, 1Pe.1:2,19, 1Jn.1:7 (cf. 5:6,8), Rev.1:5 5:9 7:14 12:11. (Cremer, 69 f., 612 f.)
αἱματεκχυσία, -ας, ἡ (αἷμα, ἐκ, χέω), shedding of blood (Eccl.; Cremer, 71): Heb.9:22.
αἱμορροέω, (αἷμα, ῥέω), [in LXX: Lev.15:33 (דָּוֶה) ;] to lose blood, suffer from a flow of blood (Hipp.): Mat.9:20.
Αἰνέας, -οῦ, ὁ, Æneas: Act.9:33,34.
αἴνεσις, -εως, ἡ (αἰνέω), [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹדָה, תְּהִלָּה ;] praise (Eccl.): θυσία αἰνέσεως (Lev.7:12, זֶבַח תּוֹדָה), Heb.13:15.

αἰνέω, -ῶ (αἶνος), poët., Ion. and late prose (MM, VGT, see word) for cl. ἐπαινέω, [in LXX chiefly for הלל, ידה ;] to praise: with accusative, τ. θεόν, Luk.2:13,20 19:37 24:53, Act.2:47 3:8,9, Rom.15:11; with dative, τ. Θεῷ (Jer.20:13, al. for הִלֵּל לְ; see Field, Notes, 245), Rev.19:5. SYN.: ἐξομολογέω, εὐλογέω, εὐχαριστέω, μακαρίζω (see DCG, i, 211).

αἴνιγμα, -ατος, τό (αἰνίσσομαι, to speak in riddles; αἰνός = δεινός, dread, strange), [in LXX for חִידָה, Num.12:8 and always exc. Deu.28:37 (שַׁמָּה) ;] a dark saying, riddle: 1Co.13:12 (cf. Nu, l.with).
= μῦθος, a tale (Horn., al.).
= Att. ἔπαινος, praise (Horn., al.): Mat.21:16 (LXX), Luk.18:43.
In π., a decree (MM, VGT, see word).
Αἰνών, ἡ, indecl. (cf. Heb.עַיִן, a spring), Aenon: Jhn.3:23.
αἱρέω, [in LXX for אמר hi., בּחר, etc. ;] to take; Mid., -ομαι (M, Pr., 158 f.; MM, VGT, see word), to choose: Php.1:22, 2Th.2:13, Heb.11:25. (Cf. ἀν-, ἀφ-, δι-, ἐξ-, καθ-, περι-, προ-αιρέω.)
capture.
choosing, choice (see MM, VGT, see word).
that which is chosen, hence, opinion; esp. a peculiar opinion, heresy: 1Co.11:19, Gal.5:20, 2Pe.2:1 R, txt.

In late writers (MM, VGT), of a set of persons professing particular principles or opinions, a school, sect, party, faction: Act.5:17 15:5 24:5,14 26:5 28:22, I Co, Ga, II Pe, l.with, R, mg. (Cremer, 614).
αἱρετίζω, [in LXX chiefly for בּחר (see Cremer, 615) ;] = αἱρέομαι (Hipp., Inscr.), to choose: Mat.12:18 (LXX, ἀντιλήμψομαι).
capable of choosing (Plat.).
causing division, heretical, factious (Cremer, 614): as subst., Tit.3:10.
to raise, take up, lift or draw up: Jhn.8:59 11:41, Act.27:17, al.
to bear, carry: Mat.4:6 16:24, al.

to bear or take away, carry off, remove: Mat.21:21, Jhn.19:31, 1Co.5:12 6:15 (see Lft., Notes, 216), al.; of the taking away sin by Christ, Jhn.1:29, 1Jn.3:5. (Cf. ἀπ᾽, ἐξ-, ἐπ-, μετ-, συν-, ὑπερ-αίρω). For exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word)
αἰσθάνομαι, [in LXX for בּין, חפז, ידע ;] to perceive: withaccusative of thing(s) (Bl., §36, 5; MM, VGT, see word), Luk.9:45 (Cremer, 619 f.).
αἴσθησις, -εως, ἡ (αἰσθάνομαι), [in LXX chiefly for דַּעַת ;] perception (MM, VGT, see word): Php.1:9. SYN.: ἐπίγνωσις, which see (cf. Cremer, 620).
αἰσθητήριον, -ου, τό (αἰσθάνομαι), [in LXX: Jer.4:19 (קִיר), 4Ma.2:22 ;] sense, organ of perception: Heb.5:14 (MM, VGT, see word).
αἰσχροκερδής, -ες (αἰσχρός, κέρδος), greedy of base gains: 1Ti.3:3,8 Abbot-Smith omits 1Ti.3:3, Tit.1:7.
αἰσχροκερδῶς, adv., from eagerness for base gain: 1Pe.5:2 (here only).
αἰσχρολογία, -ας, ἡ (αἰσχρός, λέγω), abusive language, abuse (Lft., ICC, in l.; MM, VGT, see word): Col.3:8.
αἰσχρός, -ά, όν (αἶσχος, shame, disgrace), [in LXX: Gen.41:3 ff. (רַע, רֹעַ), Jdth.12:12, al. ;] base, shameful: 1Co.11:6 14:35, Eph.5:12, Tit.1:11 (MM, VGT, see word).
αἰσχρός, -ά, όν (αἶσχος, shame, disgrace), [in LXX: Gen.41:3 ff. (רַע, רֹעַ), Jdth.12:12, al. ;] base, shameful: 1Co.11:6 14:35, Eph.5:12, Tit.1:11 (MM, VGT, see word).
αἰσχρότης, -τητος, ἡ (αἰσχρός), baseness: Eph.5:4.

αἰσχύνη, -ης, ἡ (αἶσχος, shame, disgrace), [in LXX chiefly for בּשֶׁת ;] shame (MM, VGT, see word): subjectively, Luk.14:19, 2Co.4:2; objectively, Php.3:19, Heb.12:2; as something to be ashamed of, Rev.3:18; pl. (Bl., §32, 6), shameful deeds, Ju 13. SYN.: αἰδώς, which see
to disfigure (Hom.).
to dishonour (Pro.29:15).
to make ashamed (Sir.13:7). Pass., to be put to shame, be ashamed: 2Co.10:8; Php.1:20, 1Pe.4:16, 1Jn.2:28; with inf. (M, Pr., 205), Luk.16:3 (cf. ἐπ- (-ομαι), κατ- αἰσχύνω).

αἰτέω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for שׁאל ;] to ask, request: absol., Mat.7:7, Jas.1:6; with accusative of person(s), Mat.5:42, Luk.6:30; with accusative of thing(s), before ἀπό, Mat.20:20, 1Jn.5:15; id. before παρά, Act.3:2, Jas.1:5; with dupl. accusative Mat.7:9, Mrk.6:22, Jhn.16:23. Mid. (on the distinction bet. mid. and act., see M, Pr., 160): absol., Mrk.15:8, Jhn.16:26, Jas.4:3; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.14:7, Mrk.6:24, al.; with accusative of person(s), Mat.27:20, Luk.23:25; with accusat...
αἴτημα, -ατος, τό (αἰτέω), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁאֵלָה ;] that which has been asked for, a petition, request: Luk.23:24, Php.4:6, Jhn.5:15. SYN.: see: δέησις.
cause, reason, occasion, case: Mat.19:3, Luk.8:47, Act.10:21 22:24 28:20, 2Ti.1:6,12, Tit.1:13, Heb.2:11 ; εἰ οὕτως ἐστιν ἡ αἰ. (cf. Lat. si ita res se habet, and see MM. VGT, see word), Mat.19:10.
In forensic sense,

subs.text: accusation: Act.25:18,27;

cause for punishment, crime: Mat.27:37, Mrk.15:26, Jhn.18:38 19:4,6, Act.13:28 23:28 28:18. SYN.: ἔλεγχος, a charge, whether moral or judicial, which has been proven, αἰ. is an accusation simply, false or true.

full.text: αἰτίωμα (Rec. αἰτίαμα, the usual form; see MM, VGT, see word), -τος, τό (αἰτιάομαι, αἰτία), a charge, accusation: Act.25:7.
causative of, responsible for; as subst., ὁ αἰ., the cause, author: Heb.5:9; τὸ αἰ., the cause, Act.19:40.
blameworthy, culpable; as subst., ὁ αἰ., the culprit, the accused (Lat. reus); τὸ αἰ. (= αἰτία, 3), the crime, Luk.23:4,14,22.
causative of, responsible for; as subst., ὁ αἰ., the cause, author: Heb.5:9; τὸ αἰ., the cause, Act.19:40.
blameworthy, culpable; as subst., ὁ αἰ., the culprit, the accused (Lat. reus); τὸ αἰ. (= αἰτία, 3), the crime, Luk.23:4,14,22.
αἰφνίδιος (in Lk, l.with, ἐφν- WH; see M, Pr., 35), -ον (αἴφνης = ἄφνως, suddenly), [in LXX: Wis.17:15, 2Ma.14:17, 3Ma.3:24 ;] sudden, unexpected: Luk.21:34, 1Th.5:3.
αἰχμαλωσία -ας, ἡ (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁבִי, גּוֹלָה ;] captivity (Diod., al.): Rev.13:10; pl., abstr. for concr., = αἰχμάλωτοι, Eph.4:8 (LXX).
αἰχμαλωτεύω (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁבה ;] = αἰχμαλωτίζω, which see, to lead captive: Eph.4:8 (LXX).

αἰχμαλωτίζω (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁבה ;] in late writers = cl. αἰχμάλωτον ποιῶ (ἄγω), to take or lead captive: before εἰς, Luk.21:24 (cf. Tob.1:10); metaphorically, Rom.7:23, 2Co.10:5, 2Ti.3:6.
αἰχμάλωτος, -ον (αἰχμή, a spear, ἁλίσκομαι, to be taken), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁבָה, גּוֹלָה ;] captive: Luk.4:18 (LXX).

in cl., like Lat. aevum (LS, MM, VGT, see word), a space of time, as, a lifetime, generation, period of history, an indefinitely long period; in NT of an indefinitely long period, an age, eternity, usually with prep. (MM, VGT); (a) of the past: ἀπ᾽ αἰ. (cf. Heb. מֵעוֹלָם), Luk.1:70; (b) of the future: εἰς τ. αἰ. (cf. לְעוֹלָם), forever, Mat.21:19; id., with neg., never, Jhn.4:14; more strongly, εἰς τὸν αἰ. τοῦ αἰ., Heb.1:8 (LXX); εἰς τοὺς αἰ., Mat.6:13; εἰς τοὺς αἰ. τῶν αἰ. (cf. Isa.45:17, עַד־עוֹלְמֵי עַד), Rom.16:27, LT; cf. also Eph.3:21, 2Pe.3:18, Ju 25, Rev.14:11.

οἱ αἰ., the worlds, the universe, "the sum of the periods of time, including all that is manifested in them": He :12 11:3 (cf. 1Ti.1:17, where τῶν αἰ. are prob. "the ages or world-periods which when summed up make eternity".

the present age (Heb. הָעוֹלָם הַזֶּה): ὁ αἰ., Mat.13:22; ὁ αἰ. οὗτος, Mat.12:32; ὁ νῦν αἰ., 1Ti.6:17; ὁ ἐνεστὼς αἰ., Gal.1:4; similarly, of the time after Christ's second coming (הָעוֹלָם הַבָּא), ὁ αἰ. ἐκεῖνος, Luk.20:35; ὁ αἰ. μέλλων, Mat.12:32; ὁ αἰ. ὁ ἐρχόμενος, Mrk.10:30. SYN.: κόσμος, the ordered universe, the scheme of material things; οἰκουμένη, the inhabited earth; in contrast with both of which αἰ. is the world under aspects of time (cf. Westc. on Heb.1:2; Tr., Syn., §lix; Thayer, see word, αἰ.; Cremer, 74, 620; MM, VGT).

αἰώνιος, -ον (as usual in Attic), also -α, -ον. 2Th.2:16, Heb.9:12; (αἰών), [in LXX chiefly for עוֹלָם ;] age-long, eternal, (a) of that which is without either beginning or end: Rom.16:26, Heb.9:14; (b) of that which is without beginning: Rom.16:25, 2Ti.1:9, Tit.1:2; (with) of that which is without end (MM, VGT, see word): σκηναί, Luk.16:9 οἰκία, 2Co.5:1; διαθήκη, Heb.13:20; εὐαγγέλιον, Rev.14:6; παράκλησις, 2Th.2:16; λύτρωσις, Heb.9:12; κληρονομία, ib. 15; κόλασις, Mat.25:46; κρίμα, Heb.6:2; κ...

ἀκαθαρσία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκάθαρτος), [in LXX chiefly for טֻמְאָה, טָמֵא ;] uncleanness, impurity, (a) physical (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.23:27; (b) moral: Rom.1:24 6:19, 2Co.12:21, Gal.5:19, Eph.4:19 5:3, Col.3:5, 1Th.2:3 4:7.
ἀκαθάρτης, -τος, ἡ, uncleanness: Rev.17:4, Rec. (for τ. ἀκάθαρτα).

ἀκάθαρτος, -ον (ἀ- neg., καθαίρω), [in LXX chiefly for טָמֵא ;] unclean, impure; (a) physically (LS, MM, VGT, see word); (b) ceremonially: Act.10:14,28 11:8, 1Co.7:14, 2Co.6:17, Rev.18:2; (with) morally: Eph.5:5, Rev.17:4; with πνεῦμα, as always in Gosp., Mat.10:1 12:43, Mrk.1:23,26,27 3:11,30 5:2,8,13 6:7 7:25 9:25, Luk.4:33,36 6:18 8:29 9:42 11:24, Act.5:16 8:7, Rev.16:13 (cf. Cremer, 320).
ἀκαιρέομαι, -οῦμαι (ἄκαιρος, unseasonable), to have no opportunity (opposite to εὐκαιρέω): Php.4:10.
ἀκαίρως, adv. (ἄκαιρος, unseasonable), [in LXX: Si 35 (32):4 ;] out of season, unseasonably: opposite to εὐκαίρως (which see), 2Ti.4:2 (cf. Cremer, 740; MM, VGT, see word).
ἄκακος, -ον, [in LXX for פְּתִי, תָּם, etc. ;] (a) as in cl. (Æsch., Plat., al.), of persons, simple, guileless: Rom.16:18, Heb.7:26 (cf. Cremer, 327); (b) of things, undamaged (? MM, VGT, see word).

ἄκανθα, -ης, ἡ (ἀκή, a point), [in LXX chiefly for קוֹץ, also for סִיר, שַׁיִת, etc. ;] a prickly plant, thorn, brier; in NT always pl.: Mat.7:16 13:7,22 27:29, Mrk.4:7,18, Luk.6:44 8:7,14, Jhn.19:2, Heb.6:8 (see MM, VGT, see word).
of thorns: Mrk.15:17, Jhn.19:5.
of acantha-wood (Hdt.; π. ap. MM, VGT, see word).
ἄ-καρπος, -ον, [in LXX: Jer.2:6 (צַלְמָוֶת), Wis.15:4, 4Ma.16:7 ;] unfruitful, barren: figuratively, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19, 1Co.14:14, Eph.5:11, Tit.3:14, 2Pe.1:8, Ju 12.
ἀ-κατά-γνωστος, -ον (καταγινώσκω), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:47 ;] not open to just rebuke, irreprehensible: Tit.2:8 (see Cremer, 676; and for other exx., MM, VGT, see word).
ἀ-κατα-κάλυπτος, -ον (κατακαλύπτω), [in LXX: Lev.13:45 A (פָּרוּע) ;] uncovered, unveiled: 1Co.11:5, 13.
uncondemned (EV): Act.16:37, 22:25.
= cl. ἄκριτος, without trial, not yet tried (MM, VGT, see word) Ac, ll. cc.
ἀ-κατά-λυτος, -ον (καταλύω), [in LXX: 4Ma.10:11 ;] indissoluble: Heb.7:16.
ἀκατάπαυστος, -ον (καταπαύω), that cannot cease, not to be restrained: with genitive of thing(s), 2Pe.2:14, T, Tr. txt.

ἀκαταστασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκατάστατος), [in LXX: Pro.26:28 (מִדְחֶה), Tob.4:13 ;] instability (MM, VGT, see word); hence, confusion, tumult: 1Co.14:33, Jas.3:16; pl. (Bl., §32, 6), Luk.21:9, 2Co.6:5, 12:20 (Polyb., al.; see Cremer, 739).
ἀ-κατά-στατος, -ον (καθίστημι), [in LXX: Isa.54:11 (סֹעֲרָה) ;] unsettled, unstable: Jas.1:8, 3:8.
ἀκατάσχετος, -ον (κατέχω), [in LXX: Job.31:11, 3Ma.6:17 ;] that cannot be restrained: Jas.3:8 Rec. (for -στατος, which see).
§ Akeldama dama (Proper name) (traditionally located south of the valley of Hinnom), "field of blood"

etymology: of Chaldee origin (meaning field of blood;

full.text: ἀκέραιος, -ον (κεράννυμι), [in LXX: Est.8:13 ;] unmixed, pure, hence, metaphorically (cf. MM, VGT, see word), guileless, simple: Mat.10:16, Rom.16:19, Php.2:15. SYN.: ἄδολος, ἄκακος, ἁπλοῦς (cf. Ellic. on Phl., l.with; Tr., Syn., §lvi).
ἀκλινής, -ές (κλίνω), [in LXX: 4Ma.6:7 17:3 ;] unbending, firm: metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word). Heb.10:23.
ἀκμάζω (ἀκμήν), [in LXX: 4Ma.2:3 ;] to be at the prime; of produce of the ground, to be ripe (Thuc.): Rev.14:18 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀκμήν, accusative of ἀκμή, a point, used as adv., at the present point of time, even now, even yet: Mat.15:16.

etymology: accusative case of a noun ("acme") akin to

full.text: hearing, the sense of hearing: 1Co.12:17, 2Pe.2:8; "Hebraic dative," ἀκοῇ ακούειν (frequently in LXX; V. M, Pr., 14, 75), Mat.13:14, Act.28:26.
organ of hearing, the ear (Arist., al.; MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.4:3-4; pl., Mrk.7:35, Luk.7:1, Act.17:20, Heb.5:11.
a thing heard, i.e.,

subs.text: a report, rumour: with genitive, of person

full.text: ἀκολουθέω, -ῶ (ἀκόλουθος, following; ἀ- cop., κέλευθος, poët., away), [in LXX chiefly for הָלַךְ ;] to accompany, follow: Mat.4:25, al. Metaphorical, of discipleship: Mat.9:9, Mrk.9:38, Jhn.12:26, al. Absol.: Mat.18:10; more freq., with dative (cl.), Mat.8:1, al.; before μετά, with genitive, (cl.; Eutherford, N.Phr., 458 f.), Luk.9:49; ὀπίσω, with genitive (Heb. הָלַךְ אָהֲרֵי), Mat.10:38 (cf. ἐξ-, ἐπ-, κατ-, παρ-, συν-ἀκολουθέω). SYN.: (cl.) ἕπομαι, not in NT (see Cremer, 80; MM, VGT, see word)...

Intrans.: Mrk.4:3 7:37, Jas.2:5, Rev.2:7, al.; τ. ὠσίν, Mat.13:15 (LXX); with cogn. dative, ακοῇ ἀ. (see: ἀκοή), Mat.13:14, Act.28:26" (LXX) ; ὁ ἔχων ὦτα (οὖς) ἀκούειν, ἀκουσάτω, Mat.11:15, Mrk.4:23, Rev.2:7, al.
Trans., prop. with accusative of thing

subs.text: , of thing heard, genitive of person

full.text: ἀκρασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκρατής, which see), [in LXX: 1Ma.6:26 ;] in Arist. and later writers = ἀκράτεια (Lft., Notes, 222 f.), want of power, hence want of self-control, incontinence: Mat.23:25, 1Co.7:5.
ἀκρατής, -ές (κράτος), [in LXX: Pro.27:20 ;] (a) powerless, impotent; (b) in moral sense, lacking self-control, incontinent: 2Ti.3:3.
ἄκρατος, -ον (κεράννυμι), [in LXX: Psa.75:8 (חֲמַר), Jer.25:15 (חֵמָה), 3Ma.5:2 ;] unmixed, pure: οἶνος, Rev.14:10.
ἀκρίβεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀκριβής), [in LXX: Dan LXX TH 7:16 (יַצִּיב), Wis.12:21, Sir.16:25 42:4 ;] exactness, precision (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word): Act.22:3.
ἀκριβής, -ές [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:45 6:12 (יַצִּיב) Dan.4:25, Est.4:5, Sir.18:29 19:25 31:24 32:3 ;] exact, precise, careful, of things and persons: superl., Act.26:5.

ἀκριβῶς adv. (ἀκριβής), [in LXX: Deu.19:18 (יָטַב), Da TH 7:19 (יְצֵב), Eze.39:14, Wis.19:18, Sir.18:29 ;] with exactness, carefully: Mat.2:8, Luk.1:3, Act.18:25, Eph.5:15, 1Th.5:2 (M, Th., in l). Compar., ἀκριβέστερον (Milligan, NTD, 111; MM, VGT, see word), Act.18:26 23:15, 20 24:22.

etymology: neuter of the comparative of the same as (G0196)

full.text: ἀκριβόω, -ῶ (ἀκριβής), [in Aq.: Isa.30:8 49:16 ;] to enquire with exactness, learn carefully: Mat.2:7, 16 (for similar ex., see MM, VGT, sv.).

ἀκριβῶς adv. (ἀκριβής), [in LXX: Deu.19:18 (יָטַב), Da TH 7:19 (יְצֵב), Eze.39:14, Wis.19:18, Sir.18:29 ;] with exactness, carefully: Mat.2:8, Luk.1:3, Act.18:25, Eph.5:15, 1Th.5:2 (M, Th., in l). Compar., ἀκριβέστερον (Milligan, NTD, 111; MM, VGT, see word), Act.18:26 23:15, 20 24:22.
ἀκρίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אַרְבֶּה, also for חָגָב, etc. ;] a locust: Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6, Rev.9:3, 7.
ἀκροατήριον, -ου, τό (ἀκροάομαι, to listen) a place of audience: Act.25:23 (Plut.).
ἀκροατής, οῦ, ὁ (see supr.) [in LXX: Isa.3:3 (לַחַשׁ), Sir.3:29 ;] a hearer: Rom.2:13, Jas.1:22, 23, 25.

ἀκροβυστία, -ας, ή (perh. an Alexandrian form of cl. ἀκροποσθία; cf. MM, VGT, see word) [in LXX for עׇרְלָה ;] the prepuce, foreskin (LXX), hence abstr., uncircumcision: Act.11:3, Rom.2:25-27 3:30 4:10-12, 1Co.7:18-19, Gal.5:6 6:15, Col.2:13 3:11 By meton., the uncircumcised: Rom.4:9, Gal.2:7, Eph.2:11.

ἀκρο-γωνιαῖος, -αία, -αῖον (ἄκροι, γωνία, an angle) [in LXX: Isa.28:16 (פִּנָּה) ;] = Attic γωνιαῖος (frequently in Inscr.; MM, VGT, see word ἀ.), at the extreme angle: ὁ ἀ., the corner foundation stone, Eph.2:20, 1Pe.2:6.
first-fruits (Xen.; MM, VGT, see word).
In war, the choicest spoils (cf. Hdt., viii, 121 f.): Heb.7:4.
ἄκρος, -α, -ον [in LXX for קֶצַח, בֹּהֶן, etc. ;] highest, extreme; as subst., τὸ ἄ., the top, extremity: Mrk.13:27, Luk.16:24, Heb.11:21; pl. (cf. MM, VGT, see word), Mat.24:31.

etymology: neuter of an adjective probably akin to the base of (G0188)

full.text: Ἀκύλας, -ου (and -α; MM, VGT, see word), ὁ (Lat.), Aquila: Act.18:2, 18 18:26, Rom.16:3, 1Co.16:19, 2Ti.4:19.
ἀκυρόω, -ῶ (κῦρος, authority), [in LXX: 1Es.6:32 Est.6:1-34, 2Ma.6:1-31 ;] to revoke, invalidate (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.15:6, Mrk.7:13, Gal.3:17 (Plut.).
ἀκωλύτως adv. (κωλύω), [in Sm.: Job.34:31 ;] without hindrance (so frequently in legal documents; MM, VGT, see word): Act.28:31.
ἀκων (Attic contr. for ἀέκων), -ουα, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἅκων, willing), [in LXX: Job.14:17, 2Ma.11:12 ;] unwilling: 1Co.9:17.

ἀλάβαστρον, -ου, τό (also -ος, ὁ, ἡ; colloq. and κοινή for ἀλάβαστος), [in LXX: 4Ki.21:13 (צַלַּחַת) ;] a box of alabaster (ἀλαβαστίτης) for ointment: Mat.26:7, Mrk.14:3, Luk.7:37 (see DCG, i, 41b; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀλαζονία(Rec. -εία, the earlier form), -ας, ἡ (ἀλαζών), [in LXX: Wis.5:8 17:7, 2; 4Ma.5:1-38 ;] the character of an ἀλαζών, boastfulness, vainglory, vaunting: Jas.4:16 (Mayor, in l), 1Jn.2:16.

ἀλαζών, -όνος, ὁ, ἡ (ἄλη, wandering) [in LXX: Job.28:8 (שַׁחַץ) Hab.2:5 (יָהִיר), Pro.21:24 (לוּץ) ;] prop, a vagabond, hence, an impostor, a boaster: Rom.1:30, 2Ti.3:2. SYN.: ὐβριστής, ὑπερήφανος (see Tr., Syn., § xxix; Lft., Notes, 256)

ἀλαλάζω (onomat. from the battle-cry ἀλαλά), [in LXX chiefly for רוּעַ hi., יָלַל ;] prop, to raise a war-cry, shout with triumph or joy; rarely of grief, to wail: Mrk.5:38 (cf. Jer.4:8); of a cymbal, ἀλαλάζον (RV. clanging), 1Co.13:1 (cf. ὀλολύζω).
ἀ-λάλητος, -ον (λαλέω), inexpressible, not to be uttered: Rom.8:26.
ἄ-λαλος, -ον (λάλος, talkative), [in LXX: Psa.31:18 (אָלַם ni.) Psa.38:13 (אִלֵּם) ;] dumb, speechless: Mrk.7:37 9:17, 25.

ἅλας (T, ἅλα), -ατος, τό, late form of cl. ἅλς, -ος, ὁ (MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX chiefly for מְלַח ;] salt, lit, and figuratively: Mat.5:13, Mrk.9:50 Luk.14:34; like cl. ἅλες, wit, of wisdom and grace in speech: Col.4:6.

ἀλείφω (cf. λίπος, oil), [in LXX: Gen.31:13, Exo.40:15, Num.3:3 (מָשַׁח), Eze.13:10 ff. (טוּחַ), Rut.3:3, 2Ki.12:20 14:2, 4Ki.4:2, 2Ch.28:15, Mic.6:15, Dan LXX TH Dan.10:3 (סוּךְ), Est.2:12, Jdth.16:8 ;] to anoint, festally or in homage: with accusative of thing(s) or of person(s), Mat.6:17, Jhn.12:3, Mrk.16:1; before dative, ἐλαίῳ, Mrk.6:13, Jas.5:14; μύρῳ, Luk.7:38, 46, Jhn.11:2. SYN.: χρίω, μυρίζω (against the distinction made bet. ἀ. and χ. in Tr., Syn., § 38, see MM, VGT, see word, ἀ.)
ἀλεκτοροφωνία, -ας, ἡ (ἀλέκτωρ, φωνή), cock-crowing, i.e. the third watch in the night: Mrk.13:35.
ἀλέκτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (poët. form of ἀλεκτρυών; see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Pro.30:31) (זַרְזִיר; BDB, Lex., 267) ;] a cock: Mat.26:34, Mrk.14:30, 68 14:72 Luk.22:34, 60-61, Jhn.13:38 18:27.
Ἀλεξανδρεύς, -έως, ὁ an Alexandrian: Act.6:9 18:24.
Ἀλεξανδρινός (Rec. -δρῖνο; see Kühner3, II, 296), -ή, -όν, Alexan­drian: Act.27:6 28:11.
Son of Simon of Cyrene: Mrk.15:21
A kinsman of the High Priest: Act.4:6.
A certain Jew: Act.19:33.
A coppersmith: 1Ti.1:20.
Perh. = 4 (see Ellic. on I Ti, l.with): 2Ti.4:14.

etymology: from the same as (the first part of) (G0220) (G0435)

full.text: ἄλευρον, -ου, τό (ἀλεύω, to grind), [in LXX for קֶמַח, Num.5:15, al. ;] real: Mat.13:33, Luk.13:21.

Objectively, "the reality lying at the basis of an appearance; the manifested, veritable essence of a matter" (Cremer, 86): Rom.9:1, al.; of religious truth, Rom.1:25, al.; esp. of Christian doctrine, Gal.2:5, al.; ἀ. θεοῦ, Rom.15:8.
Subjectively, truthfulness, truth, not merely verbal (cl.), but sincerity and integrity of character: Jhn.8:44, 3Jo.3.
In phrases (MM, VGT, see word): ἐπ᾽ ἀληθείας, Mrk.12:14, al.; ἀ. λέγειν (εἰπεῖν, λαλεῖν), Rom.9:1, 2Co.12:6, Eph.4:25, al.; ἀ. ποιεῖν, Jhn.3:21, 1Jn.1:6 (cf. DB, iv, 818b, ff.).
ἀληθεύω (ἀληθής), [in LXX: Gen.20:16 (יָכַח) Gen.42:16 (אֶמֶת), Pro.21:3 (מִשְׁפָּט), Isa.44:26 (שָׁלַם), Sir.34:4 ;] to speak the truth (R, mg., deal truly; Field, Notes, 192): Gal.4:16, Eph.4:15.

ἀληθής, -ές (λήθω = λανθάνω, hence primarily, unconcealed, manifest; hence, actual, real), [in LXX for אֶמֶת, etc. ;] (a) of things, true, conforming to reality: Jhn.4:18 5:31-32 6:55 (= ἀληθινός, which see) Jhn.8:13-14, 17 10:41 19:35 21:24, Act.12:9, Php.4:8, Tit.1:13, 1Pe.5:12, 2Pe.2:22, 1Jn.2:6, 3Jn.12; (b) of persons, truthful: Mat.22:16, Mrk.12:14, Jhn.3:33 7:18 8:26, Rom.3:4, 2Co.6:8. SYN.: ἀληθινός, real, genuine, ideal, as opposite to spurious or imperfect ἀληθής, true to fact, as oppos...

ἀληθινός, -ή, -όν (ἀληθής), [in LXX for אֶמֶת ;] true, in the sense of real, ideal, genuine: Luk.16:11 Jhn.1:9 4:23, 37 6:32 7:28 8:16 15:1 17:3 19:35, 1Th.1:9, Heb.8:2 9:24 10:22, 1Jn.2:8 Jn 5:20, Rev.3:7, 14 6:10 15:3 16:7 19:2; = ἀληθής, Rev.19:9 21:5 22:6 (MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: ἀληθής, q.v
ἀλήθω (κοινή form of the Attic ἀλέω), [in LXX for טָחַן ;] to grind: Mat.24:41, Luk.17:35.

ἀληθῶς adv. (ἀληθής). [in LXX (Jer.28:6, Psa.58:1, al.) chiefly for אָמֵן and cogn. forms ;] truly, surely: Mat.14:33 26:73 Mrk.14:70 15:39, Luk.9:27 12:44 21:3, Jhn.1:48 4:42 6:14 7:26, 40 8:31 17:8 Act.12:11, 1Th.2:13, 1Jn.2:5.
ἁλεεύς (Rec. ἁλιεύς, the older form; WH, App., 151, -έως, ὁ (ἅλς, the sea) [in LXX for דָּג, דַּוָּג ;] a fisherman: Mat.4:18, 19, Mrk.1:16, 17, Luk.5:2
ἁλιεύω (ἁλιεύς), [in LXX: Jer.16:16 (דִּיג) ;] to fish: Jhn.21:3 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἁλίζω (ἅλς), [in LXX for מָלַח ;] to salt, season with salt: Mat.5:13, Mrk.9:49.
ἀλίσγημα, -τος, τό (late ἀλισγέω, to pollute) pollution: Act.15:20.

opposing a previous negation, but: οὐ (μὴ) . . . ἀ., Mat.5:15, 17 Mrk.5:39, Jhn.7:16, al.; rhetorically subordinating but not entirely negativing what precedes, οὐ . . . ἀ., not so much . . . as, Mrk.9:37, Mat.10:20, Jhn.12:44, al.; with ellipse of the negation, Mat.11:7-9, Act.19:2, 1Co.3:6 6:11 7:7, 2Co.7:1, Gal.2:3, al.; in opposition to a foregoing pos. sentence, ἀ. οὐ, Mat.24:6, 1Co.10:23; οὐ μόνον . . . ἀ. καί, Jhn.5:18, Rom.1:32, al.; elliptically, after a negation, ἀ. ἵνα, Mrk.14:49, Jhn.1:8 9:3, al.; = εἰ μή (Bl., §77, 13; M, Pr., 241; but cf. WM, §iii, 10), Mat.20:23, Mrk.4:22.

Without previous negation, to express opposition, interruption, transition, etc., but: Jhn.16:20 12:27, Gal.2:14; before commands or requests, Act.10:20 26:16, Mat.9:18, Mrk.9:22, al.; to introduce an accessory idea, 2Co.7:11; in the apodosis after a condition or concession with εἰ, ἐάν, εἴπερ, yet, still, at least, Mrk.14:29, 1Co.9:2, 2Co.4:16, Col.2:5, al.; after μέν, Act.4:17, Rom.14:20, 1Co.14:17; giving emphasis to the following clause, ἀλλ᾽ ἔρχεται ὥρα, yea, etc., Jhn.16:2; so with neg., ἀλλ᾽ οὐδέ, nay, nor yet, Luk.23:15.

Joined with other particles (a practice which increases in late writers; Simcox, LNT, 166), ἀ. γε, yet at least, Luk.24:21, 1Co.9:2; ἄ ἤ., save only, except, Luk.12:51, 2Co.1:13; ἀ. μὲν οὖν, Php.3:8 (on this usage, see MM, VGT, see word).
to change: Act.6:14, Gal.4:20.
to transform: 1Co.15:51-52, Heb.1:12.
to exchange: with accusative, before ἐν (= בְּ, Psa.106:20) instead of simple genitive (Bl., § 36, 8), Rom.1:23 (cf. ἀπ-, δι-, κατ-, ἀπο-κατ-, μετ-, συν-αλλάσσω; see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀλλαχόθεν adv. (ἀλλος), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:7 ;] = ἀλλοθεν (seeMM, VGT, see word) from another place: Jhn.10:1.
ἀλληγορέω, -ῶ (ἄλλος, ἀγορεύω) to speak allegorically (Cremer, 96 ff.): Gal.4:24.

ἁλληλουιά (Rec. ἀλληλούϊα; Heb. הָלַל יָהּ, praise the Lord), [in LXX in the titles of certain Psa (104 (105), al.), and at the end of Psa.150:1-6; also Tob.13:18, 3Ma.7:13 ;] hallelujah, alleluia: Rev.19:1, 3-4 19:6.
ἀλλήλων (genitive pl.), dative -οις, -αις, accusative -ους, -ας, -α (no nom.), recipr. pron. (ἄλλος), of one another, mutually: Mat.25:32, Mrk.4:41, Jhn.13:22, al.
ἀλλογενής, -ές (ἄλλος, γένος), [in LXX chiefly for זוּר, נֵכָר ;] of another race, a foreigner ( = ἀλλόφυλος; Cremer, 150; MM, VGT, see word): Luk.17:18.
ἅλλομαι [in LXX for צָלַח, דָּלַג pi., etc. ;] to leap: Act.3:8 14:10; of water, to spring up, Jhn.4:14 (MM, VGT, see word).

etymology: middle voice of apparently a primary verb;

full.text: ἄλλος, -η, -ο, (cf. Lat. alius, Eng. else), [in LXX for אַחֵר ,אֶחָד, etc. ;] other, another: absol., Mat.20:3, al.; ἄ. δέ, 1Co.3:10 12:8; pl., Mrk.6:15; attached to a noun, Mat.2:12 4:21, al.; with art., ὁ ἄ., the other, Mat.5:39, Jhn.19:32 (Bl., §47, 8); οἱ ἄ., the others, the rest, Jhn.20:25, 1Co.14:29; ἄ. πρὸς ἄλλον = πρὸς ἀλλήλους (BL, §48, 10), Act.2:12; ἄλλ᾽ (i.e. ἄλλο) ἤ (Bl., §77, 13), Luk.12:51; before πλήν, Mrk.12:32; εἰ μή, Jhn.6:22; παρά with accusative, 1Co.3:11. SYN.: ἕτερος, whic...
ἀλλοτρι-επίσκοπος (Rec. ἀλλοτριοεπ-), -ου, ὁ one who meddles in things alien to his calling: 1Pe.4:15 (see ICC, in l; Deiss., BS, 224-4; MM, VGT, see word).
be-longing to another, not one's own (opposite to ἴδιος): Luk.16:12, Rom.14:4 15:20 (Field, Notes, 165 f.), 2Co.10:15-16, 1Ti.5:22, Heb.9:25.
foreign, strange, alien (opposite to οἰκεῖος; see MM, VGT, see word): Mat.17:25-26, Jhn.10:5, Act.7:6, Heb.11:9, 34.
ἀλλόφυλος, -ον (ἄλλος, φῦλον, a tribe), [in LXX chiefly for פְּלֶשֶׁת ;] foreign, of another race (MM, VGT, see word); as opposite to a Jew, a Gentile: Act.10:28.
ἄλλως adv. (ἄλλος), otherwise: 1Ti.5:25.
ἀλοάω, -ῶ (ἅλως, see: ἅλων; and cf. MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX chiefly for דּוּשׁ ;] to thresh: 1Co.9:9 9:13; 1Ti.5:18.
without reason, irrational: ζῷα, 2Pe.2:12, Ju 10.
contrary to reason: Act.25:27 (see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀλόη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Sng.4:14 א (אֲהָלִים) ;] the aloe, aloes (the powder of a fragrant wood): Jhn.19:39.
ἅλς, ἁλός, ὁ variant for ἅλας (which see): Mrk.9:49, Rec. WH, mg., R, mg.
ἁλυκός, -ή, -όν (ἅλς), [in LXX for מְלַח, שִׂדִּים ;] salt: Jas.3:12.
ἄλυπος, -ον (λύπη), free from grief: Php.2:28.
ἅλυσις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: Wis.17:17 ;] a chain, bond: Mrk.5:3-4, Luk.8:29, Act.12:6-7 21:33 28:20, Eph.6:20, 2Ti.1:16, Rev.20:1.
ἀ-λυσιτελής, -ές (cf. λυσιτελέω), unprofitable: Heb.13:17.
Father of Levi: Mrk.2:14.
Father of James: Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:13, Luk.6:15, Act.1:13.
ἅλων, -ωνος (for Attic ἅλως, -ω, see MM, VGT, see word), ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for גֹּרֶן ;] a threshing floor: Mat.3:12, Luk.3:17 (here prob. by meton. = the grain on the threshing floor).
ἀλώπηξ, -εκος, ἡ [in LXX for שׁוּעָל ;] a fox: Mat.8:20, Luk.9:58; metaphorically, of Herod, Luk.13:32.
ἅλωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἁλίσκομαι), [in LXX: Jer.50:46 (תָּפַשׂ ni.) ;] a taking, capture: 2Pe.2:12.

ἅμα adv., at once (Lat. simul): Act.24:26 27:40, Rom.3:12 (one and all = יַחַד, Psa.14:3), Col.4:3, 1Ti.5:13, Phm 22; before σύν, 1Th.4:17 5:10; as prep. with dative, together with: Mat.13:29 (see MM, VGT, see word); also, with adv., ἅ. πρωΐ (cl., ἅ. ἕω, etc.), early in the morning: Mat.20:1.
ἀμαθής, -ές (μανθάνω), [in Sm.: Psa.49:11 ;] unlearned, ignorant: 2Pe.3:16 (on the rareness of this word, see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀμαράντινος, -ον (ἀμάραντος), of amaranth (Inscr.); hence un­fading: 1Pe.5:4.
ἀμάραντος, -ον (μαραίνομαι), [in LXX: Wis.6:12 (σοφία) ;] un­fading (whence ὁ ἀ., the amaranth, an unfading flower): 1Pe.1:4 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
to miss the mark (Hom., Æsch., al.), hence metaphorically (Hom., al.), to err, do wrong

In LXX and NT, to violate God's law, to sin (for non-Christian exx., see MM, VGT, see word): absol., Mat.18:25 27:4, Luk.17:3, Jhn.5:14 8:11 9:2-3 Rom.2:12 3:23 5:12, 14 5:16 6:15, 1Co.7:28 7:36 15:34, Eph.4:26, 1Ti.5:20, Tit.3:11, Heb.3:17 10:26, 1Pe.2:20, 2Pe.2:4 2:4 1Jn.1:10 Jn 2:1 Jn 3:6-9 Jn 5:18; with cogn. accusative, ἁ. ἁμαρτίαν (cf. Exo.32:30, חֲטָאָה חָטָא), 1Jn.5:16; before εἰς, Mat.18:21, Luk.15:18, 21 17:4, Act.25:8 (καίσαρα). 1Co.6:18 8:12 (Field, Notes, 173); ἐνώπιον, Luk.15:18, 21; πρὸς θάνατον (cf. Num.18:22, מוּת חֵטְא), 1Jn.5:16 (Cremer, 98, 633).

ἁμάρτημα, -τος, τό (ἁμαρτεῖν, see supr.), [in LXX for חַטָּאָה, עָוֺן etc. ;] an act of disobedience to divine law (Lft., Notes, 273), a sinful deed, a sin: Mrk.3:28-29, Rom.3:25, 1Co.6:18, 2Pe.1:9, WH, mg.; αἰώνιωv ἀ. (DCG, i, 788a), Mrk.3:29 (for exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: ἀγνόημα, ἁμαρτία, ἀνομία, ἀσέβεια, ἥττημα, παράβασις, παρακοή, παρανομία, παράπτωμα (ν. Cremer, 100; Tr., Syn., § lxvi; DB, iv, 532; DCG, l.with; Westc, Eph., 165 f.)

as a principle and quality of action, = τὸ ἁμαρτάνειν, a sinning, sin: Rom.5:12, 13 20; ὑφ᾽ ἁμαρτίαν εἶναι, Rom.3:9; ἐπιμένειν, Rom.6:1; ἀποθνήσκειν, νεκρὸν εἶναι τῇ ἁ., Rom.6:2, 11; τὴν ἀ. γινώσκειν, Rom.7:7; σῶμα τῆς ἁ., Rom.6:6; ἀπάτη τῆς ἁ., Heb.3:13; personified as a ruling principle, ἁ. βασιλεύει, κυριεύει, etc., Rom.5:21 6:12, 14 7:17, 20; δουλεύειν, Rom.6:6; δοῦλος τῆς ἁ., ib. 17; νόμος τῆς ἁ., Rom.7:23 8:2; δύναμις τῆς ἁ., 1Co.15:56 (cf. Gen.4:7).

As a generic term (disting. fr. the specific terms ἁμάρτημα, which see, etc.) for concrete wrongdoing, violation of the divine law, sin: Jhn.8:46, Jas.1:15, al.; ποιεῖν (τὴν) ἁ., Jhn.8:34, 2Co.11:7, 1Jn.3:8; εἴχειν ἁ., Jhn.9:41 15:22, 24 19:11, 1Jn.1:8; in pl. ἁμαρτίαι, sin in the aggregate, 1Th.2:16 (see Milligan, in l.); ποιεῖν ἁμαρτίας, Jas.5:15; πλῆθος ἁμαρτιῶν, Jas.5:20, 1Pe.4:8; ἄφεσις ἁμαρτιῶν, Mat.26:28, Mrk.1:4, al.; ἐν ἁμαρτίαις εἶναι, 1Co.15:17; collectively, αἴρειν τὴν ἁ. τ. κόσμου, Jhn.1:29; ἀποθνήσκειν ἐν τῇ ἁ., Jhn.8:21.
= ἁμάρτημα, a sinful deed, a sin: Mat.12:31, Act.7:60, 1Jn.5:16. SYN.: see: ἁμάρτημα.
ἀμάρτυρος, -ον (μάρτυς), without witness: Act.14:17.

ἁμαρτωλός, -όν (ἁμαρτάνω), [in LXX chiefly for רָשָׁע ;] sinful, a sinner: of all men, 1Ti.1:15; of those especially wicked, 1Ti.1:9, 1Pe.4:18; p1., Mat.9:10-13 11:19 26:45, al. (see MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 102, 634)
invincible (frequently in cl.).
abstaining from fighting, non-combatant (Xen.). Metaphorical (cf. MM, VGT, see word), not contentious: 1Ti.3:3, Tit.3:2.
ἀμάω, -ῶ (in cl. chiefly poët.), to reap: Jas.5:4.
ἀμέθυστος, -ου, ἡ (accusative to Plut., ἀ- μεθύω, being regarded as an antidote against drunkenness) [in LXX: Exo.28:19 36:19 39:12 Eze.28:13 ;] amethyst, a purple quartz: Rev.21:20.

ἀμελέω, -ῶ (μέλει), [in LXX: Jer.4:17 (מָרָה) Jer.31:32 (בַּעַל), Wis.3:10, 2Ma.4:14 ;] (a) absol., to be careless, not to care: Mat.22:5; (b) with genitive, to be careless of, to neglect: 1Ti.4:14, Heb.2:3 8:9 (see MM, VGT, see word).

ἄ-μεμπτος, -ον (μέμφομσι), [in LXX chiefly for תָּם ;] blameless, free from fault (in π. of a marriage-contract; M, Th., I, 3:13; cf. MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:6, Php.2:15 3:6, 1Th.3:13 (WH, mg., -ως) Heb.8:7. SYN.: ἄμωμος, ἀνέγκλητος, ἀνεπίλημπτος, which see (Tr., Syn., § ciii)
ἀ-μέμπτως, adv. (ἄμεμπτος), [in LXX: Est.3:13 ;] blamelessly (Lft., Notes, 28, 89; MM, VGT, see word -ος): 1Th.2:10 3:13 WH mg., 5:23.
ἀμέριμνος, -ον (μέριμνα), [in LXX: Wis.6:15 7:23 ;] free from anxiety or care: Mat.28:14, 1Co.7:32 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀ-μετάθετος, -ον (μετατίθημι), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:1 3Mac 5:12 ;] immutable: Heb.6:18; as subat., τὸ ἀ., immutability, Heb.6:17 (see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀ-μετα-κίνητος, -ον (μετακινέω), immovable, firm: 1Co.15:58.
ἀ-μεταμέλητος, -ον (μεταμέλομαι), not repented of, unregretted: Rom.11:29, 2Co.7:10.
impenitent: Rom.2:5
= ἀμεταμέλητος (π., Philo, al.; see Deiss., BS, 257; MM, VGT, see word).
ἄμετρος, -ον (μέτρον), without measure: adverbially, εἰς τὰ ἄ., excessively, 2Co.10:13 10:15
As adj. (cf. Is, l.with), ὁ ἀ., Rev.3:14.

As adv., (a) in solemn assent to the statements or prayers of another (Nu, Ne, etc., ll. with): ὁ ἀ., 1Co.14:16; (b) similarly, at the end of one's own prayer or ascription of praise: Rom.1:25 15:33, Gal.1:5, 1Ti.1:17; (with) in the Gospels, exclusively, introducing solemn statements of our Lord, truly, verily: Mat.5:18, 26 Mrk.3:28 (see Swete, in l.), Luk.4:24, al.; ἀ. ἀ., always in Jhn.1:52 3:3 5:19, al.; τὸ ναί, καὶ . . . τὸ ἀ., 2Co.1:20 (on usage in π., see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀμήτωρ, -ορος, ὁ, ἡ (μήτηρ), without a mother (frequently in Gk. writers of the gods): ἀπάτωρ ἀ. of one without recorded genealogy, Heb.7:3 (cf. MM, VGT, see word)

ἀ-μίαντος, -ον (μιαίνω), [in LXX: Wis.3:13 4:2 8:20, 2Ma.14:36 2Mac 15:34 ;] undefiled, free from contamination (in π., of αἰθήρ; MM, VGT, see word): Heb.7:26 13:4, 1Pe.1:4, Jas.1:27. SYN.: ἄμωμος, ἄσπιλος (Cremer, 784)
Ἀμιναδάβ, ὁ indecl. (Heb עַמִּינָדָב), Amminadab: Mat.1:4, Luk.3:33 (WH om.).
ἄμμος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חוֹל ;] sand, sandy ground: Mat.7:26, Rom.9:27, Heb.11:12, Rev.18:1, Rev.20:8.
ἀμνός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for כֶּבֶשׂ ;] a lamb: figuratively, of Christ (DCG, ii, 620b), Jhn.1:29, 36, Act.8:32" (LXX), 1Pe.1:19 (cf. ἀρνίον; Cremer, 102, 635).
ἀμοιβή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀμείβομαι, to repay); [in Aq., Sm.: Pro.12:14, al. ;] requital, recompense: 1Ti.5:4 (for illustration from τ.. see MM, VGT, see word).
ἄμπελος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for גֶּפֶן ;] vine: Mat.26:29, Mrk.14:25, Luk.22:18 Jas.3:12; figuratively, of Christ, Jhn.15:1, 4-5; of his enemies (on the usage here, see MM, VGT, see word): Rev.14:18-19.
ἀμπελουργός, -οῦ, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX for כֹּרֵם ;] a vine dresser: Luk.13:7.
ἀμπελών, -ῶνος, ὁ (ἄμπελος), [in LXX for כֶּרֶם ;] a vineyard: Mat.20:1 ff. Mat.21:28 ff, Luk.13:6 20:9 ff., 1Co.9:7. Æschin., 49, 13; Diod., al.; see MM, VGT, see word; LS, see word ἀμπελουργεῖον.)
Ἀμπλιᾶτος (T, -ίατος; Rec. Ἀμπλιᾶς; see MM, VGT, 5.see), -ου, ὁ Ampliatus: Rom.1:6 8:8.

ἀμύνω [in LXX (mid.): Jos.10:13 (נָקַם), נָקַם), Psa.118:10-12 מוּל hi.), Isa.59:16 (יָשַׁע hi.), Wis.11:3, al. ;] to ward off, etc. Mid. (a) to defend oneself against; (b) to requite; (with) = act., to defend, assist (Isa, l.with): with accusative of person(s), Act.7:24 (MM, VGT, see word).

etymology: middle voice of a primary verb;

full.text: ἀμφίβληστρον, -ου, τό (ἀμφιβάλλω), [in LXX chiefly for חֵרֶם ;] something thrown around, as a garment; spec., a casting-net: Mat.4:18.

etymology: from a compound of the base of (G0297) (G0906)

full.text: ἀμφιέννυμι (ἕννυμι, to clothe), to clothe: Mat.6:30 11:8, Luk.7:25 (cf. ἀμφιάζω).
Ἀμφίπολις, -εως, ἡ Amphipolis, in Macedonia, so called because the river Strymon flowed around it: Act.17:1.
ἄμφοδον, -ου, το (ἀμφί ὁδός), [in LXX for אַרְמוֹן (Jer.17:27 30:10 49:27) ;] prop., a road around anything (RV, the open street): Mrk.11:4, Act.19:28, WH, mg.
ἀμφότεροι, -αι, -α (replaces ἄμφω in κοινή, V. M, Pr., 57; used of more than two, Pr., 80; MM, VGT, see word), both of two: Mat.9:17, al.
ἀ-μώμητος, -ου (μωμάομαι), blameless: 2Pe.3:14. SYN.: ἄμεμπτος (which see), ἀνέγκλητος, ἀνεπίλημπτος

ἄ-μωμος, -ον (μῶμος, which see), [in LXX chiefly for תָּמִים ]; of sacrificial victims, without blemish: of Christ, Heb.9:14, 1Pe.1:19; etbically, unblemished, faultless: Eph.1:4 5:27, Php.2:15, Col.1:22, Ju 24, Rev.14:5 (Cremer, 425, 788; MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: ἀμίαντος, ἄσπιλος
Ἀμών, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָמוֹן), Amon, King of Judah: Mat.1:10 (Rec.).
as in 2Ki, Lc. B (Α. Ἀμμών; Jos., Ἀμμών, "Αμωσσς), Amon: Mat.1:10.
Amos: Luk.3:25.

In apodosis, (i) with indic, imperfect or aor., expressing what would be or would have been if (εἰ with impf., aor. or plpf.) some condition were or had been fulfilled: Luk.7:39 17:6, Jhn.5:46, Gal.1:10, Mat.12:7 24:43, 1Co.2:8, Act.18:14, 1Jn.2:19, al. The protasis is sometimes understood (as also in cl.): Mat.25:27, Luk.19:23. In hypothetical sentences, expressing unreality, ἄν (as often in late writers, more rarely in cl.) is omitted: Jhn.8:39 15:24 19:11, Rom.7:7, Gal.4:15; (ii) with opt., inf., ptcp. (cl.; see LS, see word; M, Int., §275; M, Pr., 167:4).
In combination with conditional, relative, temporal, and final words;

subs.text: as in cl., with subj.,
in protasis with εἰ, in Attic contr. ἐάν, which see;

full.text: in iterative construction, with imperfect and aor. indic. (M, Pr., 167): Act.2:45 4:35, 1Co.12:2.
with optative, giving a potential sense to a question or wish: Act.8:31 26:29.
Elliptical constructions: εἰ μή τι ἄν (M, Pr., 169), 1Co.7:5; ὡς ἄν, with inf., as it were (op. cit. 167), 2Co.10:9.

etymology: a primary particle, denoting a supposition, wish, possibility or uncertainty

full.text: In phrases: ἀ. μέσον, among, between, with genitive, Mat.13:1-58 25:1-46, Mrk.7:31, 1Co.6:5 (M, Pr., 99), Rev.7:17 [so in LXX for בְּתוׄך׃]; ἀ. μέρος, in turn, 1Co.14:27 (both found in Polyb.; cf. MGr. ἀνάμεσα).
Distrib., apiece, by: Mat.20:9-10, Luk.9:3 (WH om.), Luk.9:14 Luk.10:1, Jhn.2:6, Rev.4:8.
Adverbially ("a vulgarism," B1., § 51, 5; cf. Deiss., BS, 139 f.), ἀ. εἷς ἕκαστος, Rev.21:21. As prefix, ἀ. signifies

subs.text: up: ἀναβαίνειν;
back: ἀνακάμπτειν.

full.text: a going up, an ascent (Pss, ll. with ?).
a step (LXX); p1., a flight of stairs: Act.21:35, 40. (On the formation -θμός, see MM, VGT, see word)

ἀναβαίνω [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה ;] to go up, ascend, (a) of persons: ἐπί συκομωρέαν, Luk.19:4; εἰς τ. πλοῖον, Mrk.6:51; εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα, Mat.20:17; εἰς τ. ἱερόν, with inf. (M, Pr., 205), Luk.18:10; with mention of place of departure, Mat.3:16 (ἀπό), Act.8:39 (ἐκ); (b) of things, to rise, spring up, come up: a fish, Mat.17:27; smoke, Rev.8:4; plants growing, Mat.13:7; metaphorically, of things coming up in one's mind (as Heb. לֵב אֵל עָלָה; 4Ki.12:4, al.), Luk.24:38, 1Co.2:9; of prayers, Act.10...
ἀνα-βάλλω [in LXX: Psa.78:21 89:38 (עָבַר), 1Ki.28:14, Psa.104:2 (עָטָה) ;] to defer, put off (MM, VGT, see word): mid., Act.24:22.
ἀνα-βιβάζω (causal of ἀναβαίνω), [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה hi., also for רָכַב hi., etc. ;] to make go up, draw up, as a ship (Xen.): σαγήνην, Mat.13:48 (metaphorically, MM, VGT, see word).
to look up: Mrk.8:24, al.; before εἰς, Mat.14:19, al. (Xen., Plat.).
to recover sight (Plat., Aristoph.; cf. MM, VGT, see word): Mat.11:5, Jhn.9:11, al
ἀνά-βλεψις, -εως, ή (ἀναβλέπω), [in LXX: Isa.61:1 (פְּקַח־קוֹחַ) ;] recovery of sight: Luk.4:18 (Lxx).
ἀνα-βοάω, -ῶ [in LXX for צָעַק, זָעַק, קָרָא, etc. ;] to cry out: Mat.27:46 (WH, ἐβόησεν; see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀναβολή, - ῆς, ἡ (ά αβάλλω), [in LXX for בּנף, etc. ;] delay: Act.25:17 (for exx. of other meanings, see MM, VGT, see word).
to bring back word, report (Æsch., Thuc., al.): Jhn.5:15 (WH, εἶπεν), Act.14:27 15:4, 2Co.7:7
Later, = ἀπαγγέλλω (MM, VGT, see word), to announce, declare (LXX; Cremer, 24): Mat.28:11 (WH, ἀπ-), Jhn.4:25 16:13-15, Act.19:18 20:20, 27 Rom.15:21, 1Pe.1:12, 1Jn.1:5.
ἀνα-γεννάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Sir prol.17 א (ΑΒאc παρα-) ;] to beget again: metaphorically, of spiritual birth, 1Pe.1:3 1:23 (cf. Cremer, 147; MM, VGT, see word).
to know certainly, know again, recognize.
Of written characters, to read: Mat.24:15, Mrk.13:14, Act.15:31 23:34, Eph.3:4; with accusative of thing

subs.text: , Mat.22:31, Mrk.12:10 Luk.6:3, Jhn.19:20, Act.8:30, 32, 2Co.1:13, Rev.1:3; with accusative of person

full.text: ἀναγκάζω (ἀνάγκη), [in LXX: Pro.6:7 (שֹׁטֵר), I Est.3:24, 1Ma.2:25, al. ;] to necessitate, compel by force or persuasion, constrain: with accusative, 2Co.12:11; id. with inf., Mat.14:22, Mrk.6:45, Luk.14:23, Act.26:11 (on the imperfect here, see Field, Notes, 141; M, Pr., 128 f., 247), Gal.2:14 6:12; pass., C. inf., Act.28:19, Gal.2:3 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).
necessary: Act.13:46, 1Co.12:22, 2Co.9:5, Php.2:25, Tit.3:14, Heb.8:3; comp. -αιότερον, Php.1:24.
Of persons connected by bonds of nature or friendship, near, intimate (Field, Notes, 118; ΜΜ, VGT, see word): ἀ. φίλοι, Act.10:24.
ἀναγκαστῶς adv., necessarily or by constraint: opposite to ἑκουσίως, 1Pe.5:2 (rare).

necessity: ἔχειν ἀ-, with inf., to be compelled, Luk.14:18 23:17 (Rec., R, mg.), 1Co.7:37, Ju 3, Heb.7:27; ἐξ ἀ., κατ᾽ ἀ., of necessity, 2Co.9:7, Heb.7:12, Phm 14; ἀ. μοι ἐπίκειται, n. is laid on me, 1Co.9:16; with inf. (= ἀναγκαῖον ἐστι), Mat.18:7, Rom.13:5, Heb.9:16, 23
force, violence, hence pain, distress (Diod., al.; LXX; see M, Th., 41; MM, VGT, see word; cf. θλίψις): Luk.21:23, 1Co.7:26, 1Th.3:7; pl. (see B1., § 32, 6; Swete, Mk., 153), ἐν ἀ., 2Co.6:4 12:10.
ἀνα-γνωρίζω [in LXX: Gen.45:1 (יָדַע hith.) ;] to recognize: Act.7:13 (WH, txt., ἐγνωρίσθη).
recognition (Hdt.).
reading (Plat., al.): of the public reading of Scripture (Milligan, NTD, 173, 210 f.): Act.13:15, 2Co.3:14, 1Ti.4:13 (Cremer, 158; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀν-άγω [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה hi. ;] to lead or bring up: before είς, with accusative loc., Mat.4:1, Luk.2:22 4:5 (WH om. είς, κ.τ.λ.), Act.9:39 16:34; of raising the dead (cl.), ἐκ νεκρῶν, Rom.10:7, Heb.13:20; to produce and set before, τ. λαῷ, Act.12:4 (MM, VGT, see word); in sacrificial sense (MM, 1 with), to offer, θυσίαν, Act.7:41. Mid., in nautical sense (Hom., Hdt., Thuc., al.), to put to sea: Luk.8:22, Act.13:13 16:11 18:21 20:3, 13 21:1-2 27:2, 4 27:12, 21 28:10-11 (cf.ἐπ-ανάγω).
to lift up and show, show forth, declare (cf. 2Ma.2:8, see MM, VGT, see word): Act.1:24.
to consecrate, set apart, (Strab., Plut., Anth.): Luk.10:1.
ἀνά-δειξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναδείκνυμι), [in LXX: Sir.43:6 ;] a sheaving forth, announcement: Luk.1:80.
to assume, undertake (in π. frequently as legal term: MM, VGT, see word): ἐπαγγελίας, Heb.11:17.
= cl. ὑποδέχομαι, to receive: of guests, Act.28:7.
to give forth, send up, as of plants (Hdt., al.).
to give up, yield, hand over (MM, VGT, see word): Act.23:33.

ἀνα-ζάω, -ῶ [in Al.: Gen.45:27 ;] to live again, regain life (cf. cl. ἀναβιόω; Cremer, 722; and for other exx., v MM, VGT, see word): metaphorically of moral revival, Luk.15:24 (WH, mg., ἔζησεν); of sin, Rom.7:9.

ἀνα-ζητέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.3:4 (דָּרַשׁ), Job.10:6 (בָּקַשׁ pi.), 2Ma.13:21 ;] to look for or seek carefully ("specially of searching for human beings, with an implication of difficulty": MM, VGT, see word): Luk.2:44-45 Act.11:25.
ἀνα-ζώννυμι [in LXX: Jdg.18:16, Pro.31:17 (חָגַר) ;] to gird up: figuratively, τ. ὀσφύας τ. διανοίας, 1Pe.1:13.
ἀνα-ζωπυργέω, -ῶ (ζωός, πῦρ), [in LXX: 1Ma.13:7 ;] to kindle afresh: metaphorically, 2Ti.1:6 (for vernac. exx., see MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνα-θάλλω (θάλλω, to flourish), [in LXX: Psa.28:7 (עָלַז), Eze.17:24 (פָּרַח hi.), Hos.8:9, Wis.4:4, Sir.5:1-15 ;] to revive: Php.4:10 (of MM, VGT, see word).
prop. = τὸ ἀνατιθέμένον, that which is laid by to be kept, a votive offering (as ἀνάθημα in 2Ma.2:13, Luk.21:5—where LT read -θεμα, see M, Pr., 46).

[As equiv. in LXX for חֵרֶם,] devoted, a thing devoted to God (see Driver, De., 98 f., and cf. Lev.27:28-29), hence; (a) of the sentence pronounced (Deu.13:15), a curse: Act.23:14; (b) of the object on which the curse is laid, accursed (Deu.7:26): Rom.9:3, 1Co.12:3 16:22, Gal.1:8-9 (see ICC on Ro.; Lft., Ga., ll. with; Cremer, 547; Tr., Syn., § v; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀνα-θεματίζω (ἀνίθεμα), [in LXX chiefly for חָרַם hi. (Num.21:2, 1Ki.15:3, al.), 1Ma.5:5 ;] to devote to destruction, declare or invoke ana­thema: absol., Mrk.14:71; ἐαντόν, to bind oneself under a curse: Act.23:12, 14 23:21 (cf. καταναθεματίζω, and on the occurrence of the word in π., see Deiss., LAE, 92 f.; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνα-θεωρέω, -ῶ to observe carefully, consider well: Act.17:23, Heb.13:7 (Diod., al.).

ἀνάθημα, -τος, τό (cf. ἀνάθημα, and see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX often as seel. for ἀνάθεμα (חָרַם), and in Num.21:3, Jdg.1:17 for חׇרְמָה, but prop. in 3Ma.3:17, al. ;] a gift set up in a temple, a votive offering: Luk.21:5 (LT, -θεμα).
ἀναιδία (Rec. -εία, as in cl.), -ας, ἡ (αἰδώς), [in LXX: Sir.25:22 ;] shamelessness, importunity: Luk.11:8 (for exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word).
a taking up or away (Thuc.).
a destroying, slaying, murder (Field, Notes, 116; MM, VGT, see word): Act.8:1.
to take up: mid., Act.7:21.
to take away, make an end of, destroy (for late exx. of various senses, see MM, VGT, see word);
ἀν-αίτιος, -ον (αἰτία), [in LXX: Deu.19:10, 13 21:8-9 (נָקִי), Dan LXX TH Sus1:62, always of αἷμα (of. MM, VGT, see word) ;] guiltless, innocent: Mat.12:5, 7.
trans., to set up.
Intrans., to sit up: Luk.7:15 (WH, mg., ἐκάθισεν), Act.9:40 (frequently in medical writings: MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνα-καινίζω (καινός), [in LXX: 2Ch.15:8, Psa.103:5 104:30, La 5:21 (חָדַשׁ pi., hith)., Psa.39:2 (עָכַר ni.), 1Ma.6:9 ;] to renew: Heb.6:6 (Isocr., Plut.).
ἀνα-καινόω, -ῶ = ἀνακαινίζω (cf. MM, VGT, see word), to make new: 2Co.4:16, Col.3:10 (see Cremer, 323).

ἀνακαίνωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνακαινόω), renewal: Rom.12:2, Tit.3:5 (Cremer, 324; MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: παλινγενεσία, in NT, new birth, of which ἀ. is the conse­quent renewal or renovation, in which man as well as God takes part (see Tr., Syn., § xviii)
ἀνα-καλύπτω [in LXX chiefly for גָּלָה ni., pi. ;] to unveil: metaphorically of removing hindrance to perception of spiritual things, 2Co.3:14 3:18.
trans., to bend or turn back.
Intrans., to return: Mat.2:12, Act.18:21, Heb.11:15; metaphorically (cf. MM, VGT, see word), Luk.10:6.
in cl., as pass. of ἀνατίθημ, to be laid up, laid: Mrk.5:40 Rec.

In late writers (cf. MM, VGT, see word) = κεῖσθαι, κατακεῖσθαι, to recline at table: Mat.26:20; part. ἀνακείμενος, Mat.9:10 22:10-11 26:7, Mrk.6:26 14:18 16:14, Luk.22:27, Jhn.6:11 12:2 13:23, 28. SYN.: ἀνακλίνω, ἀναπίπτω, the latter denoting an act rather than a state and thus in Jhn.13:25 differing from ἀνάκειμαι (see. Jhn.13:23) by indicating a change of position.
ἀνα-κεφαλαιόω, ῶ (see: κεφαλαιόω) [in Th., Al.: Psa.72:20 ;] to sum up, gather up, present as a whole: mid., Rom.13:9, Eph.1:10 (on WH. see Lft., Notes, 321 L; AR, in l; Cremer, 354, 748).

ἀνα-κλίνω [in LXX: 3Ma.5:16 ;] to lay upon, lean against, hence, (a) to lay down: Luk.2:7; (b) to make to recline: Mrk.6:39, WH, mg., Luk.12:37. Pass., to lie back, recline: Mat.8:11 14:19, Luk.13:29. SYN.: ἀνάκειμαι (which see), ἀναπίπτω
to drive back , (Odyssey by Homer)
to beat back an assailant, (Thucydides)
to stop:—; Pass. to be stopped, stop short , τινός from a thing, (Lucian)
ἀνα-κράζω [in LXX for קָרָא, etc. ;] to cry out, shout: Mrk.1:23 6:49, Luk.4:33 8:28 23:18.

ἀνα-κρίνω [in LXX: 1Ki.20:12 (חָקַר), Dan LXX Su 1:13, LXX, TH Su 1:48, 51 ;] to examine, investigate, question (Lft., Notes, 181 f.): Act.17:11, 1Co.2:14-15 4:3-4 9:3 10:25 10:27 14:24; in forensic sense (MM, VGT, see word; esp. of examination by torture; see Field, Notes, 120 f.), Luk.23:14, Act.4:9 12:19 24:8 28:18. SYN.: see: ἐξετάζω
ἀνά-κρισις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: 3Ma.7:5 ;] an examination: spec. of legal preliminary investigation, Act.25:26 (see MM, VGT, see word).
άνα-κύπτω [in LXX: Job.10:15 (נָשָׂא רֹאשׁ), Dan LXX, Su 1:35 ;] to lift oneself up; (a) bodily; Luk.13:11, Jhn.8:7, 10; (b) mentally, to be elated: Luk.21:28 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
to take up, take to oneself: Act.7:43 20:13-14 23:31, Eph.6:13, 16, 2Ti.4:11 (for late exx., v MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνά-λημψις, -εως, ἡ (κοινή form of ἀνάληψις; see Th., Gr., 108 f.), a taking up: Luk.9:51 (MM, VGT, see word). ἀνά-ληψις, -εως, ἡ, Rec. for ἀνάλημψις, which see
to expend.
to consume, destroy: Luk.9:54, Gal.5:15, 2Th.2:8, Rec. WH, mg.
ἀναλογία, -ας, ἡ (λόγος), [in Al.: Lev.27:1-34"18 ;] proportion (MM, VGT, see word): Rom.12:6 (cf. Cremer, 397).
ἀνα-λογίζομαι [in LXX: Wis.17:13 א, 2Ma.12:43 A, 3Ma.7:7 ;] to consider: Heb.12:3 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἄναλος, -ον (ἅλς), [in Aq.: Eze.13:10-11, 15 22:28 ;] saltless, insipid: Mrk.9:50.
ἀνά-λυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναλύω), a loosing, e.g. of a vessel from its moorings, hence, departure: from life, 2Ti.4:6.
to unloose.
to unloose for departure, depart (MM, VGT, see word): from life, Php.1:23
to return, Luk.12:36.
without missing, unerring (Xen.).
In moral sense, faultless (Plat.), without sin: Jhn.8:7 (see Cremer, 102, 634; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνα-μένω [in LXX for קָוָה pi. ;] to await "one whose coming is expected, perhaps with the added idea of patience and confidence" with accusative, 1Th.1:10 (see M, Th., in l; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀνα-μιμνήσκω [in LXX for זָכַר hi. ;] to remind, call to one's remembrance: with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.4:17; with inf., 2Ti.1:6. Pass., to remember, call to mind: Mrk.11:21 14:72, 2Co.7:15, Heb.10:32.

ἀνάμνησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναμιμνήσκω), [in LXX: Psa.38:1-22 70:1-5 title. (זָכַר hi.), Lev.24:7 (אַזְכָּרָה), Num.10:10 (זִכְרוֹן), Wis.16:6 ;] remembrance: εἰς τ. ἐμὴν ἀ., Luk.22:19 (WH om.), 1Co.11:24-25; ἀ. ἁμαρτιῶν, Heb.10:3 (see Abbott, Essays, 122 ff.; DCG, ii, 74a). SYN.: ὑπόμνησις (see Tr., Syn., § cvii).
ἀνα-νεόω, -ῶ (νέος), [in LXX: Job.33:24, Est.3:13, 1; Ezr.3.1-13, 4Ma.8:1-29 ;] to renew: pass., Eph.4:23 (see Cremer, 428; MM, VGT, see word)
ἀνα-νήφω to return to soberness: metaphorically, 2Ti.2:26 (of. ἐκνήφω).
of Jerusalem: Act.5:1, 3 5:5.
Of Damascus: Act.9:10-13, 17 22:12.
High Priest: Act.23:2 24:1.
ἀν-αντί-ρητος (Τ, -ρρητος, -ον (ῥητός, spoken), [in Sm.: Job.11:2 33:13 ;] not to be contradicted, undeniable: Act.19:36 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-αντι-ρήτως (T, -ρρήτως), adv., without contradiction: Act.10:29.
ἀν-άξιος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἄξιος), [in LXX: Jer.15:19 א2 (זָלַל), Est.8:13, Sir.25:8 ;] unworthy: with genitive, 1Co.6:2 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀναξίως (see supr.), adv., [in LXX: 2Ma.14:42 ;] in an unworthy manner: 1Co.11:27.

ἀνά-παυσις, -εως, ή (ἀνάπαύω) [in LXX chiefly for נוּחַ and its derivatives, שַׁבָּת and its cognates (Ex, Le) ;] cessation, rest, refreshment: Mat.11:26 12:43, Luk.11:24, Rev.4:8 14:11. SYN.: ἄνεσις (lit, the relaxation of the strings of a lyre), prop. signifies the rest or ease which comes from the relaxation of unfavour­able conditions, as, e.g. affliction: ἀνάπ., the rest which comes from the temporary cessation of labour (see Tr., Stn., § xl; Cremer, 827; MM, VGT, see word)

ἀνα-παύω, [in LXX for fourteen different words, chiefly נוּחַ, also רָבַץ, שָׁאַן, etc. ;] to give intermission from labour, to give rest, refresh: Mat.11:28, 1Co.16:18, Phm 20; pass., Phm 7, 2Co.7:13. Mid., to take rest, enjoy rest: Mat.26:45, Mrk.6:31 14:41, Luk.12:19, Rev.6:11 14:13; as in Heb. ofIs 11:2 (עַל נוּחַ), τὸ πνεῦμα ἐφ᾽ ὑᾶμς ἀ., 1Pe.4:14. (In π. this word is used as a technical agricultural term; see MM, VGT, see word; and cf. Le 2634 f.; Cremer, 826.)
ἀνα-πείθω [in LXX: Jer.29:8 (נָשָׁא hi.), 1Ma.1:11 ;] to per­suade, incite: Act.18:13 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
to send up, (a) to a higher place (Æsch., Plat., al.); (b) to a higher authority (Deiss., BS, 229; MM, VGT, see word; cf. also Field, Notes, 140): Luk.23:7, 15, Act.25:21.
to send back (Pind.): Luk.23:11, Phm 11.
ἀνά-πηρος (WH, -ειρος; see Field, Notes, 67), -ον (πηρός, maimed) [in LXX: Tob.14:2 א, 2Ma.8:24 ;] maimed, crippled: Luk.14:13, 21.
(cl.) to fall back.

In late writers = ἀνα-κλίνομαι, to recline for a repast (MM, VGT, see word): at table, Luk.11:37 14:10 17:7 22:14, Jhn.13:12 21:20; on the ground, Mat.15:35 Mrk.6:40 8:6 Jhn.6:10; to lean back, Jhn.13:25 (T, ἐπιπεσών; see: ἀνάκειμαι, ad fin.). SYN.: ἀνάκειμαι (which see), ἀνακλίνομαι

to fill up, make full (in π. of com­pleting contracts and making up rent; cf. MM, VGT, see word): τόπον, take one's place (cf. Heb. מָקוֹם שָׁלַם), 1Co.14:16; άμαρτίας, complete the number, 1Th.2:16; τ. νόμον, observe perfectly, Gal.6:2; pass., προφητεία, fulfilled, Mat.13:14.
to supply: τό ὑστέρημα, 1Co.16:17, Php.2:30 (Cremer, 838).
ἀναπολόγητος, -ον (ἀπολογεόμαι), without excuse, inexcusable (in Polyb., al., as a forensic term; see Lft., Notes, 252): Rom.1:20 2:1.
ἀνα-πτύσσω [in LXX for פָּרַשׂ,etc. ;] to unroll: τ. βιβλίον, Luk.4:17 (WH, R, ἀνοίξας).
ἀν-άπτω [in LXX chiefly for יָצָא ;] to kindle: Luk.12:49, Jas.3:5 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-αρίθμητος, -ον (ἀριθμέω), [Job.31:25, al.], innumerable: Heb.11:12.
to shake out, shake back, move to and fro (Thuc., a1.).
In late writers (Diod., al.; see MM, VGT, see word), to stir up; metaphorically, to excite: τ. ὄχλον, Mrk.15:11; τ. λαόν, Luk.23:5.
ἀνα-σκευάζω (σκεῦος, a vessel), prop, to pack up baggage, hence, to dismantle, ravage, destroy; metaphorically, to unsettle, subvert (MM, VGT, see word): ψυχάς, Act.15:24.
ἀνα-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX for לָקַח, עָלָה hi. ;] to draw up: Luk.14:5, Act.11:10 (in π. of pulling up barley; MM, VGT, see word).
a raising up, awakening, rising (in Inscr. of the erection of a monument, see MM, VGT, see word): Luk.2:34.

a rising from the dead (see DCG, ii, 605b); (a) of Christ: Act.1:22 2:31 4:33 Rom.6:5, Php.3:10, 1Pe.3:21; ἐξ ἀ. νεκρῶν, Rom.1:4 (ICC, in l); ἐκ νεκρῶν, 1Pe.1:3; (b) of persons in OT hist. (e.g. 3Ki.17:17 ff.): Heb.11:35; (with) of the general resurrection: Mat.22:23, 28 22:30 Mrk.12:18, 23 Luk.20:27, 33 20:36, Jhn.11:24, Act.17:18 23:8 24:15, 2Ti.2:18; ἀ. ἐκ νεκρῶν, Luk.20:35, Act.4:2; τῶν νεκρῶν, Mat.22:31, Act.17:32 23:6 24:21 26:23, 1Co.15:12-13 15:21 15:42, Heb.6:2; ἀ. ζωῆς, resurrection to life (cf. 2Ma.7:14, ἀ. εἰς ζωήν) and ἀ. τ. κρίσεως, τ. to judgment, Jhn.5:29; ἀ. τ. δικαίων, Luk.14:14; κρείττων ἀ., Heb.11:35; on ἡ ἀ. ἡ πρώτη, Rev.20:5-8, see Swete, in l, Weste. on Jhn.5:1-47, but see also Thayer, see word; by meton. of Christ as Author of ἀ., Jhn.11:25 (see DB, iv, 231; Cremer, 307).

ἀνα-στατόω, ῶ (ἀνάστατος, driven from home; ἀνίστημι), [in LXX: Dan.7:23 (דּוּשׁ; also in Aq., and in π. (see Deiss., LAE, 80 f.; MM, VGT, see word),] to stir up, excite, unsettle: with accusative; (a) to tumult and sedition: Act.17:6 21:38; (b) by false teaching: Gal.5:12 (see Milligan, NTD, 73 f.).
to impale (Hdt.).
to raise on a cross, crucify (Polyb., al.).
to crucify again: Heb.6:6 (see Westc., in l).
ἀνα-στενάζω [in LXX: La 1:4 (אָנַח ni.), Sir.25:17), Da TH Sus 1:22, 2Ma.6:30 ;] to sigh deeply: Mrk.8:12.
to overturn: Jhn.2:15.
to turn back, return: Act.5:22 15:16.
