to turn hither and thither; pass., to turn oneself about, sojourn, dwell: Mat.17:22 Rec.; metaphorically (like Heb. הָלַךְ, in κοινή writers and in π.; see Deiss., LAE, 315; BS, 88, 194; MM, VGT, see word), to conduct oneself, behave, live: 2Co.1:12, Eph.2:3, 1Ti.3:15, Heb.10:33 13:18, 1Pe.1:17, 2Pe.2:18. SYN.: περιπατέω (Hellenistic), πολιτεύω
a turning down or back, a wheeling about (Soph., Thuc., al.).

In late writers (Polyb., al.; see: ἀναστρέφω, and cf. Hort on Jas.3:13; MM, VGT, see word), manner of life, behaviour, conduct: Gal.1:13, Eph.4:22, 1Ti.4:12, Heb.13:7, Jas.3:13, 1Pe.1:15 1:18 2:12 3:1-2 3:16, 2Pe.2:7 3:11.
ἀνα-τάσσομαι [in LXX only as seel. (Ald.) in Ecc.2:20 ;] to arrange in order, bring together from memory (Blass., Phil. Gosp., 14 ff.; MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:1.
trans., to cause to rise: Mat.5:45.

Intrans., to rise: φῶς, Mat.4:16 (= Isa.9:1); ὁ ἥλιος, Mat.13:6, Mrk.4:6 16:2, Jas.1:11; νεφέλη, Luk.12:54; φωσφόρος, 2Pe.1:19; ὁ Κύριος, prob. with ref. to metaphorically of sun or star, Heb.7:14 (cf. ἐξ-ανατέλλω).
ἀνα-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for חָרַם (Cremer, 546) ;] to lay upon, set up, etc. Mid. -εμαι, in late writers (Plut., al.; see also MM, VGT, see word), to set forth, declare: Act.25:14, Gal.2:2.
a rising: of light, Luk.1:78.
the sun-rising, the east (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.2:2, 9, Rev.21:13; ἀ. ἡλίου, Rev.7:2 16:12 (WΗ, p1.); pl., Mat.2:1 8:11 24:27, Luk.13:29.
ἀνα-τρέπω [in LXX for דָּחָה, הָדַף, etc. ;] to overturn, destroy: Jhn.2:15 WH, txt.; metaphorically, to subvert (MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.2:18, Tit.1:11.
ἀνα-τρέφω [in LXX: Wis.7:4 B, 4Ma.10:2 4Mac 11:15 א ;] to nurse up, nourish, educate, bring up: Luk.4:16, WΗ, mg., Act.7:20-21 22:3.
ἀνα-φαίνομαι [in LXX for צָדַק hi., גָּלַשׁ ;] to bring to light, make to appear: ἀναφάναντες τ. Κύπρον, i.e. having sighted C.: Act.21:3 WH; pass., to appear, be made manifest: Luk.19:11

to carry or lead up: with accusative of person(s), Mat.17:1, Mrk.9:2; pass., Luk.24:51 (WH, rejects, R, mg. omits); ἀ. τ. ἁμαρτίας ἐπί τ. ξύλον (see Deiss., 135, 88 f.; ICC, in l; MM, VGT, see word): 1Pe.2:24.
In LXX and NT, to bring to the altar, to offer (see Hort on 1Pe, l.with): θυσίας, etc., Heb.7:27 13:15, 1Pe.2:5; ἐπί τ. θυσιαστήριον, Jas.2:21 (see Mayor, in l).
to bear, sustain (cf. Num.14:33, Isa.53:12): Heb.9:28.
ἀνα-φωνέω, -ῶ [in LXX for שָׁמַע hi., זָכַר hi. ;] to cry out, exclaim: Luk.1:42 (Arist., al.).
ἀνά-χυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναχέω, to pour out), a pouring out, overflowing, excess: metaphorically, 1Pe.4:4 (MM, VGT, see word).
to go back.
to withdraw: Mat.9:24; frequently in sense of avoiding danger (MM, VGT, see word), Mat.2:12 (but see Thayer), Mat.2:13-14, 22 4:12 12:15 14:13 15:21 27:5, Mrk.3:7, Jhn.6:15, Act.23:19 26:31.
ἀνά-ψυξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναψύχω), [in LXX: Exo.8:15 (רְוָחָה) ;] a refreshing: Act.3:19.
ἀνα-ψύχω [in LXX for נָפַשׁ ni., חָיָה, etc. (frequently in sense of revive, refresh oneself) ;] to refresh: with accusative of person(s), 2Ti.1:16 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 588).
ἀνδραποδιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀνδράποδον, a slave, captured in war) a slave-dealer, kidnapper: 1Ti.1:10 (see MM, VGT, see word).

etymology: from a derivative of a compound of (G0435) (G4228)

full.text: Ἀνδρέας, -ου, ὁ Andrew, the Apostle: Mat.4:18 10:2, Mrk.1:16, 29 3:18 13:3, Luk.6:14, Jhn.1:41, 45 6:8 12:22, Act.1:13.

ἀνδρίζω [in LXX for חָזַק אָמַץ (Jos.1:6 ff., 1Ch.22:19, al.; in 2Ki.10:12, Psa.27:14 31:24 combined with κρατιοῦσθαι, as in 1Co, l.with) ;] to make a man of. Mid., to play the man (cf. MM, VGT, see word): 1Co.16:13.
Ἀνδρόνικος, -ου, ὁ Andronicus: Rom.16:7.
ἀνδρο-φόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: 2Ma.9:28 ;] a man-slayer: 1Ti.1:9 (cf. φονεύς, and see MM, VGT, see word).

ἀν-έγκλητος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐγκαλέω), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:31 ;] not to be called to account, unreprovable: 1Co.1:8, Col.1:22, 1Ti.3:10, Tit.1:6-7. SYN.: ἄμεμπτος, ἀνεπίλημπτος (see Tr., Syn., § ciii; Cremer, 742; MM, VGT, see word)
ἀν-εκδιήγητος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐκδιηγέομαι) inexpressible: 2Co.9:15 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-εκ-λάλητος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐκλαλέω), unspeakable: 1Pe.1:8.
ἀνέκλειπτος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐκλείπω), unfailing: Luk.12:33 (MM, VGT, see word).
αν-εκτός, -όν (also in late Gk. -ή, -όν; ἀνέχομαι), tolerable: compar., -ότερος, Mat.10:15 11:22, 24, Luk.10:12, 14
ἀν-ελεήμων, -ον (-ἀ, ἐλεήμων), [in LXX for אַכְזָר ;] without mercy: Rom.1:13.
ἀνεμίζω = Attic ἀνεμόω (ἄνεμος); pass., to be driven by the wind: Jas.1:6.

ἄνεμος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for רוּחַ ;] wind: Mat.11:7 14:24, 30 14:32, Mrk.4:37, 39 4:41 6:48, 51, Luk.7:24 8:23-24, Jhn.6:13, Act.27:7, 14-15, Jas.3:4, Rev.6:13 7:1; pl., Mat.7:25, 27 8:26-27, Luk.8:25, Act.27:4, Ju 12; οἱ τέσσαρες ἄ. τῆς γῆς, Rev.7:1; hence the four quarters of the heavens (see Deiss., BS, 248; MM, VGT, see word), Mat.24:31, Mrk.13:27; metaphorically, of variable teaching, Eph 414. SYN.: πνεῦμα, πνοή (and cf. θύελλα, λαῖλαψ).
ἀν-ένδεκτος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἔνδεκτος; ἐνδέχομαι), impossible, inadmissible: Luk.17:1.
ἀνεξεραύνητος (Rec. -εύνητος, as in Attic; M, Pr., 46), -ον (ερευνάω), [in Sm. (-ευ-): Pr., 25:3 ;] unsearchable: Rom.11:33.
ἀνεξί-κακος, -ον (fut., ἀνέξομαι, κακός) patiently forbearing (cf. ἀνεξικακία, Wis.2:19; and see MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.2:24.
ἀνεξιχνίαστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἐξιχνιάζω, to track out; ἴχνος) [in LXX: Job.5:9 9:10 34:24 (חֵקֶר אַיִן) ;] that cannot be traced out: Rom.11:33, Eph.3:8 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-επ-αίσχυντος, -ον (ἐπαισχύνομαι) not to be put to shame: 2Ti.2:15.

ἀν-επί-λημπτος (Rec. -λμπτος; Bl., § 6, 8), -ον (ἀ- ἐπιλαμβάνω) without reproach: 1Ti.3:2 5:7 6:14. SYN.: ἀμεμπτος, ἀνέγκλητος. It is stronger than these, for it implies not only that the man is of good report, but that he is deservedly so (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-έρχομαι [in LXX: 3Ki.13:12 (הָלַךְ) ;] to go up: Jhn.6:3, Gal.1:17-18 (cf. ἐπανέρχ-; and on its use of "going up " to the capital, MM, VGT, see word).

ἄνεσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνίημι), [in LXX: 2Es.4.22 (שָׁלוּ), 2Ch.23:15, I Est.4:62, Wis.13:13, Sir.15:20 26:10 ;] a loosening, relaxation: Act.24:23 (RV, indulgence; cf. MM, VGT, see word); by St. Paul, opposite to θλίψις, expressed or understood, relief: 2Co.2:12 7:5 8:13, 2Th.1:7. SYN.: ἀνάπαυσις (which see).
ἀν-ετάζω (ἀνά, ἐτάζω, to examine; see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Jdg.6:29 (דָּרַשׁ), Est.2:23 (בָּקַשׁ), Da TH Su 1:14 ;] to examine judicially: Act.22:24, 29..
ἄνευ prep. with genitive (rarer than χωρίς, which see; cf. Ellic. on Eph.2:12; MM, VGT, 42), without: Mat.10:29, 1Pe.3:1 4:9.
ἀν-εύ-θετος, -ον (see MM, VGT, see word) not well placed, not fit: Act.27:12.
ἀν-ευρίσκω (ἀνά, εὑρίσκω), [in LXX: 4Ma.3:14 ;] to find out by search, discover (see Field, Notes, 47 f.): Luk.2:16, Act.21:4.

ἀν-έχω [in LXX chiefly for אָפַק hithp. ;] to hold up; in NT always mid., to bear with, endure: in cl. most frequently with accusative, but in NT with genitive of person(s), Mat.17:17, Mrk.9:19, Luk.9:41, 2Co.11:1 11:19, Eph.4:2, Col.3:13; before μικρόν τι, with genitive of person(s) and with genitive of thing(s), 2Co.11:1; with dative of thing(s), 2Th.1:4 (v M, Th., in l); before εἴ τις, 2Co.11:20; absol., 1Co.4:12, 2Co.11:4; to bear with = to listen to, with genitive of person(s), Act.18:14; w...
ἀνεψιός, -οῦ, ὁ (cf. Lat. nepos), [in LXX: Num.36:11 ( בֵּן דּוֹד), Tob.7:2 9:6 א ;] a cousin: Col.4:10 (MM, VGT, see word).
anise, dill , (Aristophanes Comicus); ionic ἄννησον or ἄνησον (Herdotus Historicus); aeolic ἄννητον, or ἄνητον, (Sapho).

ἀν-ήκω (ἀνά, ἥκω), [in LXX: Jos.23:14 (בּוֹא), 1Ki.27:8, Sir. prol.10, 1, 2 Mac6 ;] prop., to have come up to; in later writers, impers. it is due, it is befitting: in ethical sense (MM, VGT, see word), Eph.5:4, Col.3:18; τὸ ἀνῆκον, Phm 8.
ἀν-ήμερος, -ον (ἀ-, ἥμερο ), not tame, savage (MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.3:3.
As opposite to a woman, Act.8:12, 1Ti.2:12; as a husband, Mat.1:16, Jhn.4:16, Rom.7:2, Tit.1:6.
As opposite to a boy or infant, 1Co.13:11, Eph.4:13, Jas.3:2.

In appos. with a noun or adj., as ἀ. ἁμαρτωλός, Luk.5:8; ἀ. προφήτης, 24:19; frequently in terms of address, as ἀ. ἀδελφοί, Act.1:16; and esp. with gentilic names, as ἀ. Ἰουδαῖος, Act.22:3; ἀ. Ἐφέσιοι, 19:35.
In general, a man, a male person: = τις, Luk.8:41, Act.6:11. SYN.: ἄνθρωπος, which see (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
in pres., impf., fut. and 1 aor. act., causal, to set against.
In mid, and pass., also pf. and 2 aor. act., to withstand, resist, oppose: with dative, Mat.5:39, Luk.21:15, Act.6:10 13:8, Rom.9:19 13:2, Gal.2:11, Eph.6:13, 2Ti.3:8 4:15, Jas.4:7, 1Pe.5:9.
to make a mutual agreement (Dem., Polyb.).
to acknowledge fully, confess (Diod., Polyb., cf. 1Esdras, l.with).
C. dative of person(s), to declare one's praises, speak fully in prayer or thanksgiving, give thanks to (cf. Ps, l.with): Luk.2:38 (Cremer, 771; MM, VGT, see word).
ἄνθος, -εος, τό [in LXX for צִיץ, etc. ;] a flower: Jas.1:10-11. 1Pe.1:24" (LXX) .
ἀνθρακιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (ἄνθραξ), [in LXX: Sir.11:32, 4Ma.9:20 ;] a heap of burning coals: Jhn.18:18 21:9.
ἄνθραξ, -ακος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for גֶּחֶל ;] coal, charcoal: . ἄ. πυρός, a burning coal, Rom.12:20.
ἀνθρωπ-άρεσκος, -ον (ἄνθρωπος, ἄρεσκος, pleasing), [in LXX: Psa.53:5 ;] studying to please men: Eph.6:6, Col.3:22 (Cremer, 642; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀνθρώπινος, -η, ον (ἄνθρωπος), [in LXX for אָדָם, אֱנוֹשׁ ;] human, belonging to man: χεῖρες, Act.17:25; σοφία, 1Co.2:13; φύσις, Jas.3:7; κτίσις, 1Pe.2:13 (MM, VGT, see word); ἀ. ἡμέρα, opposite to ἡ ἡμ. (1Co.3:13, God's Judgment-Day), human judgment, 1Co.4:3 (see Lft., Notes, 198); πειρασμὸς ἀ., temptation such as man can bear (AV, such as is common to man, see Field, Notes, 175), 1Co.10:13; ἀνθρώπινον λέγω, I speak in human fashion, with words not properly weighed, Rom.6:19 (see Field, Notes, ...
ἀνθρωποκτόνος, -ον (κτείνω, to kill) a murderer, manslayer (Eur.; see MM, VGT, see word): Jhn.8:44, 1Jn.3:16. SYN.: φονεύς, ἀνδροφόνος (see Tr., Syn, § 83)

generically, a human being, male or female (Lat. homo): Jhn.16:21; with art., Mat.4:4 12:35, Mrk.2:27, Jhn.2:25, Rom.7:1, al; disting. from God, Mat.19:6, Jhn.10:33, Col.3:23, al.; from animals, etc., Mat.4:19, Luk.5:10, Rev.9:4, al.; implying human frailty and imperfection, 1Co.3:4; σοφία ἀνθρώπων, 1Co.2:5; ἀνθρώπων ἐπιθυμίαι, 1Pe.4:2; κατὰ ἄνθρωπον περιπατεῖν, 1Co.3:3; κατὰ ἄ. λέγειν (λαλεῖν), Rom.3:5, 1Co.9:8; κατὰ ἄ- λέγειν, Gal.3:15 (cf. 1Co.15:32, Gal.1:11); by meton., of man's nature or condition, ὁ ἔσω (ἔξω) ἄ., Rom.7:22, Eph.3:16, 2Co.4:16 (cf. 1Pe.3:4); ὁ παλαιὸς, καινὸς, νέος ἄ., Rom.6:6, Eph.2:15 4:22, 24 Col.3:9, 10; joined with another subst., ἄ. ἔμπορος, a merchant, Mat.13:45 (WH, txt. om. ἄ.); οἰκοδεσπότης, Mat.13:52; βασιλεύς, 18:23; φάγος, 11:19; with name of nation, Κυρηναῖος, Mat.27:32; Ἰουδαῖος, Act.21:39; Ῥωμαῖος, Act.16:37; pl. οἱ ἄ., men, people: Mat.5:13, 16 Mrk.8:24, Jhn.4:28; οὐδεὶς ἀνθρώπων, Mrk.11:2, 1Ti.6:16.

Indef., ἄ. = τις, some one, a man: Mat.17:14, Mrk.12:1, al.; τις ἄ., Mat.18:12, Jhn.5:5, al.; indef. one (Fr. on), Rom.3:28, Gal.2:16, al.; opposite to women, servants, etc., Mat.10:36 19:10, Jhn.7:22, 23.

Definitely, with art., of some particular person; Mat.12:13, Mrk.3:5, al.; οὗτος ὁ ἄ., Luk.14:30; ὁ ἄ οὗτος, ἐκεῖνος, Mrk.14:71, Mat.12:45; ὁ ἄ. τ. ἀνομίας, 2Th.2:3; ἄ τ. θεοῦ (of Heb. אִישׁ אֱלֹהִים), 1Ti.6:11, 2Ti.3:17, 2Pe.1:21; ὁ υἰὸς τοῦ ἀ., see: υἱός. SYN.: ἀνήρ, which see (and cf. MM, VGT, 44; Cremer, 103, 635).
ἀνθ-υπατεύω (see next word), to be proconsul: Act.18:12 Rec. (see: ἀνθύπατος).

ἀνθύπατος -ου, ὁ (ἀντί, ὕπατος, altern. for ὑπέρτατος), supreme, a consul, one acting in place of a consul, a proconsul, the administrator of a senatorial province (cf. ἡγεμών, and see MM, VGT, 44): Act.13:7-8, 12 18:12 19:38.
to send up, produce, to send back.
to let go, leave without support: Heb.13:5 (cf. Deu.31:6; Hom., Iliad., ii, 71).
to relax, loosen (see Field, Notes, 124 f.): Act.16:26 27:40; hence, metaphorically, to give up, desist from: Eph.6:9.
ἀν-έλεος, -ον (Attic ἀνηλεής, ἀνελεήμων; MM, VGT, see word), merciless: Jas.2:13.
ἄνιπτος, -ον (ἀ. neg., νίπτω), unwashed: Mat.15:20, Mrk.7:5 (5 Rec).
causal, in fut. and 1 aor. act., with accusative, to raise up: Act.9:41; from death, Jhn.6:39, Act.2:32; to raise up, cause to be born or appear: Mat.22:24, Act.3:22, 26.

Intrans., in mid. and 2 aor act.; (a) to rise: from lying, Mrk.1:35; from sitting, Luk.4:16; to leave a place, Mat.9:9; pleonastically, as Heb. קוּם, before verbs of going, Mrk.10:1, al. (see Dalman, Words, 23; M, Pr., 14); of the dead, Mat.17:23, Mrk.8:31; before ἐκ νεκρῶν, Mat.17:9, Mrk.9:9; (b) to arise, appear: Act.5:36, Rom.15:12 (cf. ἐπ-, ἐξ- ἀνίστημι, and see Cremer, 306, 738; MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: ἐγείρω.
Ἄννα, -ας, ἡ (Heb. חָנָה), Anna, a prophetess: Luk.2:36.
Ἄννας, -α (FlJ, Ἄνανος, -ου), ὁ (Heb. חָנַן), Annas, the high priest: Luk.3:2, Jhn.18:13-24, Act.4:6.
not thought on, not understood (Hom., Plat.).
not understanding, foolish (Hdt., al., LXX): Luk.24:25, Rom.1:14, Gal.3:1, 3, 1Ti.6:9, Tit.3:3 (Cremer, 438, 790; MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: ἀσύνετος (see Tr., Syn., § LXXv)
ἀνοια, -ας, ἡ (ἄ-νοος, without understanding), [in LXX: Pro.14:8, 2215 (אִוֶּלֶת), Wis.15:18, al. ;] folly, foolishness: 2Ti.3:9; expressed in violent rage (cf. Plat., Tim., 86B): Luk.6:11.

trans., with accusative; a door or gate, Act.5:19 12:14, Rev.4:1; pass., Act.12:10 16:26-27; metaphorically of opportunity or welcome, Act.14:27, Col.4:3, Rev.3:20; pass., 1Co.16:9, 2Co.2:12, Rev.3:8; absol. (sc. θύραν), Act.5:23 12:16; with dative of person

subs.text: , of restoring sight, Mat.9:30 20:33, Jhn.9:1ff; 10.21, 11:37ff ; metaphorically, Act.26:18; ἀκοάς, with genitive of person
, of restoring hearing, Mrk.7:35.

full.text: Intrans. in 2 pf., ἀνέῳγα (M, Pr., 154); heaven, Jhn.1:51; τ. στόμα, before πρός, of speaking freely, 2Co.6:11 (cf. δι-ανοίγω and see MM, VGT, 45).
ἀν-οικο-δομέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בָּנָה, גָּדַר ;] to build. again, rebuild (MM, VGT, see word): Act.15:16.
ἄνοιξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνοίγω), an opening (in MGr., springtime): ἐν ἀ., as often as I open, Eph.6:19.

ἀνομία, -ας, ἡ (ἄνομος), [in LXX for עָוֺן, פָּשַׁע, פֶּשַׁע, תּוֹעֵבַה, רֶשַׁע, etc. ;] lawlessness, iniquity: Mat.7:23 13:41 23:28 24:12, Rom.6:19, 2Co.6:14, 2Th.2:3 2:7, Tit.2:14, Heb.1:9, 1Jn.3:4; in pl. (as LXX, Psa.31:1, al.; see 131., § 32, 6; Swete, Mk., 153), of acts or manifestations of lawlessness: Rom.4:7 (LXX), Heb.10:17. SYN.: see: ἁμάρτημα, ἄνομος
lawless, wicked: Mrk.15:28, Luk.22:37, Act.2:23, 1Ti.1:9, 2Pe.2:8; ὁ ἄ., 2Th.2:8 (= ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας, 2Th.2:3).
without law (= οἱ μὴ ὑπὸ νόμον, Rom.2:14): 1Co.9:21 (MM, VGT, see word). SYN.: see: ἄθεσμος
lawlessly (2Mac, l.with).
= χωρὶς νόμου, without law: Rom.2:12.

ἀν-ορθόω, -ῶ (ἀνά, ὀρθόω, to set straight, set up), [in LXX chiefly for כּוּן hi. ;] to set upright or straight again, restore: of persons, Luk.13:13, Heb.12:12; of things, σκηνήν, Act.15:16 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 807).
ἀν-όσιος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ὅσιος), [in LXX: Eze.22:9 (זִמָּה, Wis.12:4, 2Ma.7:34 2Mac 8:32, 3Ma.2:2 3Mac 5:7, 4Ma.12:11 ;] unholy, profane (Cremer, 464): 1Ti.1:9, 2Ti.3:2 (MM, VGT, see word).
in cl., a holding back, delaying (MM, VGT, see word).

forbearance, delay of punishment: Rom.2:4 3:26. SYN.: μακροθυμία, ὑπομονή. ἀ., forbearance, is the result and expression of μ., which involves the idea of tolerance, long-suffering, as God with sinners. ὑ. expresses patience with respect to things, as μ. with persons; it is active as well as passive, denotes not merely endurance but perseverance (see Tr., Syn., § liii; Lft., Notes, 259, 273; DB, ii, 47).
ἀντ-αγωνίζομαι depon., [in LXX: 4Ma.17:14 ;] to struggle against: before πρός, with accusative, Heb.12:4.

ἀντ-άλλαγμα, -τος, τό (ἀντί, ἄλλαγμα; ἀλλάσσω) [in LXX chiefly for מְחִיר ;] an exchange, the price received as an equivalent for an article of commerce: Mat.16:26, Mrk.8:37 (cf. Sir.26:14; and see Swete, Mk., l.with; Cremer, 90).
ἀντ-ανα-πληρόω, -ῶ (ἀντί, ἀναπληρόω), to fill up in turn: Col.1:24 (see Lft., in l; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀντ-απο-δίδωμι (ἀντί ἀποδίδωμι), [in LXX for שָׁלַם pi., גָּמַל, שׁוּב hi., etc. ;] to give back as an equivalent, recompense, requital (the ἀντί ex­pressing the idea of full, complete return; see Lft., Notes, 46); (a) in favourable sense: Luk.14:14, Rom.11:35, 1Th.3:9; (b) in unfavourable sense: Rom.12:19, 2Th.1:6, Heb.10:30.
ἀντ-από-δομα, -τος, τό (ἀνταποδίδωμι) [in LXX chiefly for גְּמוּל ;] ( = cl. -δοσις, which see) requital; (a) in favourable sense: Luk.14:12; (b) in unfavourable sense: Rom.11:9.
ἀντ-από-δοσις, -εως, ἡ (see supr.), [in LXX chiefly for גְּמוּל, שִׁלּוּם ;] recompense: Col.3:24 (MM, VGT, see word).

ἀντ-απο-κρίνομαι (ἀντί, ἀποκρίνω), [in LXX: Jdg.5:29, Job.16:9 (8) Job.32:12 (עָנָה) ;] to answer again, reply against: before πρός, with accusative of thing(s), Luk.14:6; with dative of person(s), Rom.9:20.
ἀντ-εῖπον (άντί, εἶτον), [in LXX for H7725 hi., דָבַר pi., עָנָה, etc. ;] 2 2 aor., without present in use, to speak against, gainsay: Luk.21:15, Act.4:14.
trans., to hold against.
Intrans., to withstand. Mid.
in cl., to hold out against.
(cf. MM, VGT, see word), to hold firmly to, cleave to: with genitive (see Bl., § 36, 2), Mat.6:24, Luk.16:13, 1Th.5:14 (see M, Th., in l), Tit.1:9.
prop, in local sense, over against, opposite, hence

instead of, in place of, for (Hom., etc.): Mat.5:38 17:27, Luk.11:11, 1Co.11:15, Heb.12:2; with artic. inf. (cl.), Jas.4:15; of succession, Mat.2:22; χάριν ἀ. χάριτος, Jhn.1:16 (M, Pr., 100); of price in exchange, Heb.12:16; λύτρον ἀ. πολλῶν, Mat.20:28, Mrk.10:45 (M, Pr., 105); of requital, Rom.12:17, 1Th.5:16, 1Pe.3:9 (cf. Wis.11:15); ἀνθ᾽ ὧν, because, Luk.1:20 19:44, Act.12:23, 2Th.2:10 (cl., LXX for אֲשֶׁר תַּחַת); id. therefore (cl., LXX), Luk.12:3; ἀ. τούτου (LXX for כֵּן נַעֲרָה)Eph.5:31. As a prefix, ἀντι- (before vowels ἀντ-, ἀνθ᾽-), denotes

subs.text: over against, ἀντιπέραν;
opposition, ἀντίχριστος;

full.text: ἀντι-βάλλω [in LXX: 2Ma.11:13 ;] to throw in turn, exchange: metaphorically, λόγους (cf. Lat. conferre sermons; see Field, Notes, 81), Luk.24:17.
ἀντι-δια-τίθημι in mid, to place oneself in opposition, oppose: 2Ti.2:25 (EV; but see Field, Notes, 215 f.; cf. MM, VGT, see word)
ἀντίδικος, -ον (δίκη), [in LXX for רִיב ;] as subst., an opponent in a lawsuit, adversary: Mat.5:25, Luk.12:58 18:3, 1Pe.5:8 (Cremer, 696; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀντί-θεσις, -εως, ή (τίθημι), opposition: 1Ti.6:20.
causal in Pres. imperfect fut. and 1 aor.; to replace, oppose.
Intrans. in pass. and 2 aor. act.;

subs.text: to supersede;
to resist: Heb.12:4.

full.text: to invite in turn , (Xenophon Historicus)
to lie opposite to.
to oppose, withstand, resist: with dative, Luk.13:17 21:15, Gal.5:17, 1Ti.1:10; as participial subst. (ὁ) ἀντικείμενος, 1Co.16:9, Php.1:28, 2Th.2:4, 1Ti.5:14 (Cremer, 746).

ἄντικρυς (Tr. -ύς, Rec. ἀντικρύ), adv. (ἀντί) [in LXX: Neh.12:8 (נֶגֶד), 3Ma.5:16 ;] in cl., outright; in κοινή (= cl. καταντικρύ), over against: Act.20:15 (see Bl., § 5, 4; 40, 7; Rutherford, NPhr., 500 f.; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀντι-λαμβάνω [frequently in LXX for חָזַק hi., סָמַךְ, etc ;] to take instead of or in turn. Mid., with genitive, to take hold of; (a) of persons, to help (see MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:54, Act.20:35; (b) of things, to partake of: 1Ti.6:2 (see Field, Notes, 210; Cremer, 386; and cf. συν-αντιλαμβάνω).

ἀντι-λέγω [in LXX: Hos.4:4 (רִיב hi.), Isa.50:5 (סוּג ni.) Isa.22:22 65:2, Sir.4:25, 3Ma.2:28, 4Ma.4:7 4Mac 8:2 ;] cοntradict, oppose, resist (see Field, Notes, 106; MM, VGT, see word); absol.: Act.28:19, Rom.10:21, Tit.1:9 2:9; with dative, Jhn.19:12, Act.13:45; with accusative and inf., Luk.20:27 T; pass., Luk.2:34, Act.28:22.
cl. a laying hold of, an exchange.
Hellenistic (LXX, π.) help: pl. of ministrations of deacons; 1Co.12:28 (DB, ii, 347 f.; Cremer, 386).
ἀντιλογία, -ας ἡ (ἀντιλέγω), [in LXX chiefly for רִיב ;] gain-saying, strife (the latter sense being found in π.; see MM, VGT, see word; cf. Field, Notes, 106): Heb.6:16 7:7 12:3, Ju 11.
to rail at or abuse in turn , (New Testament); Mid., with accusative rei, (Lucian)
ἀντί-λυτρον, -ου, τό [in Al.: Psa.49:9 ;] a ransom: 1Ti.2:6 (see CGT, in l; and cf. λύτρον).
ἀντι-μετρέω, -ῶ to measure in return: Luk.6:38 (WH, mg., μετρέω).
ἀντιμισθία, -ας, ἡ (ἀντίμισθος, for a reward) a reward, requital: in good sense, 2Co.6:13; in bad sense, Rom.1:27 (MM, VGT, see word).
Ἀντιοχεύς, -έως, ὁ a citizen of Antioch, an Antiochian: Act.6:5.
ἀντι-παρ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Wis.16:10 ;] to pass by opposite to: Luk.10:31-32 (MM, VGT, see word).
Ἀντίπας (T, Ἀντείπας), -α (in some MSS. it appears to be indecl.; but see M, Pro.12:1-28; it is abbrev. from Ἀντίπατρος), ὁ, Antipas: Rev.2:13.
Ἀντιπατρίς, -ίδος, ἡ Antipatris, bet. Joppa and Caesarea: Act.23:31.
ἀντί-περα (Rec. ἀντιπέραν, LTr. ἀντιπέρα) adv., = cl. ἀντιπέρας (MM, VGT, 49), on the opposite side: with genitive, Luk.8:26.
to fall against or upon (Arist., Polyb.).
to strive against, resist (Arist.): with dative, Act.7:51
ἀντι-στρατεύομαι to make war against: with dative, Rom.7:23.

ἀντι-τάσσω (Att..., -ττω) [in LXX for לוּץ hi., נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to range on battle against; mid., to set oneself against, resist: absol., Act.18:6; with dative, Rom.13:2, Jas.4:6 5:6, 1Pe.5:5 (MM, VGT, see word).
active striking back; metaphorically, resisting, adverse.

Pass. struck back; metaphorically, corres­ponding to (MM, VGT, see word); (a) as impression of a seal or copy of an archetype (τύπος) (RV, Like in pattern), Heb.9:24; (b) as the reality (of which τύπος is the copy or adumbration) (RV, after a true likeness), 1Pe.3:21 (Cramer, 357).

άντι-χριατος, -ον, ό Antichrist, "one who assuming the guise of Christ opposes Christ" (Westc., Epp. Jo., 70): 1Jn.2:18 Jn 2:22 Jn 4:3, 1Jn.1:7 pl. 1Jn.2:18 (cf. ψευδόχριστος, and see MM, VGT, see word).
prop., to bale out.
Generally, to draw water: absol., Jhn.2:8 4:15; ὕδωρ, Jhn.2:9 4:7 (on its use of the water made wine, see DCG, ii, 815a; MM, VGT, see word; Field, Notes, 84 f.).
ἄντλημα, -τος, τό (ἀντλέω), (a) prop., what is drawn (Diosc.); (b) a vessel to draw with, a bucket (Plut.; see Abbott, Essays, 88): Jhn.4:11.

ἀντοφθαλμέω, -ῶ (ἀντί, ὀφθαλμός), [in LXX: Wis.12:14 ;] to look in the face, look straight at (Polyb.). Metaphorical, to face, withstand (Wis, l.with, Polyb.): with dative, ἀ. τ. ἀνέμῳ, as nautical term, to beat up against the wind (see DB, ext., 366 f.; MM, VGT, see word): Act.27:15.
ἄνυδρος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ὕδωρ) [in LXX for צִיָּה, יְשִׁימוֹן (γῆ ἄ), etc. ;] waterless: τόποι, Mat.12:43, Luk.11:24; πηγαί, 2Pe.2:17; νεφέλαι, Ju 12 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀν-υπόκριτος, -ον (ά- neg., ὑποκρίνομαι) [in LXX: Wis.5:18 18:16 ;] unfeigned: Rom.12:9, 2Co.6:6, 1Ti.1:5, 2Ti.1:5, Jas.3:17, 1Pe.1:22 (Cremer, 380; MM, VGT, see word).

ἀνυπότακτος, -ον (ά- neg., ὑποτάσσω) [in Sm.: 1Ki.2:12 10:27 (for LXX, λοιμός, בֵּן בְּלִיַּעַל) ;] of things, not subject to rule: Heb.2:8; of persons, unruly: 1Ti.1:9, Tit.1:6, 10 (MM, VGT, see word).

ἄνω adv (ἀνά), (a) up, upwards: Jhn.11:41, Heb.12:15; (b) above (opposite to κάτω): Act_2:19; with art. ἡ, Gal.4:26, Php.3:14; τά, Jhn.8:23, Col.3:1-2; ἕως ἄ. (up to the brim), Jhn.2:7 (Cremer, 106; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνάγαιον (Rec. ἀνώγεον; on the form, see Rutherford, NPhr., 357 f.; MM, VGT, see word), -ου, τό (ἀνά, γῆ), an upper room: Mrk.14:15, Luk.22:12. SYN.: ὑπερῷον

ἄνωθεν adv (ἄνω), (a) from above: ἀπὸ ἄ., Mat.27:51, Mrk.15:38; ἐκ τῶν ἄ., Jhn.19:28; meaning, from heaven: Jhn.3:31 19:11, Jas.1:17 3:15, 17 . (b) from the first, from the beginning: Luk.1:3, Act.26:5; whence (with) anew, again: Jhn.3:3, 7, (so most, but see Meyer, in l; cf. Field, Notes, 86 f.); πάλιν ἄ., Gal.4:9 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀνωτερικός, -ή όν (ἀνώτερος) upper: Act.19:1.
ἀνώτερος, α, -ον [in LXX: Neh.3:25 (עֶלְיוֹן), Eze.41:7 (מַעַל), Tob.8:3 ;] only in neut., as adv. (cf. ἐξώτερος), (a) of motion, higher: Luk.14:10; (b) of rest, above, before: Heb.10:8.
ἀν-ωφελής, -ές (ἀ- neg., ὄφελος), [in LXX: Isa.44:10 (יָעַל בִּלְתִּי) Jer.2:8 (יָעַל לֹא), Pro.28:3, Wis.1:11 ;] unprofitable: Tit.3:9; neut. as subst., unprofitableness: Heb.7:18.
αξίνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for גַּרְזֶן, קַרְדֹּם ;] an axe: Mat.3:10, Luk.3:9.

Part One (a) of weight, worth (often with genitive, cf. Pr 315, 811), before πρός: Rom.8:18 (see Field, Notes, 157); (b) befitting, meet: with genitive, Mat.3:8, Luk.3:8 23:41, Act.26:20, 1Co.16:4 (see M, Pr., 216); absol., 2Th.1:3; (with) of persons, worthy;
Part Two α in good sense: with genitive of thing

subs.text: , Mat.10:37-38, Heb.11:38; β in bad sense; with genitive of thing

full.text: ἀξιόω, -ῶ (ἄξιος), [in LXX chiefly for בְּעָא, בָּקַשׁ; frequently in Wis.1:1-16, 2 Mac ;] (a) to deem worthy: with accusative and inf. (see MM, VGT, see word, and cf. κατ-αξιόω), Luk.7:7; id. and genitive of thing(s), 2Th.1:11; pass. with genitive of thing(s), 1Ti.5:17, Heb.3:3 10:29 (b) to think fit: with inf. (see M, Pr., 205), Act.15:38 28:22.

ἀξίως, adv. [in LXX: Wis.7:15 16:1, Sir.14:11 ;] worthily: Rom.16:2, Eph.4:1, Php.1:27; with genitive (frequently in Inscr.; Deiss., BS, 248; MM, VGT, 51), ἀ. τ. Κυρίου, Col.1:10; τ. θεοῦ 1Th.2:12, 3Jn.6.
ἀόρατος, -ον (ὁράω), [in LXX: Gen.1:2 (תֹּהוּ), Isa.45:3 (מִסְתָּר), 2Ma.9:5 ;] unseen, invisible: Rom.1:20, Col.1:15-16, 1Ti.1:17, Heb.11:27.

ἀπ-αγγέλλω [in LXX chiefly for נָגַד hi. ;] of a messenger, speaker, or writer, to report, announce, declare: with accusative of thing(s), Act.4:23, al.; with dative of person(s), Mat.2:8, al.; before ὃτι, Luk.18:37; πῶς, Luk.8:36; περί, Luk.7:18 13:1, Act.28:21, 1Th.1:9; λέγων, Act.22:26; with accusative and inf., Act.12:14; before εἰς, Mrk.5:14, Luk.8:34 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 25)
ἀπ-άγχω (ἄγχω, to press, strangle), [in LXX: 2Ki.17:23 (חָנַק), Tob.3:10 ;] to strangle; mid., to hang oneself (or, to choke; see M, Pr., 155): Mat.27:5.

ἀπ-άγω [in LXX for נָהַג, הָלַךְ hi., etc. ;] to lead away: Luk.13:15, Act.23:17 24:7 (R, mg.), 1Co.12:2; esp. of leading to trial (so as law term in Attic), prison and death (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.26:57 27:2, 31, Mrk.14:44, 53 15:16, Luk.21:12 22:66 23:26, Act.12:19; of the direction of a way: Mat.7:13-14 (cf. συν-απ-άγω).
ἀ-παίδευτος, -ον (παιδεύω), [in LXX for כְּסִיל, etc., chiefly in Wis. lit. ;] uninstructed, ignorant: 2Ti.2:23.
ἀπ-αίρω [in LXX chiefly for נָסַע ;] to lift off, hence, to take away; pass.: Mat.9:15, Mrk.2:20, Luk.5:35.
ἀπ-αιτέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.15:2-3, al. (נָגַשׂ), Sir.20:15, Wis.15:8, al. ;] to ask back, demand back: Luk.6:30 12:20 (MM, VGT, see word).
prop., to cease to feel pain for (Thuc., ii, 61).
In late Gk. (a) to despair (Polyb., i, 35, 5); (b) to become callous, reckless (Polyb., xvi, 12, 7; MM, VGT, see word): Eph.4:19.

ἀπ-αλλάσσω [in LXX for סוּר hi., etc. ;] to remove, release: Heb.2:15; pass., to depart: Act.19:12; in legal sense (MM, VGT, see word), before ἀπό, with genitive of person(s), to be quit of: Luk.12:58 (Cremer, 90, 632).
ἀπ-αλλοτριόω, -ῶ [in LXX for זוּר, H5110 etc. ;] to alienate, estrange; pass.: Eph.2:12 4:18, Col.1:21 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 95, 633).
ἀπαλός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for רַךְ ;] tender: Mat.24:32, Mrk.13:28.
to go to meet.
to meet; with dative: Mrk.14:13, Luk.17:12 (WH, mg., ὑπ-; in Rec. frequently as see1. for ὑπ-, which see).

ἀπάντησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπαντάω), [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] usually with seel., ὑπ-; a meeting; εἰς ἀ., with genitive or dative, to meet: Mat.25:6 27:32 (WH, txt., omits), Act.28:15, 1Th.4:17 (see M, Th., in l; M, Pr., 14, 242; MM, VGT, see word; Lft., Notes, 69).

ἅπαξ adv., [in LXX for אֶחָד, פַּעַם ;] (a) once: 2Co.11:25, Heb_9:26-27; ἔτι ἅ., Heb.12:26-27; ἅ. τ. ἐνιαυτοῦ, Heb.9:7; καί ἅ. κ. δίς, twice: Php.4:16, 1Th.2:18; (b) once for all: Heb.6:4 9:28 10:2, 1Pe.3:18, Ju 3 5 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀ-παρά-βατος, -ον (παραβαίνω), inviolable, and so unchangeable: Heb.7:24 (see Westc., in l; Cremer, 653; MM, VGT, see word).
ἀ-παρα-σκεύαστος, -ον (παρασκευάζω) unprepared: 2Co.9:4.

ἀπ-αρνέομαι (-οῦμαι) depon., [in LXX: Isa.31:7 (מָאַס) ;] to deny, i.e. to refuse to recognize, to ignore: with accusative, of oneself (DCG, ii, 598 f.), Mat.16:24, Mrk.8:34 (MM, VGT, see word), Luk.9:23 (WH, mg., txt., ἀρν-) of Peter's denials of Christ, Mat.26:34-35, 75, Mrk.14:30-31, 72, Luk.22:34, 61; pass., Luk.12:9 (Cremer, 111).
ἀπάρτι (WH, ἀπ᾽ ἄρτι; cl., ἀπαρτί, ν. MM, VGT, see word); adv., [not in LXX, where עַתָּה is rendered by ἀπὸ τοῦ νῦν ;] from now, henceforth: Jhn.13:19 14:7, Rev.14:13.
ἀπαρτισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀπαρτίζω, to finish), completion: Luk.14:28 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).
the beginning of a sacrifice.

first fruits: τοῦ φυράματος (cf. Num.15:20), Rom.11:16. Metaphorical, ἀ. τοῦ πνεύματος: Rom.8:23; of Christians: Rom.16:5, 1, 1Co.16:15, 2Th.2:13 (WH, mg., R, mg., txt., ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς; see Lft., Notes, 119 f.), Jas.1:18, Rev.14:4; of Christ: 1Co.15:20 15:23 (Cremer, 117; MM, VGT, see word).

ἅπας, -ασα, -αν (strengthened form of πᾶς, see: ἅ-), all, the whole, altogether: bef. subst. with art., as Luk.3:21; or after, as Mrk.16:15]; absol., in masc., as Luk.5:26; in neut., as Act.2:44; ἅ. οὗτοι, Act.2:7 (LT); ἅ. ὑμεῖς, Gal.3:28 (TTr.). Most frequently in Lk, Ac (see MM, VGT, see word)

ἀπατάω, -ῶ (ἀπάτη), [in LXX for פָּתָה, נָשָׁא hi., etc. ;] to deceive: with accusative, Jas.1:26; with accusative of person(s), dative of thing(s), Eph.5:6; pass., 1Ti.2:14 (on its infrequency in late writers, see MM, VGT, see word; cf. ἐξαπατάω).

ἀπάτη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Ecc.9:6 א (no Heb. equiv.), Jdth.9:3, 10 9:13 16:8, 4Ma.18:8 ;] deceit, deceitfulness: Col.2:8; τοῦ πλούτου, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19 (MM, VGT, see word); τῆς ἀδικίας, 2Th.2:10; τῆς ἁμαρτίας, Heb.3:13; αἱ ἐπιθυμίαι τῆς ἀ., Eph.4:22. pl., ἀπά ται (see M, Th., i.e.; NTD, 75; MM, l.with): 2Pe.2:13 (WH, mg., R., txt., ἐν ἀγάπαις).
fatherless.
without father (MM, VGT, see word), i.e., with no recorded genealogy: Heb.7:3.
ἀπ-αύγασμα, -τος, τό (αὐγή, brightness, whence ἀπαυγάζω, to radiate or reflect) [in LXX: Wis.7:26 ;] of light beaming from a luminous body, radiance, effulgence: Heb.1:3.
ἀπ-εῖδον (WH, ἀφ-, ν. Bl., § 4, 3), 2 aor. without present in use (cf. εἶδον), serving as aor. to ἀφοράω, q.v

ἀπείθεια (WH, -θία, exc. Heb.11:1-40. with), -ας, ἡ (ἀπειθής), [in LXX 4Ma.8:9 4Mac 8:18 4Mac 12:4 ;] disobedience (MM, VGT, see word): Rom.11:30, 32, Heb.4:6, 11; υἱοὶ τῆς ἀ. (genitive of definition, see M, Pr., 73 f.), Eph.2:2 5:6, Col.3:6 (T, WH R, mg., omit).

ἀπειθέω, -ῶ (ἀπειθής), [in LXX for מָרָה, סָרַר, etc. ;] as in cl. (MM, VGT, see word); to disobey, be disobedient: absol., Act.14:2 19:9, Rom.10:21 11:31 15:31, Heb.3:18 11:31, 1Pe.3:20; with dative, Jhn.3:36, Rom.2:8 11:30, 1Pe.2:8 3:1 4:17 (Cremer, 475).
ἀπειθής, -ές (πείθομαι), [in LXX for מַרְאָה, מְרִיא, סָרַר ;] disobedient: absol., Luk.1:17, Tit.1:16 3:3; with dative, Act.26:19, Rom.1:30, 2Ti.3:2.
ἀπειλέω, -ῶ (ἀπειλή) [in LXX: Nam.1:4 (גָּעַר), Isa.66:14 (זָעַם), Sir.19:17, al. ;] to threaten: 1Pe.2:23; mid., Act.4:17 (see MM, VGT, see word, and cf. προσαπειλέω).
ἀπειλή, -ῆ, ἡ [in LXX for גְּעָרָה, etc. ;] threatening, threat: Act.4:29 9:1, Eph.6:9.
ἄπ-ειμι (εἰμί, sum) [in LXX for כָּחַד ni., נָדַח ni. ;] to be absent: 1Co.5:3, 2Co.10:1 10:11 13:2 13:10 Php.1:27, Col.2:5.
ἄπ-ειμι (εἶμι, ibo) to depart: Act.17:10.
to tell out.
to forbid (3Ki.11:2).
to renounce: 1 aor. mid. (WH, App., 164; MM, VGT, see word), 2Co.4:2.

etymology: reflexive past of a compound of (G0575) (G2036)

full.text: απείραστος, -ον (πειράζω for cl. ἀπείρητος, πειράω), untempted, untried, without experience: Jas.1:13 (see Hort, in l; MM, VGT, see word).
ἄπειρος, -ον (ἀ- neg., πεῖρα, trial), [in LXX: Num.14:23, Zec.11:15 (אֱוִלִי) Jer.2:6 (עֲרָבָה) ;] without experience of: with genitive of thing(s), Heb.5:13 (MM, VGT, see word).

ἀπ-εκ-δέχομαι depon., to await or expect eagerly (Lft., Notes, 149; MM, VGT, see word): absol., 1Pe.3:20; C. accusative of thing(s), Rom.8:19, 23 8:25, 1Co.1:7, Gal.5:5; with accusative of person(s), Php.3:20, Heb.9:28.
ἀπ-εκ-δύω to strip off clothes or arms; mid., to strip off from oneself: Col.3:9; to strip, despoil (mid, for act., ICC, in l; but cf. Lft., Ellic.), Col.2:15.
ἀπ-έκ-δυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπεκδύω) a putting or stripping off: Col.2:11 (MM, VGT, see word).
ἀπ-ελαύνω [in LXX for נָצַל, שָׁלַח ;] to drive away: Act.18:16 (MM, see word).
ἀπ-ελεγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀπελέγχω, to convict, refute), refutation, disrepute: ἐλθεῖν εἰς ἀ., Act.19:27 (not elsewhere; see MM, see word).
ἀπ-ελεύθερος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ a freedman: τ. κυρίου (MM, see word), 1Co.7:22.
Ἀπελλῆς, -οῦ, accusative -ῆν (MM, see word), ὁ Apelles: Rom.16:10.
to give up in despair, despair of (Polyb., Diod., LXX).
to hope to receive from or in return (Field, Notes, 59; Cremer, 712; Soph., Lex., see word): with accusative (M; Pr., 65; MM, see word), Luk.6:35.
over against (MM, see word): Mat.27:61, Mrk.12:41, WH, mg.
before, in the presence of: Mat.27:24 (WH, mg.), Act.3:16, Rom.3:18.
against: Act.17:7.
ἀπέραντος, -ον (περαίνω, to complete, finish) [in LXX: Job.36:26 (חֵקֶר אַיִן) 3Mac.20 ;] endless, interminable: 1Ti.1:4.
ἀπεριστπάστως adv. (περισπάω the adj. occurs in Wis.16:11 Sir.41:1); without distraction: 1Co.7:35.

ἀ-περί-τμητος, -ον (περιτέμνω), [in LXX chiefly for עָרֵל ;] uncir­cumcised; metaphorically (τ.) καρδίαις (cf. Jer.9:26, al.): Act.7:51 (ἄσημος, found in π., appears to have been the word used by Greek-speaking Egyptians: see Deiss., BS, 153; cf. also Cremer, 885; MM, see word)
to go away, depart (also, in late writers, with "perfective" force, to arrive at a destination, the thought being carried on to the goal; M, Pr., 111f., 247; MM, see word);

subs.text: with mention of place or person: εἰς, Mat.14:15; ἐπί, Luk.24:24; πρός, Rev.10:9; ἀπό, Luk.1:38; ἐξω, Act.4:15; ἐκεῖ, Mat.2:22.

full.text: As in LXX, before ὀπίσω, with genitive (Heb. הָלַךְ אַחֲרֵי), to go after, follow: Mrk.1:20, Jhn.12:19; metaphorically, Mrk.1:42, Rev.21:1.

to receive (in full); to be distant; (middle) to abstain, avoid trans. to have in full what is due or is sought, Mt. 6:2, 5, 16; Lk. 6:24; Phil. 4:18; to have altogether, Phlm. 15; hence, it is enough, Mk. 14:41; intransitive to be distant, Lk. 7:6; to be estranged, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6, middle to abstain from, Acts 14:20

etymology: third person singular present indicative active of (G0568)

full.text: refrain, receive/be far, keep away from
trans.,

subs.text: to hold back, keep off;

full.text: Intrans., to be away, distant: absol., Luk.15:20; before ἀπό, Mat.14:24 15:8, Mrk.7:6, Luk.7:6 15:20 24:13. Mid., to abstain: with genitive, Act.15:29, 1Ti.4:3, 1Pe.2:11; before ἀπό, Act.15:20, 1Th.4:3 5:22.
to disbelieve, be faithless: Mrk.16:11, 16, Luk.24:11, 41, Act.28:24, 1Pe.2:7; so prob. also Rom.3:3, 2Ti.2:13 (ICC, CGT, in ll; MM, see word).
= ἀπειθέω (Hdt.; on this sense in Ro, 2Ti, ll. with, see Vaughan on Ro, l.with; Lft., Notes, 265; Thayer, see word).

ἀπιστία, -ας, ἡ (ἄπιστος), [in LXX: Wis.14:25, 4Ma.12:4 ;] want of faith, unbelief: Mat.13:58, Mrk.6:6 9:24 16:14, Rom.3:3 (but see: ἀπιστέω) Rom.4:20 11:20, 23, 1Ti.1:13, Heb.3:12, 19 (DCG, ii, 775a; Cremer, 492).

ἄ-πιστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., πιστός) [in LXX: Pro.17:6 28:25, Isa.17:10 ;] (a) of things, incredible: Act.26:8; (b) of persons, without faith or trust, unbelieving: Mat.17:17, Mrk.9:19, Luk.9:41 12:46, Jhn.20:27, Tit.1:15, Rev.21:8; specif., of unbelievers as opposite to Christians: 1Co.6:6 7:12-15 10:27 14:22-24, 2Co.4:4 6:14-15, 1Ti.5:8 (cf. Lft., Notes, 265; Cremer, 491).

ἀπλότης, -ητος, ή (ἁπλοῦς), [in LXX: 4Ki.15:11 (תֹּם), 1Ch.29:17 (לֵבָב יֹשֶׁר, ἁ. τῆς καρδίας, cf. Col.3:22, where see Lft.), Wis.1:1, al. ;] simplicity, sincerity: Rom.12:8, 2Co.11:3, Eph.6:5, Col.3:22; as manifested in generous, unselfish giving, liberality, graciousness: 2Co.8:2 9:11 9:13 (see ICC, Rom.12:8; Hort, Jas.1:5, and see: ἁπλῶς).

ἁπλοῦς, -ῆ, -οῦν (contr. fr. -όος; ἁ.- cop., πλόος), [in LXX: Pro.11:25 ;] simple, single: in a moral sense (DCG, ii, 628 f.), ἀφθαλμός, Mat.6:22, Luk.11:34. (In π. of a marriage dowry, see MM, see word). ἁπλόος, see: ἁπλοῦς SYN.: ἄδολος, ἄκακος, ἀκέραιος (Tr., Syn., § lvi; Cremer, 107, 639)

ἁπλῶς adv. (ἁπλοῦς), [in LXX: Pro.10:9 (תֹּם), Wis.16:27, 2Ma.6:6 ;] simply, sincerely, graciously: Jas.1:5. ("Later writers comprehend under the one word the whole magnanimous and honour­able type of character in which ... singleness of mind is the central feature "-Hort, Ja., l.with)

Of separation and cessation; (1) of motion from a place: Mat.5:29, 30 7:23, Luk.5:2 22:41, al.; (2) in partitive sense (M, Pr., 72, 102, 245; MM, see word; Bl., §40, 2), Mat.9:16 27:21, Jhn.21:10, Act.5:2, al.; also after verbs of eating, etc.; (3) of alienation (cl. genitive of separation), after such verbs as λούω (Deiss., BS, 227), λύω, σώζω, παύω, etc.; ἀνάθεμα ἀ., Rom.9:3; ἀποθνήσκειν ἀ., Col.2:20; σαλευθῆναι, 2Th.2:2, καθαρός, -ίζειν, ἀ. (Deiss., BS, 196, 216), Act.20:26, 2Co.7:1, Heb.9:14; (4) of position, Mat.23:34 24:31, al.; after μακράν, Mat.8:30; transposed before measures of distance, Jhn.10:18 21:8, Rev.14:20 (Abbott, JG, 227); (5) of time, ἀπὸ τ. ὥρας, ἡμέρας, etc., Mat.9:22, Jhn.19:27, Act.20:18, Php.1:5, al.; ἀπ᾽ αἰῶνος, Luk.1:70, al.; ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς, etc., Mat.19:4, Rom.1:20; ἀπὸ βρέφους, 2Ti.3:15; ἀφ᾽ ἧς, since, Luk.7:45, al.; ἀπὸ τ. νῦν, Luk.1:48, al.; ἀπὸ τότε, Mat.4:17, al.; ἀπὸ πέρυσι, a year ago, 2Co.8:10 9:2; ἀπὸ πρωΐ, Act.28:23; (6) of order or rank, ἀπὸ διετοῦς, Mat.2:16; ἀπὸ Ἀβραάμ, Mat.1:17; ἐβδομος ἀπὸ Ἀδάμ, Ju 14; ἀπὸ μικροῦ ἕως μεγάλου, Act.8:10, Heb.8:11; ἄρχεσθαι ἀπό, Mat.20:8, Jhn.8:9, Act.8:35, al.

Of origin; (1) of birth, extraction, and hence, in late writers, (a) of local extraction (cl. ἐξ; Abbott, JG, 227ff.), Mat.21:11, Mrk.15:43, Jhn.1:45, Act.10:38, al.; οἱ ἀπὸ Ἰταλίας (WM, §66, 6; M, Pr., 237; Westc, Rendall, in l.), Heb.13:24; (b) of membership in a community or society (BL, §40, 2), Act.12:1, al.; (with) of material (= cl. genitive; Bl. l.with; M, Pr., 102), Mat.3:4 27:21; (d) after verbs of asking, seeking, etc., Luk.11:50, 51 1Th.2:6 (Milligan, in l.); (2) of the cause, instrument, means or occasion (frequently = ὑπό, παρά, and after verbs of learning, hearing, knowing, etc.; Bl., §40, 3), Mat.7:16 11:29, Luk.22:45, Act.2:22 4:36 9:13 12:14, 1Co.11:23, Gal.3:2, al.; ἀπὸ τ. ὄχλου, Luk.19:3 (cf. Jhn.21:6, Act.22:11); ἀπὸ τ. φόβου, Mat.14:26, al. (cf. Mat.10:26 13:44).

Noteworthy Hellenistic phrases: φοβεῖσθαι ἀπό (M, Pr., 102, 107); προσέχειν ἀπό (M, Pr., 11. with; Milligan, NTD, 50); ἀπὸ νότου (Heb. מִגֶּנֶב), Rev.21:13; ἀπὸ προσώπου (מִפְּנֵי), 2Th.1:9 (Bl., §40, 9); ἀπὸ τ. καρδιῶν (בְּלֵב), Mat.18:35; ἀπὸ ὁ ὤν (WM, §10, 2; M, Pr., 9), Rev.1:4.
In composition, ἀπό denotes separation, departure, origin, etc. (ἀπολύω, ἀπέρχομαι, ἀπογράφω); it also has a perfective force (M, Pr., 112, 247), as in ἀφικνεῖσθαι, which see
ἀπο-βαίνω [in LXX for הָיָה, etc. ;] to step off, disembark: Luk.5:2, Jhn.21:9; metaphorically, of events, to issue, turn out (Field, Notes, 74): Luk.21:13, Php.1:19 (MM, see word).
ἀπο-βάλλω [in LXX: Isa.1:30 (נָבֵל) and elsew. without Heb. equiv. ;] to throw off: Mrk.10:50; metaphorically, to lose, let go (Field, Notes, 231 f.; MM, see word): Heb.10:35.
ἀπο-βλέπω [in LXX for פָּנָה, etc. ;] to look away from all else at one object; hence, to look steadfastly: Heb.11:26 (cf. ἀφοράω).
ἀπό-βλητος, -ον (ἀποβάλλω), [in Aq.: Lev.7:18, al.; Sm.: Hos.9:3 (טָמֵא) ;] to be thrown away, rejected: 1Ti.4:4 (Hom., Plut.).
a throwing away, rejection: opposite to πρόσλημψις, Rom.11:15.
a losing, loss: Act.27:22.
to be away, removed from.
to depart life, to die (MM, see word): τ. ἀμαρτίαις, i.e. with ref. to sins, 1Pe.2:24 (Cremer, 149, 668).

etymology: past participle of a compound of (G0575) (G1096)

full.text: a written copy.
As law term, a deposition (Demos.).
In late writers, a register, enrolment, census (MM, see word; Deiss., LAE, 160, 268 f.): Luk.2:2, Act.5:37.
to write out, copy.
to enrol; mid., to enrol oneself: Luk.2:1 (M, Pr., 162; but. see ICC, in l), Luk.2:3, 5; pass., Heb.12:23 (see reff, see word ἀπογραφή ).
to bring out, show forth, exhibit (Lft., Notes, 200; ICC, in l): 1Co.4:9.
to declare, show: Act.2:22
to prove: Act.25:7.
As frequently in late Gk., to proclaim to an office: before ὅτι, 2Th.2:4 (Milligan, in l; MM, see word; Lft., Notes. 113.)
a showing off.
As used by Gk. philosophers, demonstration, certain proof: 1Co.2:4 (see ICC, in l; MM, see word; Lft., Notes, 173).
with accusative of thing

subs.text: , to tithe, pay a tenth of: Mat.23:23, Luk.11:42.

full.text: C. accusative of person

subs.text: , to exact tithes from: Heb.7:5.

full.text: to decimate (Socr., HE, 573 A; see Kennedy, Sources, 117).
ἀπό-δεκτος, -ον (άποδέχομαι), acceptable: 1Ti.2:3 5:4.

ἀπο-δέχομαι [in LXX: Tob.7:17, Jdth.13:13; Jdt.13.1, 4Mac.10:1-21 ;] to accept gladly, welcome, receive: Luk.8:40 9:11, Act.18:27 21:17 28:30; metaphorically, with accusative of thing(s), Act.2:41 24:3 (MM, see word; Cremer, 688).
ἀπο-δημέω, -ῶ (ἀπόδημος), [in LXX: Eze.19:3 A ;] to be or go abroad (M, Pr., 130, 2): Mat.21:33 25:14-15, Mrk.12:1, Luk.15:13 20:9.
ἀπό-δημος, -ον gone abroad (RV, sojourning in another country): Mrk.13:34.

ἀπο-δίδωμι [in LXX for מָכַר, שׁוּב hi., נָתַן, שָׁלֵם pi., etc. ;] to give up or back, restore, return: Mat.27:58, Luk.4:20 9:42 19:8; esp. of wages, debts, oaths, etc. (MM, see word), to render what is due, to pay (Deiss., LAE, 334 f.): absol., Mat.18:25, 28, Luk.7:42. with accusative, Mat.5:26 18:29-30, 34 20:8 21:41 22:21, Mrk.12:17, Luk.10:35 12:59 20:25, Rom.13:7, Heb.12:11, Rev.22:2; ὅρκους, Mat.5:33 (cf. Num.30:3, Deu.23:21 al.); of conjugal duty, τ. ὀφειλήν, 1Co.7:3; ἀμοιβάς, 1Ti.5:4; μ...
ἀπο-δι-ορίζω (διορίζω, ἅρος, a limit), to mark off, hence metaphorically to make separations: Ju 19 (Cremer, 806).
ἀπο-δοκιμάζω [in LXX: Psa.118:22, al. (מָאַס) ;] to reject: Mat.21:42 (LXX), Mrk.8:31 12:10" (LXX) Luk.9:22 17:25 20:17" (LXX), Heb.12:17, 1Pe.2:4 2:7" (LXX) (Cremer, 701; MM, see word).
ἀπο-δοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀποδέχομαι), acceptance, approbation (Field, Notes, 203): 1Ti.1:15 4:9 (Cremer, 686; MM, see word).
ἀπό-θεσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποτίθημι), a putting away: 1Pe.3:21, 2Pe.1:14.
ἀπο-θήκη, -ης, ἡ (ἀποτίθημι), [in LXX for אוֹצָר, טֶנֶא, etc. ;] a storehouse, granary: Mat.3:12 6:26 13:30, Luk.3:17 12:18, 24.
ἀπο-θησαυρίζω [in LXX: Sir.3:4 ;] to treasure up, store away: 1Ti.6:19.
ἀπο-θλίβω [in LXX for לָחַץ, Num.22:25 ;] to press hard: Luk.8:45.

ἀπο-θνῄσκω, [in LXX chiefly for מוּת ;] to die: of natural death, Mrk.5:35, al.; of violent death (pass. of ἀποκτείνω), esp. of Christ, Mat.26:35, Jhn.12:33, Heb.10:28, al.; of spiritual death, Jhn.6:50, Rom.8:13, al.; with dative ref., Rom.6:2, 10 14:7, 8, Gal.2:19; accusative, ὅ, Rom.6:10; before ἐν, Mat.8:32, Jhn.8:21, 24 1Co.15:22, Heb.11:37, Rev.14:13; before ὑπέρ, περί, Jhn.11:50, 51 18:14, Rom.5:6-8 14:15, 1Co.15:3, 2Co.5:15, 1Th.5:10, 1Pe.3:18; ἀπό, Col.2:20; ἐκ, Rev.8:11; figuratively, ...
to restore, i.e. to a former condition: of health, Mat.12:13, Mrk.3:5 8:25, Luk.6:10; of social or political affairs, Mat.17:11, Mrk.9:12, Act.1:6.
to give back, bring back: Heb.13:19 (so in π., MM, see word; cf. also Cremer, 312).
in general sense (cl.), to reveal, uncover, disclose,

subs.text: of persons: pass., Christ, Luk.17:30; Antichrist, 2Th.2:3 2:6 2:8

full.text: In LXX and NT, in special sense of divine revelation: Mat.11:25 (on the tense, see M, Pr., 136), ib. Mat.11:27 16:17, Luk.10:21-22, Jhn.12:38, Rom.1:17-18 8:18, 1Co.2:10 14:30, Gal.1:16 3:23, Eph.3:5, Php.3:15, 1Pe.1:5-12 5:1 (Westc., Eph., 178 f.; M, Th., 149 f.). SYN.: φανερόω (see Thayer, 62; Cremer, 342).

ἀπο-κάλυψις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκαλύπτω), [in LXX: 1Ki.20:30 (עֶרְוָה), Sir.11:27 22:22 42:1 ;] an uncovering, laying bare (Plut.). Metaphorical, a reveal­ing, revelation: a disclosure of divine truth, or a manifestion from God: Luk.2:32 Rom.2:5 8:19 16:25, 1Co.1:7 14:6 14:26, 2Co.12:1 12:7, Gal.1:12 2:2, Eph.1:17 3:3 2Th.1:7 1Pe.1:7 1:13 4:13, Rev.1:1. SYN.: ἐπιφάνεια, παρουσία, φανέρωσις (see Tr., Syn., xciv; Lft., Notes, 102, 178; Westc, Eph., 178 f.; M, Th., 145 ff.; Cremer, 343)

ἀπο-καραδοκία, -ας, ἡ (ἀποκαραδοκέω, used by Aq. in Psa.37:7 for חוּל; ἀπό, κάρα the head, δοκέω, in Ion., to watch), to watch with outstretched head, watch anxiously (Polyb., π.; see Deiss., LAE, 374.5, 377 f.), Rom.8:19, Php.1:20 (Lft., in l; Cremer, 177).
ἀπο-κατ-αλλάσσω (cf. καταλλάσσω: ἀπό here signifies completely, see Lft., Col., l.with; Ellic., Eph., l.with; but also Mey., Eph., l.with), to reconcile completely: Eph.2:16, Col.1:20-21.
ἀπο-κατά-στασις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκαθίστημι), restoration: Act.3:21 (in τ. of repairs and restorations of temples, see MM, see word).

ἀπό-κειμαι [in LXX: Gen.49:10 (שִׁילֹה), Job.38:23 (חָשַׂךְ), 2Ma.12:45, 4Ma.8:11 ;] to be laid up, in store, laid away: Luk.19:20. Metaphorical, C. dative of person(s), to be reserved (Dem., Plat.; and see MM, see word): Col.1:5, 2Ti.4:8, Heb.9:27.
ἀπο-κεφαλίζω (ἀπό, κεφαλή), [in LXX: Psa.151:7 ;] to behead: Mat.14:10, Mrk.6:16, 28 Luk.9:9.
ἀπο-κλείω [in LXX chiefly for סָגַר ;] to shut fast: Luk.13:25.

ἀπο-κόπτω [in LXX for קָצַץ, כָּרַת, etc. ;] to cut off: Mrk.9:43, 45, Jhn.18:10, 26 Act.27:32. Mid., to mutilate oneself, have oneself mutilated: Gal.5:12 (cf. Deu.23:1 LXX; and see Cremer, 751; MM, see word).
prop., a judicial sentence: 2Co.1:9, R, mg.

an answer (see Thayer, see word): 2Co.1:9, R, txt. (In FlJ, Ant., xiv, 10, 6, of a rescript of the Senate; in Inscr. of an official decision, Deiss., BS, 257; a reply to a deputation, MM, see word: cf. also Cremer, 3'75).
to separate, distinguish.

to choose. Mid., to answer: Mat.27:12, Mrk.14:61, Luk.3:16 23:9, Jhn.5:17, 19 Act.3:12. In late Gk. the pass, also is used in this sense, and pass. forms are the more frequently in NT (M, Pr., 39, 161; MM, see word);

subs.text: in general sense: absol., Mrk.12:34; with accusative of thing
, Mat.22:46; with dative of person
, Mat.12:38; before πρός, Act.25:16;
Hebraistically

full.text: ἀπό-κρισις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκρίνομαι), [in LXX for דָּבָר, etc. ;] an answering, an answer: Luk.2:47 20:26, Jhn.1:22 19:9.
ἀπο-κρύπτω [in LXX chiefly for סָתַר ;] to hide, conceal, keep secret: with accusative, Luk.10:21; pass., 1Co.2:7, Eph.3:9, Col.1:26 (MM, see word).
ἀπόκρυφος, -ον (ἀποκρύπτω), [in LXX Chiefly for סֵתֶר ;] hidden: Mrk.4:22, Luk.8:17, Col.2:3 (see Lft., in l; MM, see word).

ἀπο-κτείνω (also in late forms -κτέννω, Mat.10:28, a1., LTTr., -κτεννύω, Mrk.12:5, WH), [in LXX for הָרַג, מוּת ;] to kill: Mat.14:5, al.; before instr. ἐν (which see), Eph.2:16, Rev.2:23, al. Metaphorical: Rom.7:11; τ. ἔχθραν, Eph.2:16; τὸ γράμμα ἀποκτείνει 2Co.3:6 (on the perfective force of this verb, see M, Pr., 114)

ἀπο-κυέω (Rec. -κύω), -ῶ (ἀπό, κυέω or κύω, to be pregnant), [in LXX: 4Ma.15:17 ;] prop., "the medical word for birth as the close of pregnancy" (Hort, Ja., 26 f.). In κοιν, "an ordinary syn. of τίκτω, but definitely 'perfectivised' (M, Pr., 111 ff.; MM, see word) by the ἀπό, and so implying safe delivery," to bring forth, give birth to: Jas.1:15, 18.
ἀπο-κυλίω (see: κυλίω), [in LXX: Gen.29:3, 8 29:10 (גָּלַל) Jdth.13:9 ;] to roll away: Mat.28:2, Mrk.16:3, Luk.24:2.
to receive from another; absol., to receive as one's due: Luk.16:25 18:30 (seel. λάβῃ) Luk.23:41, Rom.1:27, Gal.4:5, Col.3:24, 2Jn.8.
to receive back: Luk.6:34 15:27.
to take apart or aside: Mrk.7:33 (cf. use in π. of the recluses of the Serapeum; MM, see word).
ἀπόλαυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπολαύω, to take of, enjoy a thing) [in LXX: 3Ma.7:16 ;] enjoyment: 1Ti.6:17, Heb.11:26 (for late exx., see MM, see word).
to leave, leave behind (in π. a term. techn. in wills; see MM, see word): 2Ti.4:13 4:20, Tit.1:5; pass., to be reserved, remain: Heb.4:6, 9 10:26.
to desert, abandon: Ju 6.
ἀπο-λείχω (for ἐπιλ-, which see), to lick up: Luk.16:21, Rec.
Act., 1 to destroy utterly, destroy, kill: Mk 124, 922, al.; τ. ψνχήν, Mat.10:28, al.; 2 to lose utterly: Mat.10:42, al.; metaphorically, of failing to save, Jhn.6:39 18:9.

Mid., 1 to perish; (a) of things: Mat.5:29, Jhn.6:12, Heb.1:11 (LXX, al.; (b) of persons: Mat.8:26, al. Metaphorical, of loss of eternal life, Jhn.3:15-16 10:28 17:12, Rom.2:12, 1Co.8:11 15:18, 2Pe.3:9. In οἱ ἀπολλὺμενοι, the perishing, contrasted in 1Co.1:18, al., with οἱ σωζόμενοι, the "perfective" force of the verb, WH. " implies the completion of the process of destruction," is illustrated (see M, Pr., 114 f.; M, Th., ii, 2:10); 2 to be lost: Luk.15:4 21:18. Metaphorical, on the basis of the relation between shepherd and flock, of spiritual destitution and alienation from God: Mat.10:6 15:24, Luk.19:10 (MM, see word; DCG, i, 191 f., ii, 76, 554; Cremer, 451)
Ἀπολλύων, -οντος, ὁ (present ptcp. of ἀπολλύω), Apollyon, i.e. the Destroyer: Rev.9:11 (cf. Ἀβάδδων). (Cremer, 453; DB, i, 125, 172.)
Ἀπολλωνία, -ας, ἡ Apollonia, a city of Macedonia: Act.17:1.
Ἀπολλώς, -ώ, ὁ (perh. contr. from Ἀπολλώνιος, Act.18:24 D) Apollos: Act.18:24 19:1, 1Co.1:12 3:4-6 3:22 4:6 16:12, Tit.3:13.
to defend: with accusative, Rom.2:15.

to defend one's self: absol., Luk.21:14, Act.26:1; before ὅτι, Act.25:8; τί, to adduce something in one's defence, Luk.12:11, Act.26:24 (ταῦτα) Act.24:10 (τὰ περὶ ἐμαυτοῦ); περί, with genitive of thing

subs.text: , and ἐπί, with genitive of person
, Act.26:2; with dative of person

etymology: middle voice from a compound of (G0575) (G3056)

full.text: ἀπολογία, -ας, ἡ (ἀπολογέομαι), [in LXX: Wis.6:10 ;] a speech in defence: Act.25:16, 2Co.7:11, Php.1:7, 16, 2Ti.4:16; with dative of person(s), 1Co.9:3, 1Pe.3:15; before πρός, Act.22:1.

ἀπο-λούω [in LXX: Job.9:30 (רָחַץ) ;] to wash off, wash away; mid., metaphorically, with accusative of thing(s), to wash off oneself: ἀμαρτίας, Act.22:16; absol., ἀπελούσασθε, ye washed yourselves clean (cf. Cremer, 406), 1Co.6:11.

ἀπο-λύτρωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπολυτρόω, to release on payment of ransom, cf. λύτρον), [in LXX: Da (LXX) Dan.4:30 with ;] release effected by pay­ment of ransom, redemption, deliverance; metaphorically, Heb.11:35; of deliverance thr. Christ from evil and the consequences of sin: Luk.21:28, Rom.3:24 (Vau., in l) 8:23, 1Co.1:30, Eph.1:7, 14 4:30, Col.1:14, Heb.9:15. (On the extent to WH. the word retains the sense of ransom, see ICC, Rom.3:24, Westc., He., 297 ff.; see also ICC, Eph., 11; DCG, ii, 605; C...
to set free, release: Luk.13:12, Jhn.19:10, al.; a debtor, Mat.18:27; metaphorically, of forgive­ness, Luk.6:37.

to let go, dismiss (Field, Notes, 9 f.): Mat.15:23, Luk.2:29 9:12, Act.19:41, al.; of divorce, τ. γυναῖκα: Mat.1:19 5:31-32 19:3, 8 19:10 Mrk.10:2, 4 10:11, Luk.16:18; with ref. to Gk. and Rom. (net Jewish) custom, τ. ἄνδρα: Mrk.10:12. Mid., to depart: Act.28:25 (MM, see word)
απο-μάσσω (μάσσω, Att... -ττω, to touch, handle), [in LXX: Tob.7:17 ;] to wipe off, wire clean: mid., Luk.10:11.
ἀπο-νέμω (νέμω, to distribute), [in LXX: Deu.4:19 (חָלַק), 3Ma.1:7 3Mac 3:16 ;] to assign, apportion: 1Pe.3:7. (In π. of a Prefect who renders to all their dues; see MM, see word)
ἀπο-νίπτω [in LXX for שָׁטַף, רָחַץ, מָחָה ;] to wash off: mid. (reflex.), τ. χεῖρας, Mat.27:24.
ἀπο-πίπτω [in LXX for מָלַל, נָפַל, etc. ;] to fall off: Act.9:18.
ἀπο-πλανάω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָדַח hi., נָטָה hi., שׁוּב pi. ;] to cause to go astray; metaphorically, of leading into error: Mrk.13:22; pass., to be led astray: 1Ti.6:10.
ἀπο-πλέω, -ῶ to sail away: Act.13:4 14:26 20:15 27:1 (Burton, 159).
ἀπο-πλύνω [in LXX chiefly for כָּבַס pi. ;] to wash off: seel. for πλύνω, Luk.5:2, Rec.
ἀπο-πνίγω [in LXX: Nam.2:13 (חָנַק pi.), Tob.3:8 ;] to choke: Mat.13:7, Luk.8:7; pass., of drowning (= καταποντίζομαι), Luk.8:33.

ἀπορέω, -ῶ (ἄπορος, ἀ- neg., πόρος, a way, resource), [in LXX for צָרַר, מוּךְ, etc. ;] to be at a loss, be perplexed: absol., Mrk.6:20 (ἐποίει, R, mg.); mid., be in doubt: absol., 2Co.4:8; with accusative, Act.25:20; before περί, Luk.24:4; ἐν, Gal.4:20; περὶ τίνος λέγει, Jhn.13:22. SYN.: διαπορέω, διακρίνομαι, διστάζω, μετεωρίζομαι (see DCG, i, 491)
ἀπορία, -ας, ἡ (ἀπορέω), [in LXX for בֶּהָלָה, etc. ;] perplexity: Luk.21:25 (MM, see word; on the construction, see Field, Notes, 74 f.).
ἀπο-ρίπτω (Rec. -ρρίπτω, c1.), [in LXX for שָׁלַךְ hi., etc. ;] to throw away, cast forth: reflexively, Act.27:43 (BV, cast themselves overboard; see MM, see word).
ἀπ-ορφανίζω (ἀπό, ὀρφανός), to be bereaved (prop., of a parent, Lft., Notes, 36); metaphorically, 1Th.2:17 (where Field thinks it = χωρισθέντες, Notes, 199).

ἀπο-σκευάζω [in LXX: Lev.14:36 (פָּנָה pi.) ;] to pack and carry off; mid., to pack and remove one's goods: Act.21:15, Rec. (see ἐπισ-). ἐπι-σκευάζω, [in LXX for pm pi., etc. ;] to equip, make ready; mid., to make one's preparations: Act.21:15.
ἀπο-σκίασμα, -τος, τό (ἀποσκιάζω, to cast a shadow; v,s. σκία) a shadow: Jas.1:17 (MM, see word).

ἀπο-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָתַק: ni., hi., etc. ;] to draw off or away, tear away (MM, see word): Act.20:30; μάχαιραν, draw a sword, Mat.26:51; pass., to be parted or withdrawn (Field, Notes, 134; but see Thayer, see word), Luk.22:41, Act.21:1.

άποστασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀφίστημι), [in LXX for מַעַל, etc. ;] defection, apostasy, revolt; in late Gk. (MM, Exp., viii; Lft., Notes, 111; Cremer, 308) for cl. ἀπόστασις, frequently in sense of political revolt, in LXX (e.g. Jos.22:22, 2Ch.29:19, Jer.2:19) and NT always of religious apostasy: Act.21:21, 2Th.2:3.
in cl., only in phrase ἀποστασίου δίκη, an action against a freedman for forsaking his προστάτης (Dem.).
In LXX, βιβλίον ἀποστασίου, a bill of divorce: Mat.19:7, Mrk.10:4; in same sense ἀ. alone (MM, see word), Mat.5:31 (for other late exx., see MM, l.with; Kennedy, Sources, 121).

etymology: neuter of a (presumed) adjective from a derivative of (G0868)

full.text: ἀπο-στεγάζω (στέγη), [in Sm.: Jer.29:11 ;] to unroof: Mrk.2:4.
to send with a commission, or on service;

subs.text: of persons: Christ, Mat.10:40; the apostles, 10:16; servants, Mrk.12:2; angels, 13:27;

full.text: to send away, dismiss: Luk.4:18, Mrk.5:10 8:26 12:3 (cf. ἐξ-, συν-αποστέλλω). SYN.: πέμπω, the general term. ἀ. "suggests official or authoritative sending" (see Thayer, see word πέμπω; Westc., Jo., 298; Epp. Jo., 125; Cremer, 529; MM, see word).

ἀπο-στερέω, -ῶ (στερέω, to rob), [in LXX: Exo.21:10 (גָּרַע), Deu.24:14 (עָשַׁק), Mal.3:5, Sir.4:1 29:6-7 31:21-22 ;] to defraud, deprive of, despoil (in cl. chiefly of the misappropriation of trust funds, Field, Notes, 33; cf. MM, see word): absol., Mrk.10:19, 1Co.6:8; with accusative of person(s), 1Co.7:5. Mid., endure deprivation: 1Co.6:7 (WM, § 38, 3; but see Bl., § 54, 5; M, Pr., 162); pass., ἀπεστερημένοι, bereft of: 1Ti.6:5.
In cl., a sending away (MM, see word), as, an expedition (Hdt.).
In LXX (a) discharge, dis­missal (Ecc.8:8); (b) a gift (3Ki.9:16, 1Ma.2:13).
In NT, the office of an Apostle of Christ, apostleship: Act.1:25, Rom.1:5, 1Co.9:2, Gal.2:8 (Cremer, 530).
a fleet, an expedition (Dem.).
a messenger, one sent on a mission (Hdt., LXX, l.with, and π.; see M, Pr., 37 f.; MM, see word; M, Th., i, 2:7 and reff.): Jhn.13:16, 2Co.8:23 Php.2:25.
In NT, an Apostle of Christ

subs.text: with special ref. to the Twelve: Mat.10:2, Mrk.3:14, Luk.11:49, Eph.3:5, Rev.18:20, al., equality with whom is claimed by St. Paul, Gal.1:1, 11 ff, 1Ti.2:7, a1.;

full.text: In cl., to speak from memory, to dic­tate to a pupil (Plat.).
In late Gk., to catechize, question: Luk.11:53 (MM, see word).

ἀπο-στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּב ;] trans., with accusative, (a) to turn away, remove: Rom.11:26, 2Ti.4:4; metaphorically, to turn away from allegiance, pervert: Luk.23:14; (b) to turn back, return: μάχαιραν, Mat.26:52. Pass., reflex., to turn oneself away from: with accusative, Mat.5:42, 2Ti.1:15, Tit.1:14, Heb.12:25; so act., absol., Act.3:26 (cf. Sir.8:5; Bl., § 53, 1; Cremer, 880).
ἀπο-στυγέω, -ῶ (στυγέω, to hate), to abhor: Rom.12:9.
ἀποσυνάγωγος, -ον (συναγωγή), expelled from the congregation (Field, Notes, 96), excommunicated: Jhn.9:22 12:42 16:2 (Cremer, 64, 607).

ἀπο-τάσσω [in LXX: Ecc.2:20 (יָאַשׁ pi.), I Est.6:27, Jer.20:2, 1-18; 1Ma.4:1-61 ;] to set apart. Mid., in late Gk. (El., § 37, 1; Swete, Mk., 136 f.; MM, e. dative, (a) to take leave of: Mrk.6:46, Luk.9:61, Act.18:18, 21, 2Co.2:13; (b) to forsake: Luk.14:33.
ἀπο-τελέω, -ῶ [in LXX: I Est.5:73, 1Ma.15:39 ;] to bring to an end, complete, accomplish (cf. MM, see word): Luk.13:32, Jas.1:15.

ἀπο-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for נוּחַ hi. ;] to put off or aside; in NT always mid., (a) to put of from oneself as a garment: τ. ἱμάτια, Act.7:58; metaphorically, in ethical sense, to put of, lay aside: Rom.13:12, Eph.4:22, 25, Col.3:8, Heb.12:1, Jas.1:21, 1Pe.2:1; (b) to stow away, put: ἐν τ. φυλακῇ (MM, see word), Mat.14:3.
ἀπο-τινάσσω [in LXX: Jdg.16:20 A (נָעַר ni.), 1Ki.10:2 (נָטַשׁ), La 2:7 (נָאַר pi.) ;] to shake of: Luk.9:5, Act.28:5.
ἀπο-τίνω (or -τίω) [in LXX chiefly for שׂוּם pi. ;] to pay off, repay: Phm 19 (MM, see word).
ἀπο-τολμάω, -ῶ to be quite bold, make a bold venture: Rom.10:20.
ἀποτομία, -ας, ἡ (ἀποτέμνω, to cut of), [in Sm.: Jer.51:35, Nam.3:1 ;] steepness, sharpness; metaphorically, severity (MM, see word): Rom.11:22.
ἀποτόμως adv., [in LXX: Wis.5:22 ;] abruptly, curtly, hence sharply, severely: 2Co.13:10, Tit.1:13 (MM, see word).

etymology: adverb from a derivative of a comparative of (G0575)

full.text: ἀπο-τρέπω [in LXX: Sir.20:29 48:18, 3Ma.1:23, 4Ma.1:33 4Mac 16:12 ;] to turn away; mid., to turn oneself away from: with accusative, 2Ti.3:5.
absence (Æsch., Thuc.): Php.2:12,
deficiency, waste (MM, s.v).
ἀπο-φέρω [in LXX for הָלַךְ, etc. ;] to carry off, bear, or lead away: with accusative, Mrk.15:1, 1Co.16:3, Rev.17:3 21:10. Pass., Luk.16:22, Act.19:12.
ἀπο-φεύγω [in LXX: Sir.22:22 ;] to flee from, escape: with accusative, 2Pe.2:18 2:20; with genitive, 2Pe.1:4.
ἀπο-φθέγγομαι [in LXX for נָבָא, etc. ;] to speak forth, give utterance: Act.2:4, 14 26:25 (MM, s. see).
ἀπο-φορτίζομαι (φορτίζω, to load), to discharge a cargo (Field, Notes, 134), to unlade: with accusative, τ. γόμον, Act.21:3.
ἀπό-χρησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποχράομαι, to use to the full, abuse) abuse, misuse: Col.2:22 (MM, see word).
ἀπο-χωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jer.46:5 (סוּג), 2Ma.4:33, 3Ma.2:33 ;] to go away, depart, withdraw: before ἀπό, Mat.7:23, Luk.9:39, Act.13:13 (absol., Luk.20:20, Tr., mg.).
ἀπο-χωρίζω [in LXX: Eze.43:21 (מִפְקָד) ;] to separate, part asunder: pass., Rev.6:14; reflexively, to separate oneself: Act.15:39.
to breathe out life, expire (Thue.; LXX, l.with).
to leave off breathing, faint (Hom., Od., xxiv, 348): before ἀπό, Luk.21:26.
Ἀππιος, -ου, ὁ Appius; Ἀππίου Φόρον, Appii Forum (Market of Appius), a town in Italy: Act.28:15.
ἀ-πρόσ-ιτος, -ον (πρόσειμι, to go to), unapproachable: φῶς, 1Ti.6:16.
act., not causing to stumble: metaphorically, of not leading others into sin, 1Co.10:32.
Pass., not stumbling, without offence, blameless: Act.24:16, Php.1:10 (for exx., see MM, see word).
ἀπροσωπολήμπτως (Rec. -λήπτως, cl.), adv. (α- neg., προσωπολήμπτης), without respect of persons, impartially: 1Pe.1:17.
ἄ-πταιστος, -ον (πταίω), [in LXX: 3Ma.6:39 ;] without stumbling, sure-footed: metaphorically (MM, see word), Ju 24.

to touch, hold, handle; (act.) to start a fire; "to touch a woman" means "to get married" primarily to bring in contact, fit, fasten; to light, kindle, Mk. 4:21; Lk. 8:16; to touch, Mt. 8:3; to meddle, venture to partake, Col. 2:21; to have intercourse with, to know carnally, 1Cor. 7:1; by implication to harm, 1Jn. 5:18

ἅπτω [in LXX chiefly for נָגַע ;] prop., to fasten to; hence, of fire, to kindle, light: Luk.8:16 11:33 15:8, Act.28:2. Mid., with genitive, to fasten oneself to, cling to, lay hold of (so in π.; MM, see word): Mat.8:3, 15, Jhn.20:17, al.; of carnal intercourse, 1Co.7:1; with reference to levitical and ceremonial prohibitions, 2Co.6:17, Col.2:21; of hostile action, 1Jn.5:18 (cf. ἀν-, καθ-, περι- άπτω) SYN.: θιγγάνω, ψηλαφάω. ἅ. is the stronger, θ., to touch, the lighter term. ψ. is to feel, as i...
Ἀπφία, -ας, ἡ Apphia: Phm 2 (MM, see word).
ἀπ-ωθέω, -ῶ [in LXX for זָנַח, מָאַס, etc. ;] to thrust away. Mid., to thrust away from oneself, refuse, reject: with accusative of person(s) (MM, see word), Act.7:27, 39 13:46, Rom.11:1-2, 1Ti.1:19.

ἀπώλεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀπόλλυμι), [in LXX (Cremer, 797) for אָבַד, אֵיד, etc. ;] destruction, waste, loss, perishing (in π., of money, see MM, see word): Mat.26:8, Mrk.14:4, Act.8:20, Rom.9:22, 1Ti.6:9, 2Pe.2:1; in special sense of the loss of eternal life, perdition, the antithesis of σωτηρία: Mat.7:13, Jhn.17:12, Php.1:28 3:19, 2Th.2:3, Heb.10:39, 2Pe.2:3 3:7 3:16, Rev.17:8, 11 (DB, iii, 744).
a prayer (MM, see word).
(as in Homer) a curse, malediction: Rom.3:14 (LXX),

ἄρα, illative particle, expressing a more subjective or informal inference than οὖν, then: prop. (as in cl.), the second word in the sentence, Rom.7:21 8:1, Gal.3:7; ἐπεὶ ἄρα, 1Co.7:14 (with another word between) 5:10; as the first word, Luk.11:48, Act.11:18, Rom.10:17, 1Co.15:18, 2Co.5:15 7:12, Heb.4:9; so prop. in apodosis after protasis with εἰ, Mat.12:28, Luk.11:20, Gal.2:21 3:29 5:11, Heb.12:8 (κενὸν ἄρα), 1Co.15:14; often in interrogations, direct and indirect, τίς (τί) ἄρα, Mat.18:1 19:25...
(num igitur) expecting a neg. reply, Luk.18:8; ἆρά γε, Act.8:30.
(ergone) in apodosis, expecting an affirm. reply, Gal.2:17 (Bl., l.with; Lft., Ga., in l.; MM, see word).
Arabia , (Herdotus Historicus), poetry Ἀρραβία, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus)
Ἀράμ indec. (Heb. רָם), Aram: Mat.1:3-4, Luk.3:33 (R, txt., WH, Ἀρνεί).
Ἄραψ, -αβος, ὁ an Arabian: Act.2:11.
ἀργέω, -ῶ (ἀργός), [in LXX: Est.4:24, Ecc.12:3 (בָּטֵל), I Est.2:30, Sir.33:27), 2Ma.5:25 ;] to be idle; τὸ κρίμα . . . ἀ., lingers (cf. MGr. ἀργά, late: MM, see word): 2Pe.2:3.

ἀργός, -όν (in late Gk., incl. NT, -ή, -όν; ἀ- neg., ἔργον) [in LXX: 3Ki.6:7 (מַסָּע), Wis.14:5 15:15, Sir.37:11 38:28 ;] inactive, idle: Mat.20:3, 6, 1Ti.5:13, Tit.1:12, 2Pe.1:8. Metaphorical, of things, inactive, ineffective, worthless: ῥῆμα, Mat.12:36; πίστις, Jas.2:20 (see Cremer, 259 f.). SYN.: βραδύς, slow; νωθρός, sluggish (Tr., Syn., § civ)
ἀργύρεος (see MM, see word), -οῦς, -ᾶ, -οῦν (ἄργυρος), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף ;] of silver: Act.19:24 (WH, br.), 2Ti.2:20, Rev.9:20.

ἀργύριον, -ου, τό (ἄργυρος), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף (Gen.13:2, al.), exc. La 4:1 (כֶּתֶם) ;] prop., a piece of silver (Lft., Notes, 191); in NT, (a) silver: Act.3:6 7:16 19:19 20:33, 1Co.3:12, 1Pe.1:18; (b) money: Mat.25:18, 27, Mrk.14:11, Luk.9:3 19:15, 23 22:5, Act.8:20; (with) a silver coin: p1., Mat.26:15 27:3-6, 9 28:12, 15, where the value is that of a shekel or tetradrachm; ἀργυρίου μυριάδες πέντε (prob. drachmas; MM, see word), Act.19:19.

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of (G0696)

full.text: ἀργυροκόπος, -ου, ὁ (ἄργυρος, κόπτω, to beat) [in LXX: Jdg.17:4, Jer.6:29 (צָרַף) ;] a silversmith: Act.19:24 (Plut.; 77., see MM, see word).
ἄργυρος, -ου, ὁ (ἀργός, shining), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף ;] silver (on its relation to ἀργύριον, see MM, see word): Mat.10:9, Act.17:29, Jas.5:5, Rev.18:12.
Ἄρειος Πάυος (T, Ἄριος Π.), -ου, ὁ Hill of Pres or Mars, Areopagus; also, the Court of Areopagus, the highest tribunal of Athens: Act.17:19, 22.
Ἀρεοπαγίτης (T, -είτης), -ου, ὁ (see supr.), a judge of the Court of Areopagus: Act.17:34.

ἀρεσκία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (ἀρέσκω), [in LXX (-εία): Pro.31:30 (חֵן) ;] pleasing, desire to please: Col.1:19 In Gk. writers (Arist., Polyb.), most frequently in bad sense, but in π., Inscr., and in Philo, as above (see Deiss., BS, 224; MM, see word; Cremer, 642).
to please (Hom., Hdt., al.): with dative of person(s), Mat.14:6, Mrk.6:22, Rom.8:8 15:2, 1Th.2:15 4:1, 1Co.7:32-34 Gal.1:10, 2Ti.2:4; before ἐνώπισν (= Heb. בּעֵנֵי, Bl., § 37, 1; 40, 7), Ac 65.
In late Gk., esp. in Inscr., to render service to (see M, Th., ICC, 1 Co., ll with; Cremer, 640 f.); Rom.15:1, 3, 1Co.10:33, 1Th.2:4.

ἀρεστός, -ή, -όν (ἀρέσκω), [in LXX for יָשָׁר, etc. ;] pleasing, agreeable (Hdt., Xen., and later writers; see Cremer, 641 f; MM, see word): with dative of person(s), Jhn.8:29, Act.12:3; before ἐνώπιον, with genitive (Bl., § 37, 1; 40, 7), 1Jn.3:22; ἀρεστόν ἐστιν, with accusative and inf (El., § 69, 5; 72, 5), Act.6:2.

Ἀρέτας (WH, Ἀρ.; Intr., 313), -α, ὁ, Aretas, an Arabian king: 2Co.11:32 (Deiss., BS, 183 f., thinks the proper spelling Ἀρέθας was changed, as Schürer suggests, "by desire to Hellenise a barbaric name by assimilation to ἀρετή").

ἀρετή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX, in sing.: Hab.3:3, Zec.6:13 (הוֹד), in pl.: Isa.42:8, 12 43:21 63:7 (תְּהִלָּה), Wis.4:1 5:13 8:7 2:1-24; 4Mac.2.2 ;] prop., whatever procures pre-eminent estimation for a person or thing, in Hom. any kind of conspicuous advantage. Later confined by philos. writers to intrinsic eminence-moral goodness, virtue; (a) of God: 2Pe.1:3; (b) of men: Php.4:8, 2Pe.1:5; pl. (Isa, Es, ll. with), excellencies: 1Pe.2:9 (the usage appears to be a survival of an early comprehensive sense...
ἀρήν, ἀρνός, ὁ (nom, not in use, exc. in early times: see MM, see word), [in LXX for מְרִיא, כֶּבֶשׂ, etc. ;] a lamb: Luk.10:3.
ἀριθμέω, -ῶ [in LXX for סָפַר pi., פָּקַד pi., etc. ;] to number (esp. for payment, MM, see word): Mat.10:30, Luk.12:7, Rev.7:9.

ἀριθμός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מִסְפָּר ;] number, a number: Luk.22:3, Jhn.6:10, Act.4:4 5:36 6:7 11:21 16:5, Rom.9:27, Rev.5:11 7:4 9:16 13:17-18 15:2 20:8 (for exx. of mystical use in π., see MM, see word).
Ἁριμαθεία (WH, Ἁρ.), -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Ἁριμαθάιμ, 1Ki.1:1, al. (רָמָה) ;] Arimathæa: Mat.27:57, Mrk.15:43, Luk.23:51, Jhn.19:38.
Ἀρίσταρχος, -ου, ὁ (ἄριστος, ἀρχός; i.e. best-ruling) Aristarchus: Act.19:29 20:4 27:2, Col.4:10, Phm 24
prop., to breakfast: Jhn.21:12, 15
In late Gk., to take a meal, dine: Luk.11:37.
ἀριστερός, -ά, -όν [in LXX for שְׂמֹאול ;] left, on the left: ὅπλα, 2Co.6:7; ἡ ἀ.. (sc. χείρ), Mat.6:3; ἐξ ἀριστερῶν, on the left (MM, see word ): Mrk.10:37, Luk.23:33.

etymology: apparently a comparative of the same as (G0712)

full.text: Ἀριστόβουλος, -ου, ὁ (ἄριστος, βουλή, i.e. best-counselling) Aristobulus, a Christian: Rom.16:10.
In late Gk. = cl. δεῖτνον, dinner: Mat.22:4, Luk.11:38 14:12.

etymology: apparently neuter of a superlative from the same as (G0730)

full.text: ἀρκετός, -ή, -όν (ἀρκέω), [in Aq.: Deu.25:2 ;] sufficient: Mat.6:34 (on the neut., see B1., § 31, 2); before ἵνα (M, Pr., 210), Mat.10:25; with inf., 1Pe.4:3 (for exx., see Deiss., BS, 257; MM, see word).
to keep off; with dative, to assist.
to suffice: with dative of person

subs.text: , Luk.3:14, 1Ti.6:8, Heb.13:5; before ἐπί, III Jn 10.

etymology: apparently a primary verb (but probably akin to (G0142)

full.text: ἅρκος (Rec. (cl.) ἅρκιος), [in LXX for דֹּב ;] -ου, ὁ, ἡ, a bear: Rev.13:2. (This form is also found in late Inscr.; MM, see word)
ἅρμα, -τος, τό (ἀραρίσκω, to join), [in LXX רָכַב ;] a chariot: Act.8:28-29, 38, Rev.9:9.

Ἁρμαγεδών (WH, Ἃρ Μαγεδών; Rec. Ἀρμαγεδδών, prop. = הַר מְגִדּוֹן), cf. LXX, Μαγεδών, 2Ch.35:22, Μαγεδώ, Jdg.1:27; Har-Magedon (AV, Armageddon): Rev.16:16 (see Swete, in l, but also Thayer, see word). Ἅρ, indecl., Rev.16:16, see, Ἁρμαγεδών Μαγεδών, Magedon: Rev.16:16 (WH, Ἅρ M. for Ἁρμαγεδών, which see).
to fit, join.
of marriage, to betroth. Mid.,

subs.text: to join to oneself, marry, take to wife;

full.text: ἁρμός, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Sir.27:2, 4Ma.10:5 ;] a joining, joint: Heb.4:12.
to deny, say no, opposite to είπεῖν: absol., Mat.26:70, Luk.8:45; before ἅτι, 1Jn.2:22; with inf., Heb.11:24

In late Gk. (MM, see word), with accusative of person(s), to deny, refuse to acknowledge, disown: Act.3:14 7:35; Ἰησοῦν, Mat.10:33, 2Ti.2:12, 1Jn.2:22, Ju 4; ἑαυτόν, Luk.9:23, 2Ti.2:13 (prove false to)
C. accusative of thing(s) (in cl. to refuse), to deny, abjure: 1Ti.5:8, Tit.2:12, 2Ti.3:5 (cf. ἀπαρνέομαι)
ἀρνίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ἀρήν; see MM, see word), [in LXX: Psa.114:4, 6 (pl., צֹאן בֵּן), Jer.11:19 (כֶּבֶשׂ), Jer.50:45 (צָעִיר) ;] a little lamb, a lamb: Jhn.21:15, Rev.2:7.
ἀροτριάω, -ῶ (ἄροτρον), [in LXX: Deu.22:10, al. (חָרַשׁ), Isa.7:25 (עֲדַר) ;] later form of ἀρόω, to plough (MM, see word): Luk.17:7, 1Co.9:10.
ἄροτρον, -ου, τό (ἀρόω, to plough), [in LXX chiefly for אֵת ;] a plough: Luk.9:62.
ἁρπαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἁρπάζω), [in LXX (as also ἅρπαγμα) for גָּזֵל, etc. ;] pillage, plundering, robbery: Mat.23:25, Luk.11:39, Heb.10:34.

prop., accusative, to the rule of its formation (Bl., § 27, 2), actively, the act of seizing, robbery (Plut., Deu Puer. Educ., p. 12A), Php.2:6, AV (Waterland, Works, II, 108; Cremer, 649 f.; Meyer, in l; cf. also JThS, July, 1909, April, 1911; MM, see word).

Passively = ἅρπαγμα (Eze.22:25, of a lion's prey, ), a thing seized, hence, a prize: Phi, l.with, RV (Lft., Ellic., ICC, in l; Donaldson, NCrat., 450 ff.; and esp. Gifford, The Incarnation, 59-71, and reff. in DB, ii, 835 B). The lexical data favour the active meaning, but as they also admit the possibility of the alternative, most modern expositors have accepted the latter as seeming to suit the logic of the passage better. The lexical difficulty, however, remains (MM, see word, esp. the last ref.). As to the usage of St. Paul, he seems inclined to adopt the -μα form where it is appropriate (e.g. Rom.11:9, where cf. LXX; 1Co.13:9, 2 Co 19), and there is certainly a presumption in favour of the active meaning here from the fact that he does not use the LXX ἅρπαγμα. Suggestions looking to a fresh exegesis are given in JThS, ll with

ἁρπάζω [in LXX chiefly for גָּזַל, טָרַף ;] to seize, catch up, snatch away, carry off by force: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.12:29 13:19, Jhn.10:12, 28-29; τ. βασιλείαν τ. θεοῦ, Mat.11:12; with accusative of person(s), Jhn.6:15, Act.8:39 23:10, Ju 23; pass., before ἕως, 2Co.12:2; εἰς, 2Co.12:4, 1Th.4:17; πρός, Rev.12:5 (cf. δι-, συν-αρπάζω, and see MM, see word).
ἅρπαξ, -αγος, ὁ, ἡ (ἁρπάζω), [in LXX: Gen.49:27 (טָרַף) ;] rapacious: Mat.7:15, Luk.18:11; as subst., a swindler, an extortioner (MM, see word), 1Co.5:10-11 6:10.

ἀρραβών (T, ἀραβ-: 2Co, ll. with), -ώνος, ὁ, [in LXX: Gen.38:17-20 (עֲרָבוֹן) ;] an earnest, part payment in advance for security, a first instalment: 2Co.1:22 5:5, Eph.1:14. (The word is found in cl. and was prob. brought to Greece by the Phænicians (AR, Eph., l.with). It is found in π. with both spellings (see Milligan, NTD, 73). In MGr. ἀρραβῶνα is an engagement ring; see MM, see word)
ἄραφος, -ον (Rec. ἄρραφο), (ῥάπτω, to sew) without seam: Jhn.19:23.

ἄρσην (ἄρρην, T, in Rom.1:27; Rec. in Rev.12:5, 13) -ενος, ὁ, ἡ, ἄρσεν, τό (old Attic for ἄρρην, see supr.; both forms are found in π.; MM, see word), [in LXX chiefly for זָכָר ;] male: Mat.19:4, Mrk.10:6, Luk.2:23, Rom.1:27, Gal.3:28, Rev.12:5, 13.
unspoken (Hom., al.).
unspeakable (Hat., al.; frequently in Inscr.; MM, see word): 2Co.12:4.
ἄρρωστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ῥώννυμι), [in LXX: 3Ki.14:5Α, Mal.1:8 (חָלָה), Sir.7:35 ;] feeble, sickly: Mat.14:14, Mrk.6:5, 13 16:18, 1Co.11:30.
ἀρσενοκοίτης, -ου, ὁ (ἄρσην, κοιτή), a sodomite: 1Co.6:9, 1Ti.1:10.
Ἀρτεμᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ Artemas: Tit.3:12.
Ἄρτεμις, -ιδος, ἡ Artemis, an Asiatic goddess, to be disting. from the Gk. goddess of the same name: Act.19:24, 27-28
ἀρτέμων, -ωνος (Rec. -ονος), ὁ (ἀρτάω, to fasten to), a fore-sail or top-sail: Act.27:40 (see DB, ext., 366b, 399a; MM, see word).

ἄρτι adv., [in LXX: Dan LXX 9:22 10:11 (עַתָּה), al. ;] adv., of coincidence, denoting strictly present time, as contrasted with past or future, just, just now, this moment: Mat.3:15 9:18 26:53, Jhn.13:7, Gal.4:20, 1Th.3:6 (see Lft., Notes, 44; Milligan, in l), Rev.12:10; opposite to past time, Jhn.9:19, 25 13:33, 1Co.16:7, Gal.1:9-10; to future, Jhn.13:37 16:12, 31, 1Co.13:12, 2Th.2:7, 1Pe.1:6 1:8; ἄχρι τῆς ἅ. ὥρας, 1Co.4:11; ἕως ἄ., Mat.11:12, Jhn.2:10 5:17 16:24, 1Co.4:13 8:7 15:6, 1Jn.2:9; ἀ...
ἀρτι-γέννητος, -ον (ἄρτι, γεννάω), new-born: 1Pe.2:2 (Luc.).
ἄρτιος, -α, -ον fitted, complete: 2Ti.3:17 (MM, see word).

ἄρτος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for לֶחֶם ;] bread, a loaf: Mat.4:3, 4 Mrk.3:20, al.; ἄρτοι τ. προθέσεως, bread of the setting forth, i.e. the shewbread. Metaphorical, ὁ ἄ. τ. θεοῦ, τ. ζωῆς, ref. to Christ, Jhn.6:33, 35; in general, food: Mat.6:11, al.; ἄ. φαγεῖν (Heb. אֲכֹל לֶחֶם), to eat (MM, see word), Lu 14:1, al.
to arrange, make ready (Hom.).
Of food (as in comic writers), to season (MM, see word): Mrk.9:50, Luk.14:34, Col.4:6.
Ἀρφαξάδ, ὁ(Heb. אַרְפַּכְשַׁד), Arphaxad: Luk.3:36.
ἀρχ-άγγελος, -ου, ὁ (ἄρχι-, ἄγγελος), archangel, a chief angel: 1Th.4:16, Ju 9 (Cremer, 24; MM, see word).

ἀρχαῖος, -αία, -αῖον (ἀρχή), [in LXX chiefly for קֶדֶם ;] original, ancient: Mat.5:21, 33, Luk.9:8, 19, Act.15:7, 21 21:16, 2Co.5:17, 2Pe.2:5, Rev.12:9 20:2. SYN.: παλαιός, old, without the reference to beginning and origin contained in ἀ. The distinction is observed in π. (MM, see word). ἀ. is the antithesis to καινός: παλ. to vέος (see Westc., He., 223; Cremer, 116)
Ἀρχέλαος, -ου, ὁ Archelaus, son of Herod the Great, King of Judma, Samaria and Idumæa: Mat.2:22.

beginning, origin; (a) absol., of the beginning of all things: of God as the Eternal, the First Cause, Rev.21:6 (cf. 18); similarly, of Christ, Rev.22:13; of Christ as the uncreated principle, the active cause of creation, Rev.3:14; in his relation to the Church, Col.1:18; ἐν ἀ., Jhn.1:1-2; ἀπ᾽ ἀ. (and ἀπ᾽ ἀ. κτισεως), Mat.19:4, 8 24:21, Mrk.10:6 13:19, Jhn.8:44, 2Th.2:13, 2Pe.3:4, 1Jn.1:1 Jn 2:13-14 Jn 2:24; κατ ἀρχἀς, Heb.1:10; (b) relatively: Heb.7:3; ἀ. ὠδίνων, Mat.24:8, Mrk.13:9; τ. σημείων, Jhn.2:11; τ. ὑποστάσεως, Heb.3:14; τ. λογίων, Heb.5:12; ὁ τ. ἀρχῆς τ. Χριστοῦ λόγος, the account of the beginning, the elementary view of Christ, Heb.6:1; ἀρχὴν λαμβάνειν, to begin, Heb.2:3; ἐξ ἀ., Jhn.6:64 16:4; ἀπ᾽ ἀ., Luk.1:2, Jhn.15:27, 1Jn.2:7 Jn 2:24 Jn 3:11, 2Jn.5-6; ἐν ἀ., Act.11:15 26:4, Php.4:15; τὴν ἀρχήν, adverbially, at all (Hdt., al.; v MM, see word): Jhn.8:25.
an extremity, a corner: Act.10:11 11:5.
sovereignty, principality, rule (cf. DB, i, 616 f.): Luk.12:11 20:20, Rom.8:38, 1Co.15:24, Eph.1:21 3:10 6:12, Col.1:16 2:10, 15 Tit.3:1, Ju 6 (Cremer, 113).
founder, author (Lat. auchor; so some­times in π., see MM, see word; Milligan, NTD, 75): Act.3:15 (R, mg.), Heb.2:10 (R, txt.; but see Westc., in l, and Page, Ac., l.with).
prince, leader (so in MGr., see Kennedy, Sources, 153): Act.3:15 (R, txt.) Act.5:31, Heb.2:10 (cf. R, mg.) 122 (Cremer, 117).
ἀρχ-ιερατικός, -ή, -όν (ἀρχιερύς), high-priestly: Act.4:6
high-priest: Mrk.2:26 14:47, al.; of Christ: Heb.2:17 3:1, al.
In pl., chief priests, including ex-high-priests and members of high-priestly families: Mat.2:4, Mrk.8:31, al. (Cremer, 294; DCG, i, 297f.; MM, see word).
ἀρχι-ποίμην, -ενος, ὁ [in Sm.: 4Ki.3:4 ;] found on an Egyptian mummy label (Deiss., LAE, 97 ff.; cf. MM, see word); used by modem Greeks of tribal chiefs; chief shepherd. of Christ, 1Pe.5:4.
Ἄρχιππος, -ου, ὁ Archippus: Col.4:17, Phm 2.

ἀρχισυνάγωγος, -ου, ὁ (συναγωγή), ruler of a synagogue, an administrative officer, supervising the worship (הַכְּנֵםֶת רֹאשׁ): Mrk.5:22, 35-36 5:38 Luk.8:49 13:14, Act.13:15 18:8, 17 (Inscr., see MM, see word; cf. also DB, ext., 101).
ἀρχι-τέκτων, -ονος, ὁ (τέκτων), [in LXX: Isa.3:3 (חָרָשׁ) Sir.38:27. 2Ma.2:29 ;] a master-builder, architect: 1Co.3:10 (in π. of building contractors, MM, see word).
ἀρχι-τελώνης, -ου, ὁ a chief tax-collector, chief publican: Luk.19:2.
ἀρχι-τρίκλινος, -ου, ὁ (τρί-κλινος or -ον, a room with three couches) the superintendent of a banquet, whose duty it was to arrange the tables and food (DB, ii, 253): Jhn.2:8-9.
to begin.

to rule (see DCG, ii, 538b.): with genitive, Mrk.10:42, Rom.15:12. Mid., to begin: before ἀπό, Mat.16:21 20:8, Luk.14:18 23:5 24:27, 47, Jhn.8:9, Act.1:22 8:35 10:37, 1Pe.4:17; with inf., an Aramaic pleonasm, Mrk.1:45 2:23 5:17, Luk.3:8, al. (see M, Pro.14:1-35 f.; Dalman, Words, 27; MM, see word)
to begin.

to rule (see DCG, ii, 538b.): with genitive, Mrk.10:42, Rom.15:12. Mid., to begin: before ἀπό, Mat.16:21 20:8, Luk.14:18 23:5 24:27, 47, Jhn.8:9, Act.1:22 8:35 10:37, 1Pe.4:17; with inf., an Aramaic pleonasm, Mrk.1:45 2:23 5:17, Luk.3:8, al. (see M, Pro.14:1-35 f.; Dalman, Words, 27; MM, see word)

ἄρχων, -οντος, ὁ (present ptcp. of ἄρχω), [in LXX for נָשִׂיא., רֹאשׁ, שַׂר, etc. ;] a ruler, chief: Jesus, Rev.1:5; rulers of nations, Mat.20:25, Act.4:26 7:35; magistrates, Act.23:5, Rom.13:3; judges, Luk.12:58, Act.7:27, 35 16:19; members of the Sanhedrin, Luk.14:1 23:13, 35 24:20, Jhn.3:1 7:26, 48 12:42, Act.3:17 4:5, 8 13:27 14:5; rulers of synagogues, Mat.9:18, 23, Luk.8:41 18:18; οἱ ἄ. τ. αἰῶνος τούτου, 1Co.2:6-8; of the devil: ἄ. τῶν δαιμονίων, Mat.9:34 12:24, Mrk.3:22, Luk.11:15; ὁ ἄ. τ...
ἄρωμα, -τος, τό [in LXX for בֶּשֶׂם ;] spice: Mrk.16:1, Luk.23:56 24:1, Jhn.19:40.
Ἀσάφ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָסַף), [H609] Asaph, an obvious error for Ἀσά, found in the best texts, and adopted by LTTr. and WH, R, mg.: Mat.1:7-8.
ἀ-σάλευτος, -ον (σαλεύω), [in LXX: Exo.13:16, Deu.6:8 11:18 (טוֹפָפָה) ;] unmoved, immovable: Act.27:41; metaphorically, Heb.12:28.
ἄ-σβεστος, -ον (σβέννυμι), [in LXX for נָפַח לֹא, Job.20:26 א3 A (ἄκαυστον, א2 B) ;] unquenched, unquenchable: πῦρ, Mat.3:12, Mrk.9:43, Luk.3:17.

ἀσέβεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσεβής), [in LXX for פֶּשַׁע, רֶשַׁע, etc. ;] ungodliness, impiety: Rom.1:18 11:26, 2Ti.2:16, Tit.2:12; ἔργα ἀσεβείας, ungodly deeds, Ju 15; ἐπιθυηίαι τ. ἀσεβειῶν, desires for ungodly things or deeds, Ju 18 (DB, iv, 532; Cremer, 523; MM, see word).
ἀσεβέω, -ῶ (ἀσεβής), [in LXX for פָּשַׁע, רָשַׁע ;] to be ungodly, act profanely: 2Pe.2:6; with cogn. accusative (MM, see word), Ju 15.
ἀσεβής, -ές (σέβω, to reverence), [in LXX chiefly for רָשַׁע ;] un­godly, impious: Rom.4:5 5:6, 1Ti.1:9, 1Pe.4:18, 2Pe.2:5-6 3:7, Ju 4 15.
SYN.: ἀσωτία, profligacy, prodigality (see Tr., Syn., § xvi; DB, iii, 46).

ἄσημος, -ον (σῆμα, a mark), [in LXX: Gen.30:42 (עָטַף), Job.42:11, 3Ma.1:3 ;] without mark (in π. of an uncircumcised boy: Deiss., BS, 153; MM, see word). Metaphorical (MM, see word), unknown, obscure: litotes, οὐκ ἄ (Eur., al.), πόλις, Act.21:39.
Ἀσήρ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָשֵׁר), Asher: Luk.2:36, Rev.7:6.

ἀσθένεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσθενής), [in LXX for כָּשַׁל, etc. ;] weakness, frailty, sickness: Luk.13:11-12, Jhn.11:4, Act.28:9, Rom.6:19 8:26, 2Co.11:30 13:4, Gal.4:13 (MM, see word), Heb.5:2 7:28 11:34; ἐν ἀ., Jhn.5:5, 1Co.2:3 15:43, 2Co.12:9; pl., Mat.8:17, Luk.5:15 8:2, 2Co.12:5 12:9-10, 1Ti.5:23, Heb.4:15. SYN.: μαλακία, νόσος (see DB, iii, 323a)

ἀσθενέω, -ῶ ( ἀσθενής) [in LXX chiefly for כָּשַׁל ;] to be weak, feeble: Act.20:35, Rom.8:3, 2Co.11:21 12:10 13:4 13:9; with dative, πίστει (Cremer, 527), Rom.4:19 14:1; same implied, Rom.14:2, 21, 1Co.8:11-12, 2Co.11:29; εἰς, 2Co.13:3. Specif., of bodily debility, to be sick: Mat.25:36, 39, Luk.4:40, Jhn.4:46 5:3, 7 5:13 11:1-3, 6 Act.9:37, Php.2:26-27, 2Ti.4:20, Jas.5:14; οἱ ἀσθενοῦντες, the sick: Mat.10:8 (MM, see word), Mrk.6:56, Luk.9:2, Act.19:12.
ἀσθένημα, -τος, τό (ἀσθενής), an infirmity (MM, see word): Rom.15:1.

ἀσθενής -ές (ἀ- neg., σθένος, strength), [in LXX for עָנִי, etc. ;] without strength, weak, feeble: 1Co.1:27 4:10 12:22, 2Co.10:10, Gal.4:9, 1Th.5:14, Heb.7:18, 1Pe.3:7. Rhetorically, τό ἀ. τ. θεοῦ, God's action of apparent weakness: 1Co.1:25; of bodily debility, sick, sickly: Mat.25:39 (Rec.) Mat.25:43-44, Luk.9:2 (Rec.) Luk.10:9, Act.4:9 5:15-16 In moral and spiritual sense (MM, see word; Cremer, 526), Mat.26:41, Mrk.14:38, Rom.5:6, 1Co.8:7 8:9-10 9:22 11:30.
Ἀσιαός, -ή, -όν Asian, of Asia, Asiatic; as subst., ὁ (οἱ) Ἀ.: Act.20:4.

Ἀσιάρχης, -ου, ὁ an Asiarch, one of ten officers elected by the various cities in the province of Asia whose duty it was to celebrate at their own charges the public games and festivals: Act.19:31 (Strab., Inscr.; DB, see word).
ἀσιτία, -ας, ἡ (ἄσιτος), fasting, abstinence from food: Act.27:21. SYN.: νηστεία (MM, ut infr.)
ἄσιτος, -ον (ἀ- neg., σῖτος) fasting, without eating (cf. MM, see word): Act.27:33.
to adorn (poët.).
to practise, exercise (Hdt., Xen.).
to endeavour (Xen., al.): with inf., Act.24:16.
ἀσκός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for חֵמֶת, נֹאד, נֶבֶל ;] a leather bottle, wine-skin: Mat.9:17, Mrk.2:22, Luk.5:37-38.
ἀσμένως adv. (ἥδομαι, to be glad), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:12 2Mac 10:33, 3Ma.3:15 3Mac 5:21 ;] gladly: Act.21:17.

etymology: adverb from a derivative of the base of (G2237)

full.text: ἄ-σοθος, -ον [in LXX: Pro.9:8 א2 Α ;] unwise, foolish: Eph.5:15.
