στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for הָפַךְ, also for סָבַב, etc. ;] to turn: with accusative of thing(s) dative of person(s), Mat.5:39; = ἀποσ-, to bring back, Mat.27:3 (cf. Isa.38:8); reflexively (WM, § 38, 1), to turn oneself, Act.7:42; with accusative before εἰς, = μετασ-, to change, Rev.11:6. Pass., reflexive, to turn oneself: before εἰς, Jhn.20:14, Act.7:39 13:46; ptcp., στραφείς, with indic., Mat.7:6 9:22 16:23, Luk.7:9 9:55 14:25 22:61, Jhn.1:38 20:16; id. before πρός, Luk.7:44 10:22 (WH, R, om.),...

στρηνιάω, ῶ (στρῆνος, which see), [in Sm.: Isa.61:6 (אָכַל) ;] a word which first appears in the middle comedy (Rutherford, NPhr., 475 f.), to run riot, wax wanton: Rev.18:7-9 (cf. κατα-στρηνιάω). SYN.: σπαταλάω (which see), τρυφάω

στρῆνος, -ους, τό (cf. Lat. strenuus), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:28 (שַׁאֲנָן) ;] insolent luxury, wantonness: Rev.18:3 (a late word, first found in a Comic poet, B.C. 300, see Kennedy, Sources, 41, cf. στρηνιάω).
στρουθίον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for צִפּוֹר ;] dimin. of στρουθός a sparrow: Mat.10:29, 31, Luk.12:6-7.

στρωννύω or στρώννυμι (seeBl., § 23, 1; Veitch, see word), [in LXX for יַצַע hoph., etc. ;] to spread: ἱμάτια ἐν τ. ὁδῷ (εἰς τ. ὁδόν), Mat.21:8, Mrk.11:8; of making a bed, στρῶσον (sc. τ. κλίνην) σεαυτῷ, Act.9:34; of furnishing a room, ἐστρωμένον, spread with carpets or carpeted couches (cf. Eze.23:41), Mrk.14:15, Luk.22:12.
στυγητός, -όν (στυγέω, to hate), hated, hateful: Tit.3:3.

etymology: from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary

full.text: στυγνάζω (στυγνός, sombre, gloomy, sullen, Isa.5:17, Wis.17:5, Dan LXX 2:12), [in LXX: Eze.27:35 28:19 32:10 (שָׁמֵם) ;] to have a sombre, gloomy appearance: of the human countenance (RV, his countenance fell), Mrk.10:22; of the sky (so στυγνότης, Polyb., iv, 21, 1), Mat.16:3 ([WH], R, mg., om.).

στύλος (T, στῦλος, as in cl.), -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עַמּוּד, also for קֶרֶשׁ, etc. ;] a pillar, regarded especially as a support: σ. πυρός, Rev.10:1; σ. ἐν τ. ναῷ, figuratively, Rev.3:12, Metaphorical, Gal.2:9; τ. ἀληθείας, 1Ti.3:15.
Στωικός (Rec. -ϊκός, T, Στοϊκός), -ή, -όν, Stoic: Act.17:18.

σύ, pron. of 2nd of person(s), thou, you, genitive, σοῦ, dative, σοί, accusative, σέ, pl., ὑμεῖς, -ῶν, -ῖν, -ᾶς (enclitic in oblique cases sing., except after prep. (BL, §48, 3), though πρὸς σέ occurs in Mat.25:39). Nom. for emphasis or contrast: Jhn.1:30, 4:10, 5:33, 39, 44, Act.4:7, Eph.5:32; so also perhaps σὺ εἶπας, Mat.26:64, al. (M, Pr., 86); before voc., Mat.2:6, Luk.1:76, Jhn.17:5, al.; sometimes without emphasis (M, Pr., 85f.), as also in cl., but esp. as rendering of Heb. phrase, e.g. ...

etymology: the personal pronoun of the second person singular;

full.text: kinship.
By meton., kinsfolk, kindred: Luk.1:61, Act.7:3, 14
congenital, natural, innate.
akin to; as subst., a kinsman: Mrk.6:4, Luk.1:58 2:44 14:12 21:16, Jhn.18:26, Act.10:24; of tribal kinship, Rom.9:3 16:7, 11 16:21. SYN.: ἴδιος, οἰκεῖος (which see)
confession.
fellow feeling; hence, concession, allowance: 1Co.7:6 (see ICC, in l).
pardon.
συν-κάθημαι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.101:6 (יָשַׁב) ;] to sit together or with: with dative, Act.26:30; before μετά, with genitive, Mrk.14:54.
to invest, besiege (Diod., 4Ki.6:24, 1Ma.11:61, al.).
to sit around: Luk.22:55, L, txt.
συν-κακοπαθέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to bear evil treatment along with, take one's share of ill-treatment: 2Ti.2:3; with dative commod., τ. εὐαγγελίῳ, 2Ti.2:18.
συν-κακουχέομαι (Rec. συγκ-), -οῦμαι, pass. to endure adversity with: with dative of person(s), Heb.11:25. Not elsewhere.

συν-καλέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, [in LXX for קָרָא ;] to call together: with accusative, Mrk.15:16, Luk.15:6, 9 (seel. -εῖται, see B1., § 55, 1), Act.5:21. Mid., to call together to oneself: Luk.9:1 (and seel. in Luk.15:6, 9 see supr.) Luk.23:13, Act.10:24 28:17.
συν-καλύπτω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX chiefly for כָּסָה pi. ;] to veil or cover completely: with accusative, pass., Luk.12:2.
συν-κάμπτω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.68:10, 23 (מעד hi.), etc. ;] to bend completely, bend together: τ. νῶτον, figuratively, Rom.11:10 (LXX).

συν-κατα-βαίνω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.49:17 (יָרַד), Wis.10:13, Dan LXX TH 3:49 ;] to go down with: Act.25:5. In late writers also metaphorically (Wis, l.with), to condescend, on WH. see Rutherford, NPhr., 485 f.
συν-κατά-θεσις (Rec συγκ-), -εως, ἡ (συνκατατίθημι) concord, agreement: 2Co.6:16 (Polyb., Plut., al.).

συν-κατα-τίθημι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Exo.23:1 (שִׁית יָד), Exo.23:32 (בְּרִית), Da TH Su 1:20 ;] to deposit together. Mid., to deposit one's vote with, hence, to agree with, assent to: with dative, Luk.23:51.
to condemn with or together (Plut., Themist., 21).
to vote one a place among: pass., Act.1:26. Not elsewhere .

συν-κεράννυμι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:43 (עֲרַב ithpa.), 2Ma.15:39 ;] to mix or blend together, compound: with accusative, 1Co.12:24; ptcp. pf. pass., Heb.4:2, T, WH, mg., R, mg.; id., of persons, with dative instr., to be united with, agree with, ib., WH, txt., R, txt. (see Westc., in l).
συν-κινέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to move together. Metaphorical, to excite, stir up: τ. λαόν, Act.6:12 (Arist., Polyb., al.).
συν-κλείω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX chiefly for סגר ;] to shut together, enclose, shut in on all sides: of a catch of fish, Luk.5:6; metaphorically (Psa.31:9, al.), Rom.11:32, Gal.3:22-23.
συν-κληρο-νόμος (Rec. συγκ-), -ου, ὁ a co-inheritor, fellow-heir: Rom.8:17, Eph.3:6; with genitive of thing(s), Heb.11:9, 1Pe.3:7 (for exx., see Deiss., LAE, 88 f.).

συν-κοινωνέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to have fellowship with or in (in cl., with genitive of thing(s), dative of person(s)): with dative of thing(s), Eph.5:11, Rev.18:4; id. with genitive of person(s), Php.4:14.
συν-κοινωνός (Rec. συγκ-), όν, partaking jointly of: with genitive of thing(s), Rom.11:17, 1Co.9:23; id. with genitive of person(s), Php.1:7; before ἐν, Rev.1:9.
to bring together, collect (Hdt., Xen., al.).
to take up a body for burial (Soph., Aj., 1048): Act.8:2 (see Field, Notes, 116).
to compound, combine: 1Co.2:13, R, mg.1 (Lft., Notes, 180 f.).
In Arist. and later writers (Wis.7:29, a1.) = παραβάλλω, to compare: 1Co, l.with, R, txt. (Field, Notes, 168), 2Co.10:12
In LXX, of dreams, to interpret (Gen.40:8, 1Co, l.with, R, mg. 2 (but see reff. supr.).
to bend forwards (Arist., al.).
to be bowed down: Luk.13:11.
συγκυρία, -ας, ἡ (συγκυρέω, to happen), [in Sm.: 1Ki.6:9 (מִקְרֶה) ;] (more frequently in late writers, συγκύρησις, -ημα), chance, coincidence: κατὰ σ. (see MM, xxiii), Luk.10:31 (Hippocr., Eccl.).
to rejoice with: with dative of person

subs.text: , Luk.1:58 15:6, 9, Php.2:17-18, EV; with dative of thing

full.text: to congratulate (Æschin., Polyb., al.): Phi, l.with (Lft., in I.; cf. MM, xxiv).
συν-χέω (Rec. συγ-), [in LXX for בָּלַל, רָגַז, etc. ;] to pour together, commingle, confuse; metaphorically, confound, throw into confusion, stir up trouble: Act.21:27 (cf. συν-χύννω).
συν-χράομαι (Rec. συγ-), -ῶμαι, [in Al.: 1Ki.30:19 ;] to use together with. Metaphorical, with dative of person(s), to associate with: Jhn.4:9 (Polyb., a1.).
σύγχυσις, -εως, ἡ (συγχέω), [in LXX: Gen.11:9 (בָּבֶל), 1Ki.5:6 5:12(11) 1Ki.14:20 (מְהוּמָה) ;] confusion: of a popular uproar, Act.19:29.
συν-ζάω (Rec. συζ-), to live with: opposite to συναποθανεῖν, 2Co.7:3; of life in union with Christ, here and hereafter, Rom.6:8, 2Ti.2:11.
συν-ζεύγνυμι (Rec. συζ-), [in LXX: Eze.1:11, 23 (חָבַר) ;] to yoke to­gether: metaphorically, of union in wedlock, Mat.19:6, Mrk.10:9.
to search or examine together (Plat.).
In NT (and π., see MM, xxiii; also in MGr., see Kennedy, Sources, 155), to discuss, dispute: Mrk.1:27 12:28, Luk.24:15; before quæst. indir., Mrk.9:10; with dative of person

subs.text: , Mrk.8:11 Act.6:9; before πρός, with accusative of person
, Mrk.9:14, 16, Act.9:29; id. before quæst. indir., Luk.22:23.

full.text: συν-ζήτηαις (Rec. συζ-), -εως, ἡ disputation: Act.28:29 (WH, R, txt., om.).
συν-ζητητής (Rec. συζ-), -οῦ, ἁ (συνζητέω), a disputer, disputant: 1Co.1:20.

σύν-ζυγος (Rec. ουζ-), or as WH, mg., Σύνζυγος, -ον (συνζεύγνυμι), [in Aq., Eze.23:21 ;] a yoke fellow. Prob., as proper name, Σ. γνήσιε, genuinely Synzygus, S. properly so-called, Php.4:3 (see ICC, Lft., in l; MM, xxiii).
συν-ζωο-ποιέω (Rec. συζ-), ῶ-, to make alive or quicken together with; metaphorically, of the spiritual life: τ. Χριστῷ, Eph.2:5; σὺν τ. Χ., Col.2:13.

συκάμινος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for שִׁקְמָה (pl.) ;] the mulberry tree (Lat. morus; cf. μόρον, 1Ma.6:34 ), the sycamine: Luk.17:6. (In LXX, σ. appears to represent the συκόμορος (see: συκομορέα), but St. Luke distinguishes between the two; see ICC, in l; DB, iv, 634.)
συκῆ (contr. fr. συκέα), -ῆς, ἡ (σῦκον), [in LXX for תְּאֵן ;] a fig-tree: Mat.21:19-21 24:32, Mrk.11:13, 20-21 13:28, Luk.13:6-7 21:29, Jhn.1:49, 51, Jas.3:12, Rev.6:13.
συκο-μορέα (Rec. -μωραία, L, -μωρέα), -ας, ἡ, more commonly (so Aq., Sm., Psa.78:47, Isa.9:10) συκόμορος (σῦκον + μόρον, the black mulberry), a fig-mulberry, a sycamore: Luk.19:4 (cf. συκάμινος).
σῦκον, -ου, τό [in LXX for תְּאֵן ] a fig: Mat.7:16, Mrk.11:13, Luk.6:44 Jas.3:12.

συκοφαντέω, -ῶ (σῦκον, φαίνω), [in LXX: Gen.43:18 (גָּלַל hithpo.), Lev.19:11 (שָׁקַר pi.), Job.35:9, Psa.119:122, Pro.14:31 2:16 28:3, Ecc.4:1 (עָשַׁק) ;] to act the συκοφάντης (on conjectures as to the origin of the term, see LS, see word), to accuse falsely (Aristoph., Xen., al.): Luk.3:14 (R, mg.) Luk.19:8 (RV, exact wrongfully; but cf. Hatch, Essays, 89 ff., see also Field, Notes, 56 f.; MM, xxiv).
συλαγωγέω, -ῶ (σύλη, booty + ἄγω), to carry off as spoil, lead captive (θυγατέρα, Heliod., Aeth., 10, 35): metaphorically, Col.2:8.
συλάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Ep. Jer.1:18 ;] with accusative of person(s), to strip, plunder, spoil: 2Co.11:8.
to talk or converse with another, (New Testament)
with accusative, to bring together, collect.
to seize, take: with accusative of person

subs.text: , as a prisoner, Mat.26:55, Mrk.14:48, Luk.22:54, Jhn.18:12, Act.1:16 12:3; pass., Act.23:27; mid., in sense of act., Act.26:21; σ. ἄγραν ἰχθύων, Luk.5:9.

full.text: C. dative of person

subs.text: , to take part with, assist, succour; mid., in same sense: Luk.5:7, Php.4:3.

full.text: Of a woman, to conceive: absol., Luk.1:24; before ἐν γαστρί, Luk.1:31; with accusative, Luk.1:36; ib. before ἐν τ. κοιλίᾳ, Luk.2:21; metaphorically, of lust producing sin, Jas.1:15.

συλ-λέγω [in LXX chiefly for לָקַט ;] to bring together, collect, gather up: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:28-30; pass., Mat.13:40; with accusative, before ἀπό, Mat.7:16; id. before ἐκ, Mat.13:41, Luk.6:44; before εἰς, Mat.13:48.
to compute.
to reason: Luk.20:5.
συν-λυπέω (Rec. συλλ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: Psa.69:20, Isa.51:19 (נוּד) ;] to make to grieve with (Arist.). Pass., to be moved to grief by sympathy: before ἐπί, with dative, Mrk.3:5.
to stand with the feet together.
to come together, come to terms.

Of events, to come to pass, happen: with dative of person(s), Mrk.10:32, Act.3:10 20:19, 1Co.10:11, 1Pe.4:12, 2Pe.2:22; absol., τὰ σνμβεβηκότα, Luk.24:14 (cf. 1Ma.4:26); with accusative and nf., Act.21:35.

συν-βάλλω (Rec. συμ-), [in LXX: 2Ch.25:19 (גָּרָה hith.), Isa.46:6 (זוּל), Jer.43:3 (סוּת hi.), Wis.5:8, al. ;] to throw together, hence, (a) of speech (before λόγους, Eur., al.), to discuss, confer (sc. λόγους): with dative of person(s), Luk.11:53, WH, mg., Act.17:18; before πρὸς ἀλλήλους, Act.4:15; (b) to reflect, consider, ponder: ἐν τ. καρδίᾳ, Luk.2:19; (with) to meet with, fall in with: Act.20:14; in hostile sense, εἰς πόλεμον, with dative (εἰς μάχην, Polyb.), Luk.14:31; (d) mid., to contri...
to advise, counsel: with dative of person

subs.text: , Jhn.18:14; id. before inf., Rev.3:18.

full.text: Mid., to take counsel, consult: before ἵνα, Mat.26:4 ("reciprocal middle," see M, Pr., 157); with inf., Act.9:23.
to join or knit together, unite: Eph.4:16, Col.2:2 (but see infr.), Col.2:19.
to compare, consider, conclude (Plat.): Act.16:10.
to deduce, prove, demonstrate (Arist.): Act.9:22.
As in LXX ("translation Greek," = ἐμβιβάζω, metaphorically), to teach, instruct: 1Co.2:16 Act.19:33, R, mg. (also in Vg., Col.2:2, instructi, but see supr.).
to advise, counsel , Lat. consulere alicui, with dative pers. et infinitive, to advise one to do a thing, (Herdotus Historicus), etc.
without the infinitive, ς. τινί τι (Herdotus Historicus); ς. τι to recommend a measure, (Herdotus Historicus), attic:—;Pass., τὰ συμβουλευόμενα the advice given , (Xenophon Historicus)
absolute to advise, give advice , (Sophocles Tragicus); ὁ συμβουλεύων or -εύσας, an adviser , Lat. auctor sententiae, (Aristotle Philosopher)
Mid. to consult with a person, i. e. ask his advice , Lat. consulere aliquem, with dative, (Herdotus Historicus), etc.: absolute to consult, deliberate , (Xenophon Historicus)
counsel: σ. λαμβάνειν, Mat.12:14 22:15 27:7 28:12; διδόναι, Mrk.3:6 (ποιεῖν, Rec.); ποιεῖν, Mrk.15:1 (ἑτοιμάζειν, WH, mg.).
By meton., a council (4Mac, l.with א; συνέδριον, AR): Act.25:12 (see MM, xxiv).

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of (G4825)

full.text: σύμβουλος, -ου, ὁ (σύν, βουλή), [in LXX for יָעַץ and cogn. forms ;] a counsellor, adviser: Rom.11:34 (LXX).
the son of Jacob (Gen.29:33): Rev.7:7.
An ancestor of our Lord: Luk.3:30
An aged worshipper in the Temple: Luk.2:25, 34.
One surnamed Niger: Act.13:1.
The apostle Peter: Act.15:14, 2Pe.1:1 (R, txt., WH, txt., Σίμων).
συν-μαθητής (Rec. συμμ-), -οῦ, ὁ a fellow-disciple: Jhn.11:16 (on the use of συν- in such compounds as this, see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.).
συν-μαρτυρέω (Rec. συμμ-), -ῶ, to bear witness with: Rom.2:15; with dative, Rom.8:16; before ὅτι, Rom.9:1.
συν-μερίζω (Rec. συμμ-), [in LXX: Pro.29:24 א2 (חָלַק) ;] to distri­bute in shares. Mid., to have a share in: 1Co.9:13 (Diod., al.).

συν-μέτοχος (Rec. συμμ-), -ον, partaking together with; as subst., ὁ σ., a joint partaker (with dative of person(s) and genitive of thing(s), FlJ, BJ, i, 24, 6): with genitive of thing(s), Eph.3:6; with genitive of person(s), Eph.5:7 (Arist.).
συν-μιμητής (Rec. συμμ-), -οῦ, ὁ a fellow-imitator: with genitive obj., Php.3:17 (see ICC, in l). Not elsewhere.
absol., similar (Luc.).
con­formed to: with dative, Php.3:21; with genitive, Rom.8:29 (see B1., § 36, 11; 37, 6).
Pass. to be conformed to , τινι (New Testament)

συν-παθέω (Rec. συμπ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: 4Ma.5:25 AR 4Ma.13:23 ;] to have a fellow feeling for or with, sympathize with or in: with dative of thing(s), Heb.4:15; dative of person(s), Heb.10:34 (Arist., Plut., al.).
affected by like feelings, sympathetic: 1Pe.3:8.
exciting sympathy (Jb, l.with; Dion. H.).
to come up or be present together: before ἐπί, Luk.23:48.
to come up to assist (Thuc., al.; 2Ti.4:16, Rec.).
συν-παρα-καλέω (Rec. συμπ-), -ῶ, to call upon or exhort together (Plat.). Pass., to be strengthened or comforted with and among: Rom.1:12.

συν-παρα-λαμβάνω (Rec. συμπ-), [in LXX: Gen.19:17 (סָפָה ni.), Job.1:4 (שָׁלַח ni), 3Ma.1:1 ;] to take along with: as a companion, with accusative, Act.12:25 15:37-38 (on the tenses, see M, Pr., 130), Gal.2:1.
συμ-παρα-μένω Rec, for παρα-μένω (which see), Php.1:25.
συν-πάρειμι (Rec. σνμπ-), [in LXX: Tob.12:12 ΑΒ, Pro.8:27 (?), Wis.9:10 ;] to be present together or with: with dative of person(s), Act.25:24.
to suffer together or with: Rom.8:17, 1Co.12:28.
to sympathize with (Plat., Arist.).
συν-πέμπω (Rec. συμπ-) to send together with: with accusative and dative, 2Co.8:22; id. before μετά, with genitive, 2Co.8:18.
to enclose (Eze.5:3 (צוּר) ).
to embrace: with accusative of person
a drinking party
By meton., of the party itself, the guests (Plut); pl., σ. σ. (colloq.; see M, Pr., 97), in parties or companies: Mrk.6:39.
to fill up completely (Thuc., of manning ships): of a ship filling with water, and by meton., of those on board, Luk.8:23.
to complete, fulfil: of time (Je, l.with, and see MM, xxiv), Luk.9:51, Act.2:1 (on the "durative inf.," see M, Pr., 233).
συν-πνίγω (Rec. συμπ-), to choke: of thronging by a crowd, Luk.8:42. Fig., of seed, τ. λόγον, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:7, 19, Luk.8:14.
συν-πολίτης (Rec. σνμπ-), -ου, ὁ a fellow-citizen: pl., σ. τ. ἁγίων, opposite to ξένοι κ. πάροικοι, Eph.2:19 (Eur.; C.I., 6446; condemned by Atticists; see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.).
to journey together (Plat., al.): with dative of person
to come together: Mrk.10:1 (Plut., al.).
a drinking-party, symposium , (Theognis Elegiacus), etc.
the room in which such parties were given, drinking-room , (Lucian)

etymology: neuter of a derivative of the alternate of (G4844)

full.text: συν-πρεσβύτερος (Rec. συμπ-), -ου, ὁ a fellow-elder: 1Pe.5:1 (Eccl.).
trans. to bring together: with accusative of thing

Intrans., and mostly impers., συμφέρει, it is expedient, profitable or an advantage: 1Co.6:12 10:23, 2Co.8:10; with inf. (M, Pr., 210), Mat.19:10, Jhn.18:14; with dative, before ἵνα (Bl., § 69, 5), Mat.5:29-30 18:6, Jhn.11:50 16:7. Ptcp., ςυμφέρων, profitable: 1Co.12:7; pl., Act.20:20; (οὐ σ. sc. ἐστιν; = οὐ συμφέρει; cf. Thuc., iii, 44, 2), 2Co.12:1; as subst., τὸ σ., profit, Heb.12:10.
σύν φημι (Rec. συμ-), to consent, confess: with dative, before ὅτι, Rom.7:16.

συμ-φυλέτης, -ου, ὁ (σύν, φυλή; cf. σύμφυλος, Aq.: Zec.13:7; and see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.) a fellow-tribesman, fellow-countryman: 1Th.2:14 (see M, Th., in l, and Intr. liii). Not found elsewhere.
congenital, innate (Plat., al.; 3Mac, l.with).
grown along with, united with: τ. ὁμοιώματι τ. θανάτου αὐτσῦ (see Field, Notes, 155 f.), Rom.6:5.
συν-φύω (Rec. συμ-), [in LXX: Wis.13:13 ;] to cause to grow together. Pass., to grow together or with (Plat. al.): Luk.8:7.

συμ-φωνέω, -ῶ, [in LXX: Gen.14:3 (H2266) 2Ki.12:8 (9) (H225 ni.), Isa.7:2 (H5117), 4Ma.14:6 ;] prop., to agree in sound, be in harmony (Plat., Arist.). Metaphorical, (a) to agree with, agree together: Luk.5:36; with dative of thing(s), Act.15:15; before περί, Mat.18:19; pass., with dative of person(s), συνεφωνήθη ὑμῖν, Act.5:9; (b) to agree as to a price (Polyb., Diod.): with dative of person(s), genitive pret., Mat.20:13; before μετά . . . ἐκ, Mat.20:2.
συμ-φώνησις, -εως, ἡ (συμφωνέω), concord, agreement: before πρός, with accusative of person(s), 2Co.6:15.
symphony, music: Luk.15:25
a musical instrument (Polyb., al.; Da, ll. with; v MM, xxiv).

σύμφωνος, -ον (ούν, φωνή), [in LXX: Ecc.7:14 (עֻמָּה), 4Ma.7:7 4Mac 14:7 ;] agreeing in sound. Metaphorical, harmonious, agreeing: ἐκ συμφώνου (for exx. from π., see Deiss., BS, 255), by agreement, 1Co.7:5.
συμ-ψηφίζω [in LXX: Jer.29:20 (Jer.49:20) A ;] to reckon together, count up: Act.19:19.
σύν-ψυχος (Rec. συμ-), -ον (ψυχή), of one mind: Php.2:2.

σύν (old Att. ξύν), prep. with dative, expressing association, fellowship and inclusion. It gradually gave way to μετά, with genitive (cf. LS, see word; Bl., §41, 3), and is therefore comparatively infrequent in NT, being rare in Mat.4, Mrk.6, Jhn.3, and elsewhere (exx. Jas.1:11, 2Pe.1:18) only in Lk (Gosp. and Ac) and Paul. With, together with: of companionship and association, Luk.2:13, Jhn.21:3, Act.10:23, al.; εἶναι σύν τινι, Luk.7:12, Act.4:13, Php.1:23, al.; of partisanship, Act.4:13; οἱ σ...

συν-άγω [in LXX chiefly for אָסַף, also for קָבַץ, etc. (cf. Kennedy, Sources, 128) ;] to gather or bring together: of things, Jhn.6:12-13; before εἰς, Mat.3:12, al.; ἐκεῖ, Luk.12:18; ποῦ, Luk.12:17; συναγαγὼν πάντα (sc. εἰς ἀργύριον; see Field, Notes, 68, MM, xxiv), having sold off all: Luk.15:13; of persons, Jhn.11:52; esp. of assemblies, Mat.2:4, Jhn.11:47, Act.14:27, al. Pass., to be gathered or come together: Mat.22:41, Mrk.2:2, Luk.22:66, al.; before ἐπί Mrk.5:21, Act.4:27; πρός, Mat.13:2,...
of things,

subs.text: a gathering in of harvest;
a collection of money.

full.text: Of persons, (a) a collecting, assembling (Polyb.); (b) an assembly (MM, xxiv; Deiss., LAE, 101 ff.): Rev.2:9 3:9; esp. of a Jewish religious assembly, a synagogue: Luk.12:11, Act.9:2, al.; of a Christian assembly, Jas.2:2. By meton., of the building in which the assembly is held, a synagogue: Mat.10:17, Mrk.1:21, al. (cf. Cremer, see word ἐκκλησία) SYN.: ἐκκλησία (which see)
συν-αγωνίζομαι to strive together with, to help (prop., of sharing in a contest): with dative of person(s) and of thing(s), Rom.15:30.
συν-αθλέω, -ῶ = συναγωνίζομαι: with dative commod., Php.1:27; with dative of person(s), before ἐν, Php.4:3.

συν-αθροίζω [in LXX chiefly for קָבַץ ;] to gather together, assemble: with accusative of person(s), Act.19:25; pass., Act.12:12. ἀθροίζω (ἀθρόος, assembled in crowds, MM, VGT, see word; θρόος, a noise, tumult), [in LXX chiefly for קָבַץ ;] to Gather, assemble: Luk.24:33.

συν-αίρω [in LXX: Exo.23:5 (עָזַב) ;] to take up together: σ. λόγον (of which there are several exx. in π.; see Deiss., LAE, 118 f.; MM, i, xxiv; M, Pr., 160), to settle accounts, Mat.18:23-24; before μετά, C. genitive of person(s), Mat.25:19.
συν-αιχμάλωτος, -ου, ὁ a fellow prisoner (prop., of a captive in war, see Lft., Col., 234): Rom.16:7, Col.4:10, Phm 23.
συν-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.2:4 2Mac 2:6 ;] to follow along with, accompany: with dative of person(s), Mrk.14:51, Luk.23:49; before μετά, with genitive of person(s), Mrk.5:37.
συν-αλίζω [in Al.: Psa.141:4 ;] to assemble with: Act.1:4 (EV, mg., eating with, but on this rendering and on the force of the Pres. ptcp. here, see Field, Notes, 110).
συν-ανα-βαίνω [in LXX for עָלָה ;] to go up with: with dative of person(s), before εἰς, with accusative loc., Mrk.15:41, Act.13:31.
συν-ανά-κειμαι [in LXX: 3Ma.5:39 ;] to recline with or together at table: Mat.14:9, Mrk.6:22, Luk.7:49 14:15; with dative of person(s), Mat.9:10, Mrk.2:15, Luk.14:10.

συν-ανα-μίγνυμι (accusative to Bl., 8, -μείγνυμι), [in LXX: Hos.7:8 A ( hithpo.), Eze.20:18 A ;] to mix up together; pass., reflex, and metaphorically, to associate with (Plut., Ath., al.): 1Co.5:9 5:11, 2Th.3:14 (see M, Th., 117).
συν-ανα-παύομαι [in LXX: Isa.11:6 (רָבַץ) ;] with dative of person(s), to lie down to rest with, sleep with. Metaphorical, to be refreshed in spirit with: Rom.15:32 (L, om.).

συν-αντάω, -ῶ [in LXX for פָּגַע, פָּגַשׁ, קָרָא, קָרָה, etc. ;] to meet with: with dative of person(s), Luk.9:18, 37 22:10, Act.10:25, Heb.7:1, 10. Metaphorical, of events, to happen, befall (Ding. L., Plut., al.): Act.20:22.
συν-άντησις, -εως, ἡ Rec. for ὑπάντησις (which see), Mat.8:34.

συν-αντι-λαμβάνομαι [in LXX: Gen.30:8 R (), Exo.18:22, Num.11:17 (נָשָׂא), Psa.89:21 (כּוּן ni.) ;] to take hold with at the side for assistance; hence, to take a share in, help in bearing, and generally, help (with various constructions, see LXX, ll. with, Deiss., LAE, 83 f.): with dative, Luk.10:40, Rom.8:26.

συν-απ-άγω [in LXX: Exo.14:6 (לָקַח) ;] to lead away with or together (Ex, l.with). Pass., metaphorically (as συμπεριφέρομαι, see LS, see word), to be carried away with: with dative, Gal.2:13, 2Pe.3:17; of accommodating one­self to (EV, condescend to) things or persons (on the meaning, see ICC, in I.; Field, Notes, 163), Rom.12:16.
συν-απο-θνήσκω [in LXX: Sir.19:10 ;] to die with or together: Mrk.14:31, 2Co.7:3 (see Meyer, in l), 2Ti.2:11.
συν-απ-όλλυμι [in LXX chiefly for סָפָה ;] to destroy with or together. Pass., to perish together (with): with dative of person(s), Heb.11:31.
συν-απο-στέλλω [in LXX: Exo.33:2, 12 (שָׁלַח), I Est.5:2 ;] to send along with: with accusative, 2Co.12:18 (cf. MM, xxiv).
συν-αρμολογέω, -ῶ (ἁρμός, λέγω), = cl. συναρμόζω, to fit or frame together: of the parts of a building, Eph.2:21; of the members of the body, Eph.4:16 (Eccl.).

συν-αρπάζω [in LXX: Pro.6:25 (לָקַח), 2Ma.3:27 2Mac 4:41, 4Ma.5:4 ;] "perfective " of ἁοπάζω (see M, Pr., 113), (a) to seize and carry away (so most commonly) : Act.27:15; (b) to seize and hold: Luk.8:29, Act.6:12 19:29.
συν-αυξάνω [in LXX: 2Ma.4:4, 4Ma.13:27 ;] to cause to increase or grow together. Pass., to grow together: Mat.13:30 (Xen., al.).

σύν-δεσμος, -ου, ὁ (συνδέω), [in LXX for קֶשֶׁר, קְטַר, etc. ;] that which binds together, a bond: of the ligaments of the body, Col.2:19. Metaphorical, σ. ἀδικίας, Act.8:23; τ. τελειότητος, Col.3:14; τ. εἰρήνης, Eph.4:3 (see Lft., Col., ll. with).
συν-δέω [in LXX for שָׁבַץ, etc. ;] (a) to bind together; (b) to bind together with: pass., Heb.13:3.
to join in approving (Arist.).
In NT only, to glorify together: pass., Rom.8:17.

σύν-δουλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Est.4:7, 9 4:17, 23 5:3, 6 6:6, 13 (כְּנָת) ;] a fellow-servant: Mat.18:28-33 24:49; of servants of the same divine Lord, Col.1:7 4:7, Rev.6:11; so of angels, Rev.19:10 22:9.
συνδρομή, -ῆς, ἡ (συντρέχω), [in LXX: Jdth.10:18, 3Ma.3:8 ;] concourse, esp. of a riotous gathering: Act.21:30 (Arist., Polyb., al.).
συν-εγείρω [in LXX: Exo.23:5 B2 (עָזַב), Isa.14:9 (עוּר pil.), 4Ma.2:14 ;] to raise together: metaphorically, of the Christian's mystical resur­rection with Christ, Eph.2:6; pass., Col.2:12 3:1.
a council (Plat., Xen., al.; LXX; in π., σ. τ. πρεσβυτέρων, Deiss., BS, 156): of a local Jewish tribunal, Mat.10:17, Mrk.13:9.

The supreme ecclesiastical court of the Jews, the Sanhedrin (i.e. Talmudic סַנְהֵדְרִין = συνέδριον): Mat.5:22 26:59, Mrk.14:55 15:1, Luk.22:66, Act.5:21, 27 5:34, 41 6:12, 15 22:30 23:1, 6 23:15, 20 23:28 (WH, R, mg., om.) Act.24:20 of a meeting of the Sanhedrin, Jhn.11:47; of the place of meeting, Act.4:15.

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of (G4862) (G1476)

full.text: consciousness: with genitive obj., Heb.10:2, 1Pe.2:19.

In ethical sense, innate discernment, self-judging consciousness, conscience (Stoics and late writers): Rom.2:15 9:1, 1Co.10:29, 2Co.1:12 4:2 5:11, 1Ti.4:2, Heb.9:14; σ. ἀγαθή, Act.23:1, 1Ti.1:5 1:19, 1Pe.3:16 3:21; ἀσθενής, 1Co.8:7 8:10; ἀσθενοῦσα, 1Co.8:12; ἀπρόσκοπος, Act.24:16; καθαρά, 1Ti.3:9, 2Ti.1:3; καλή, Heb.13:18; πονηρά, Heb.10:22; ὁ νοῦς καὶ ἡ σ., Tit.1:15; διὰ τὴν σ., Rom.13:5, 1Co.10:25 10:27-28; κατὰ σ., Heb.9:9; ὑπὸ (τῆς) σ., Jhn.8:1-59 [9] (Rec.), 1Co.10:29 (cf. Cremer, 233 ff.; ICC on Rom.2:15; DB, i, 468 ff.).
to see together or at the same time (Arist., Xen.).
to see in one view, hence, of mental vision, to comprehend, understand (LXX, Polyb., Plut., al.) : Act.12:12 14:6 (on the related σύνοιδα, see see word).
σύν-ειμι (εἰμί to be), [in LXX: Jer.3:20 (רֵעַ), al. ;] to be with: Luk.9:18 (WH, mg., συνήντησαν), Act.22:11.
σύν-ειμι (εἶμι, to go), to come together: Luk.8:4.
συν-εισ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Job.22:4 (עִם בּוֹא), etc. ;] to enter together: with dative of person(s), before εἰς, with accusative loc., Jhn.6:22 18:15.
συν-έκδημος, -ου, ὁ (ἔκδημος, abroad), a fellow-traveller: Act.19:29, 2Co.8:1.
συν-εκ-λεκτός, -ή, -όν chosen together with, co-elect: ἡ ἐν Βαβυλῶνι συνεκλεκτή (on the meaning, see ICC, in l), 1Pe.5:13.
συν-ελαύνω [in LXX: 2Ma.4:26 2Mac 4:42 2Mac 5:5 ;] to drive together, force together: εἰς εἰρήνην, Act.7:26, Rec. (WH, RV, συνήλλασσεν).
συν-επι-μαρτυρέω, -ῶ to join in attesting, bear witness together with: Heb.2:4 (Arist., Polyb., a1.).
συν-έπομαι [in LXX: 2Ma.15:2, 3Ma.5:48 R 3Ma.6:21 ;] to follow with, accompany: with dative of person(s), Act.20:4.
prop., to work together (with): absol., Mrk.16:20, 1Co.16:16, 2Co.6:1; with dative, Jas.2:22; dative commod., Rom.8:28, T, R, txt. (but see infr.).
In Hellenistic writers (M, Pr., 65), trans., to cause to work together (cf. ICC, in l): with accusative of thing(s), Rom.8:28 [WH] R, mg. (but see supr.).

συνεργός, -όν [in LXX: 2Ma.8:7 Mac 14:5 ;] a fellow-worker: with genitive of person(s), Rom.16:3, 9 16:21, Php.2:25 4:3, 1Th.3:2, Rec., Phm 1 24; θεοῦ (cf. Lft., Notes, 41, 188), 1Co.3:9 (pl.), 1Th.3:2 [WH] R, mg. (R, txt., διάκονον τοῦ θ.; on the original reading, see ICC, in l); τ. χαρᾶς ὑηῶν, 2Co.1:24; τ. ἀληθείᾳ, III Jn 8; before εἰς, 2Co.8:23, Col.4:11.

(cl.) to come together, assemble: Mrk.3:20 14:53, T, WH, txt., Act.1:6 2:6 10:27 16:13 19:32 21:22 22:30 28:17, 1Co.14:26; before εἰς, with accusative loc., Act.5:16; ἐπὶ τ. αὐτό, 1Co.11:20 14:23; with dative of person(s), Mrk.14:53, WH, mg., R (πρὸς αὐτόν, see Field, Notes, 40), Jhn.11:33; ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ, 1Co.11:18; ἐνθάδε, Act.25:17; Mrk.6:33, WH, mg. (cf. WH, Intr., 95 ff.); ὅπου, Jhn.18:20; with inf., Luk.5:15; εἰς τ. φαγεῖν, 1Co.11:33; εἰς τ. ἧσσον, 1Co.11:17 11:34; of sexual intercourse (Xen., al.; γυναικί or absol.), Mat.1:18.
In later sense (see exx. in Milligan, Selections, 64, 105), to accompany: with dative of person

συν-εσθίω [in LXX: Gen.43:32, Exo.18:12 (אָכַל), 4Ki.12:17 (בָּרָה), Psa.101:5 ;] to eat with one: with dative of person(s), Luk.15:2, Act.10:41 11:3, 1Co.5:11; before μετά, with genitive of person(s), Gal.2:12.
a running or flowing together (Hom.).
the understanding, the mind or intelligence: Mrk.12:33. SYN.: see: σοφία

subs.text: the understanding, the mind or intelligence: Mrk.12:33. SYN.: see: σοφία

full.text: συνετός, ή, -όν (συνίημι), [in LXX for בִּין, חָכָם, etc. ;] intelligent, sagacious, understanding: Mat.11:25, Luk.10:21, Act.13:7, 1Co.1:19" (LXX) .

συν-ευ-δοκέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Ma.1:57, 1Ma.4:28 א1, 2Ma.11:24 2Mac 11:35 ;] to join in approving, consent, agree to or with: absol., Act.22:20; with dative of person(s), Rom.1:32; with dative of thing(s), Luk.11:48, Act.8:1; with inf., 1Co.7:12-13 (chiefly in late writers).
συν-ευωχέω, -ῶ to entertain together. Pass., to fare sumptuously or feast together or with: Ju 12; with dative of person(s), 2Pe.2:13 (Arist., Luc., al.).
to stand over (Thuc.).
to rise together: before κατά, with genitive of person(s) (against), Act.16:22.
to hold together (τ. συνέχον τ. πάντα, Wis.1:7): of closing the ears, Act.7:57 (τ. στόμα, Isa.52:15); to hem in, press on every side: Luk.8:45 19:43.

to hold fast; (a) of a prisoner, to hold in charge (Luc.; cf. exx. in Deiss., BS, 160; MM, xxiv): Luk.22:63; (b) to constrain: 2Co.5:14; pass., Luk.12:50, Act.18:5 (τ. λόγῳ; cf. Field, Notes, 128), Php.1:23; in pass., of ills, to be seized or afflicted by, suffering from: Mat.4:24, Luk.4:38 8:37, Act.28:8.
συν-ήδομαι to rejoice together; with dative, to rejoice with or in: τ. νόμῳ τ. θεοῦ, Rom.7:22 (see Lft., Notes, 304).
habit, custom: Jhn.18:39, 1Co.11:16; with genitive obj. (cf. Æschin., 23, 37, and see MM, xxiv), habitual use, force of habit with respect to, 1Co.8:7.
συν-ηλικιώτης (ἡλικία), = συνῆλιζ (Æsch.), one of the same age, an equal in age: Gal.1:14.
συν-θάπτω to bury together with: in symbolical sense, of baptism, σὐτῷ (with Christ), Rom.6:4, Col.2:12.
συν-θλάω, -ῶ [in LXX for מָחַץ etc. ;] to crush together, crush: Mat.21:44 ([WH], R, mg., om.), Luk.20:18.
συν-θλίβω [in LXX: Ecc.12:6 א A (רָצַץ), Sir.34:14 (31:14), 1Ma.15:14 A ;] to press together, press on all sides: Mrk.5:24, 31.
συν-θρύπτω to break in pieces, crush: metaphorically, τ. καρδίαν, Act.21:13.
to bring or set together.

Metaphorical, to perceive, understand: Mat.13:13-15 (LXX), Mat.13:19 15:10, Mrk.4:9 (WH, mg.), Mrk.4:12 7:14 8:17, 21 Luk.8:10, Act.7:25 28:26-27, Rom.15:21" (LXX), 2Co.10:12; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:23, 51, Luk.2:50 18:34 24:45; before ὅτι, Mat.16:12 17:13; before quæst. indir., Eph.5:17; ἐπὶ τ. ἄρτοις, Mrk.6:52; as subst., συνίων (ὁ σ., WH, mg.), a man of understanding, i.e. in moral and religious sense, Rom.3:11 (LXX).
trans.,

subs.text: to commend, recommend: with accusative of person
to show, prove, establish: with accusative, Rom.3:5 5:8, 2Co.6:4; dupl. accusative, Gal.2:13; accusative and inf., 2Co.7:11

full.text: Intrans., pf., συνέστηκα (as also 2 aor. and plpf.);

subs.text: to stand with or near: Luk.9:32;

full.text: συν-οδεύω [in LXX: Zec.8:21 א1 (הָלַךְ), Tob.5:16 א, Wis.6:23 ;] to journey with: with dative of person(s), Act.9:7 (Plut., al.).
a journey in company (Plut., al.).
By meton., κ company of travellers, a caravan: Luk.2:44.
συν-οικέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בַּעַל etc. ;] to dwell together: of man and wife (Hdt., al.), with dative of person(s), 1Pe.3:7,
to build together or with (with dative of person

subs.text: , 1Esdras, l.with).

full.text: Of various materials, to build up together (Thuc., al.): metaphorically, of Christians, Eph.2:22.
συν-ομιλέω, -ῶ to converse with (absol., FlJ, Bl, v, 13, 1; before μετά, Cebes, 13): with dative of person(s), Act.10:27.
συν-ομορέω, -ῶ to border on: with dative, Act.18:7 (Byz.).
a holding or being held together.
a narrow place (Hom., al.). Metaphorical, straits, distress: Luk.21:25; καρδίας, 2Co.2:4.
συν-τάσσω [in LXX chiefly for צָוָה pi. ;] to prescribe, ordain, arrange: with dative of person(s), Mat.21:6 26:19 27:10" (LXX) .
in cl., a joint payment or contribution for public service; hence, generally, joint action (Plat.).
In late writers (Polyb., al.), consummation, completion: σ. τ. αἰῶνος, -ων (cf. Dan LXX TH Dan.12:13, al.), Mat.13:39-40, 49 24:3 28:20, Heb.9:26.
to complete, finish, bring to an end: Luk.4:13; pass., Luk.4:2, Jhn.2:3, WH, mg., Act.21:27.
to effect, accomplish, bring to fulfilment (cf. MM, xxiv): Mrk.13:4, Rom.9:28, Heb.8:8.
to cut in pieces.
to cut down, cut short: metapb., λόγον, Rom.9:28 (LXX) (Hdt., Plat., Fur., al.).

συν-τηρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Eze.18:19 (שָׁמַר), Da TH Dan.7:28 A (נְטַר), Sir.13:12 and freq., Tob.1:11, 2Ma.12:42, al. ;] "perfective" of τηρέω (see M, Pr., 113, 116), to preserve, keep safe, keep close: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.6:20; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.9:17; ἐν τ. καρδίᾳ, Luk.2:19 (Arist. and later writers; cf. MM, xxiv).

συν-τίθημι [in LXX: 1Ki.22:13 (קָשַׁר), etc. ;] to place or put together, in various senses. Mid., (a) to observe, perceive; (b) to determine, agree, covenant: with inf., Luk.22:5; before τοῦ, with inf., Act.23:20; before ἵνα, Jhn.9:22. συν-επι-τίθημι, [in LXX for שִׁית, etc. ;] to help in putting on. Mid., to join in attacking (Thuc.): Act.24:9 (RV, joined in the charge).
συν-τόμως, adv. (συντέμνω) [in LXX: Pro.13:23 23:28, 3Ma.5:25 ;] concisely, briefly: Act.24:4 (for a similar ex., see MM, xxiv).
συν-τρέχω [in LXX: Psa.50:18 (עִם רָצָה), עִם רָצָה), 2Ma.2:1-32, Jdt.4.1-15 ;] to run together or with: before ἐκεῖ, Mrk.6:33; πρόσ, with accusative, Act.3:11; metaphorically, 1Pe.4:4.

συν-τρίβω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁבַר ;] to shatter, break in pieces: Mat.12:20 (LXX), Mrk.5:4 14:3, Jhn.19:36" (LXX), Rev.2:27; of persons and parts of the body, to break, crush, bruise: Luk.9:39; figuratively, Rom.16:20.

σύν-τριμμα, -τος, τό (συντρίβω), [in LXX (Lev.21:19, al.) chiefly for שֶׁבֶר; also for שֹׁד (Isa.59:7), etc. ;] a fracture (Arist.; Le, l.with, al.). Metaphorical, calamity, destruction (Isa, l.with, Wis.3:3, al.): Rom.3:16" (LXX) .
prop., one nourished or brought up with, a foster-brother: Act.13:1 EV.
In Hellenistic usage, as a court term, an intimate friend of a king (see Deiss., BS, 305, 310 f.): Act.13:1 (cf. also MM, xxiv).
συν-τυγχάνω [in LXX: 2Ma.8:14 ;] to meet with, fall in with: with dative of person(s), Luk.8:19.
Συντύχη (T, -τυχή), -ης, ἡ Syntyche: Php.4:2.
συν-υπο-κρίνομαι to play a part with, dissemble with: with dative, Gal.2:13 (Polyb.).
συν-υπουργέω, -ῶ to help together, join in serving: with dative instr., 2Co.1:11.
συν-ωδίνω to be in travail together (Arist.): metapb. (Eur.), Rom.8:22.
συνωμοσία, -ας, ή (συνόμνυμι, to conspire), [in Sm.: Eze.22:25 ;] a conspiracy: Act.23:13.
ionic Συρήκουσαι, doric Συράκοσαι, and Σῦράκοσσαι, Syracuse , (Herdotus Historicus), etc.
Σύρος, -ου, ὁ, fem. Σύρα a Syrian: Luk.4:27, Mrk.7:26, WH, mg.
Συροφοινίκισσα (Rec. -φοίνισσα), -ης, ἡ a Syropænician woman: Mrk.7:26, WH, txt., RV (cf. ἡ Φοινίκη Συρία, Diod., 19, 93).
Σύρτις (L, σῦρτις), -εως, accusative -ιν, ἡ, Syrtis, the name of two large sand-banks on the Libyan coast: Act.27:17.

σύρω [in LXX: 4Ki.17:13 (סָחַב), 4Ma.6:1, al. ;] to draw, drag: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.21:8, Rev.12:4; with accusative of person(s), of taking to trial or punish­ment, Act.8:3; ἔξω τ. πόλεως, Act.14:19; ἐπὶ τ. πολιτάρχας (cf. 4Mac, l.with), Act.17:6 (cf. κατα-ούρω).
συ-σπαράσσω to convulse completely: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.9:20, Luk.9:42 (cf. σπαράσσω).

σύσ-σημον (T, συν-), -ου, τό (σῆμα), [in LXX: Jdg.20:38, 40 (מַשְׂאֵת), Isa.5:26 49:22 62:10 (נֵס) ;] a fixed sign or signal: Mrk.14:44. (Strab., Plut., al.; condemned by Atticists, see Rutherford, NPhr., 492 f.)
σύν-σωμος (Rec. συσσ-), -ον (σῶμα), of the same body: Eph.3:6 (Eccl.).

στασιαστής (στασιάζω, to stir up sedition, Jdth.7:15, 2Ma.4:30 2Mac 14:6 ) = Att.. στασιώτης, a rebel, revolutionist, one who stirs up sedition: Mrk.15:7 (FlJ, al.). συ-στασιαστής, -οῦ, ὁ, a fellow-rioter: Mrk.15:7, Rec.
for putting together, constructive.
for bringing together, introductory, commendatory: ἐπιστολή (Arist., Diog. L., al.; and see MM, xxiv), 2Co.3:1.

συν-σταυρόω (Rec. συσ-), -ῶ, to crucify together with: pass., with dative, Jhn.19:32; id. before σύν, Mat.27:44, Mrk.15:32. Metaphorical, of the mystical death of the Christian with Christ: Rom.6:6, Gal.2:20 (Eccl.).
to draw together, contract, shorten: of time, 1Co.7:29.
to wrap up: of enshrouding a body for burial (Eur.), with accusative, Act.5:6.
συν-στενάζω (Rec. συσ-), to groan together or with: Rom.8:22 (with dative of person(s), Eur., Ion., 935).
συν-στοιχέω (Rec. συσ-), -ῶ, to stand in the same rank or line (Polyb). Metaphorical, to correspond to: Gal.4:25 (see Lft., in l).
συν-στρατιώτης (Rec. συσ-), -ου, ὁ a fellow-soldier (Plat., Xen., al.). Metaphorical, of fellowship in Christian service: Php.2:25, Phm 2 .
to twist together: with accusative of thing
Pass., of persons, to gather themselves together: Mat.17:22 (WH, R, mg.).
a twisting together (Plat.).
a concourse, a riotous gathering (Polyb.): Act.19:40; σ. ποιεῖσθαι (Am, l.with), to make a compact (RV, banded themselves together): Act.23:12.
συν-σχηματιζω (Rec. συσ-), (σχῆμα), to conform to (Arist.). Pass., to be conformed to, conform oneself to: Rom.12:2, 1Pe.1:14.
Συχάρ (Ε, Σιχάρ), indecl., ἡ Sychar, a town of Samaria: Jhn.4:5.
the son of Hamor (Gen.33:19): Act.7:16 b, Rec.
A city of Samaria (AV, Sychem; in LXX also sometimes Σίκιμα, -ων, as in FlJ): Act.7:16 a,b.
σφαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (σφάζω), [in LXX for טָבַח, הֲרֵגָה, etc. ;] slaughter: Act.8:32 (LXX); πρόβατα σφαγῆς, Rom.8:36 (LXX); ἡμέρα σφαγῆς, Jas.5:5.
σφάγιον, -ου, τό (σφαγή), [in LXX: Amo.5:25 (זֶבַח), Eze.21:10, 15 21:28 (טֶבַח), Lev.22:23 (נְדָבָה) ;] a victim for slaughter: Act.7:42 (LXX).

σφάζω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַט ;] to slay, slaughter (esp. of victims for sacrifice): ἀρνίον, Rev.5:6, 12 13:8; of persons, 1Jn.3:12, Rev.5:9 6:4, 9 18:24; ἐσφαγμένη εἰς θάνατον (RV, smitten unto death), Rev.13:3 (cf. κατα-σφάζω).

σφόδρα adv. (prop. neut. of σφοδρός, excessive, violent) [in LXX chiefly for מְאֹד ;] very, very much, exceedingly: with verbs, Mat.17:6, 23 18:31 19:25 26:22 27:54, Act.6:7; with adjectives, Mat.2:10, Mrk.16:4, Luk.18:23, Rev.16:21.
σφοδρῶς adv., exceedingly: Act.27:18.

σφραγίζω [in LXX chiefly for חָתַם (Deu.32:34, al.), also for סָתַם (Da TH Dan.8:26) ;] to seal, (a) for security: Mat.27:66, Rev.20:3; τ. καρπὸν τοῦτον, figuratively (cf. Deiss., BS, 238 f.; MM, xxiv; DB, iv, 427a), Rom.15:28; (b) for concealment, hence, metaphorically, to hide (Da TH Dan.9:24, Jhn.14:17): Rev.10:4 22:10; (with) for distinction, Rev.7:3-8; metaphorically, Eph.4:30; mid., 2Co.1:22; (d) for authentication (Est.8:8): Jhn.3:33 6:27 (cf. κατα-σφραγίζω).
a seal, signet: Rev.7:2.

The impression of a seal or signet, a seal (on its various purposes, see: σφραγίζω): on a book or roll, Rev.5:1-2, 5 5:9 6:1, 3 6:5, 7 6:9, 12 8:1; metaphorically, Rom.4:11, 1Co.9:2, 2Ti.2:19, Rev.9:4.
σφυρόν, -οῦ, τό, [in LXX: Job.41:21 (לָהַט) ;] the ankle: Act.3:7, Rec. (T, WH, σφυδρόν; see Bl., § 6, 8).

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative probably of the same as

full.text: of place, near.
Of degree, almost, nearly: Act.13:44 19:26, Heb.9:22.

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of the alternate of (G2192)

full.text: σχῆμα, -τος, τό (ἔχω), [in LXX: Isa.3:17 (פֹּת) ;] figure, fashion: 1Co.7:31, Php.2:8. SYN.: see: μορφή
σχίζω [in LXX chiefly for בָּקַע ;] to cleave, rend: Mat.27:51, Mrk.1:10 15:38, Luk.5:36 23:45, Jhn.19:24 21:11; metaphorically, in pass., to be divided into factions, Act.14:4 23:7.
σχίσμα, -τος, τό (σχίζω), a rent (Arist., al.): Mat.9:16, Mrk.2:21. Metaphorical, a dissension, division: Jhn.7:43 9:16 10:19, 1Co.1:10 11:18 12:25. SYN.: see: αἵρεσις
σχοινίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of σχοῖνος, a rush), [in LXX chiefly for a rope (prop., one made of rushes): Jhn.2:15, Act.27:32.

σχολάζω (σχολή), [in LXX: Exo.5:8, 17, Psa.46:10 (רָפָה ni., hi.) ;] to be at leisure, hence, to have time or opportunity for, to devote oneself to, be occupied in: 1Co.7:5; of things, to be unoccupied, empty (Plut., Eur., al.): οἶκος, Mat.12:44, Luk.11:25 (T [WH], R, om.).
leisure.
Later (from Plato on),

subs.text: that for which leisure is employed, a disputation, lecture;

etymology: probably feminine of a presumed derivative of the alternate of (G2192)

full.text: σώζω (on the more accurate σῴζω, see WH, Intr., §410; Bl., §3, 1-3), [in LXX chiefly for ישׁע hi., also for מלט ni., נצל ni., etc. ;] to save from peril, injury or suffering: Mat.8:25, Mrk.13:20, Luk.23:35, al.; τ. ψυχήν, Mat.16:25, al.; before ἐκ, Jhn.12:27, Heb.5:7, Ju 5; of healing, restoring to health: Mat.9:22, Mrk.5:34, al. In NT, esp. of salvation from spiritual disease and death, in which sense it is "spoken of in Scripture as either (1) past, (2) present, or (3) future, according as red...

Prop., of the human body, (a) as always in Hom. (opposite to δέμας), of the dead body: Mat.27:58, 59 Mrk.15:43, al.; (b) of the living body: Luk.11:34, 1Co.6:13, al.; ἐν σ. εἶναι, Heb.13:3; as the instrument of the soul, τὰ διὰ τοῦ σ., 2Co.5:10; opposite to πνεῦμα, Rom.8:10, 1Co.5:3 7:4, Jas.2:26; to ψυχή, Mat.6:25 10:28, Luk.12:22 (cf. Wis.1:1, al.); to τὸ π. καὶ ἡ ψ., 1Th.5:23; σ. ψυχικόν, opposite to σ. πνευματικόν, 1Co.15:44; ὁ ναὸς τοῦ σ. αὐτοῦ (genitive epexeg.), Jhn.2:21; τὸ σ. τ. ταπεινώσεως (Hebraistic "genitive of definition"; M, Pr., 73f.; Bl., §35, 5), opposite to τὸ σ. τ. δοξῆς αὐτοῦ, Php.3:21; similarly, τὸ σ. τ. σαρκός, Col.1:22; σ. τοῦ θανάτου (subject to death), Rom.7:24; σ. τ. ἁμαρτίας, Rom.6:6; (with) periphr., ἀνθρώπου, then absol., σῶμα (Soph., Xen., al.), a person, and in later writers (Polyb., al.), a slave: Rev.18:13 (cf. MM, i, ii, xxiv; Deiss., BS, 160).
Of the bodies of animals: living, Jas.3:3; dead, Heb.13:11 ( Exo.29:14, al.).
Of inanimate objects (cf. Eng. "heavenly bodies"): 1Co.15:37, 38 40 (Diod., al.).

Of any corporeal substance (Plat., al.): opposite to σκιά, Col.2:17. Metaphorical, of a number of persons united by a common bond; in NT, of the Church as the spiritual body of Christ: Rom.12:5, 1Co.10:16, 17 12:13, 27, Eph.1:23 2:16 4:4, 12, 16 5:23, 30, Col.1:18, 24 2:19 3:15; ἓν σ. κ. ἓν πνεῦμα, Eph.4:4.
σωματικός, -ή, -όν (σῶμα), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:32 4Mac 3:1 ;] (a) of or for the body, bodily: γυμνασία, 1Ti.4:8; (b) (opposite to ἀσώματος) bodily, corporeal: εἶδος, Luk.3:22.
σωματικῶς adv., bodily, corporeally: Col.2:9 (see ICC, Lft., in l).
Σώπατρος, -ου, ὁ Sopater: Act.20:4.

σωρεύω [in LXX: Pro.25:22 (חָתָה), Jdth.15:11 ;] (a) to heap on: with accusative before ἐπί with accusative, Rom.12:20 (LXX) (Arist., al.); (b) to heap with: with accusative and dative, metaphorically, ἁμαρτίαις, 2Ti.3:6 (overwhelmed with, Field, Notes, 217) (Polyb.).
Σωσθένης, -ου, ὁ Sosthenes; (a) a Jewish ruler: Act.18:7; (b) a Christian: 1Co.1:1.
Σωσίπατρος, -ου, ὁ Sosipater: Rom.16:21.

σωτήρ, -ῆρος, ὁ (σώζω), [in LXX for יָשַׁע and cognate forms ;] saviour, deliverer, preserver, a frequently epithet of kings in the Ptolemaic and Rom. periods (Deiss., BS, 83; LAE, 368 f.; MM, xxiv), in NT, (a) of God (as LXX: Psa.24:5, Isa.12:2, al.): Luk.1:47, 1Ti.1:1 2:3 4:10, Tit.1:3 2:10 3:4, Ju 25; (b) of Christ: Luk.2:11, Act.5:31 13:23, Php.3:20; τ. κόσμου, Jhn.4:42, 1Jn.4:14 (for the general use of the word, see Westc., in l); ἡμῶν, 2Ti.1:10, Tit.1:4 3:6; θεὸς (κύριος) καὶ σ. (see Deiss...

σωτηρία, -ας, ἡ (σωτήρ), [in LXX for יֶשַׁע, יְשׁוּעָה, תְּשׁוּעָה, פְּלֵיטָה, etc. ;] deliverance, preservation, salvation, safety (Lat. salus): Act.7:25 27:34, Heb.11:7; ἐξ ἐχθρῶν, Luk.1:71. In NT esp. of Messianic and spiritual salvation (see: σώζω): Luk.19:9, Jhn.4:22, Act.4:12 13:47, Rom.11:11, 2Th.2:13, 2Ti.3:15, Heb.2:3 6:9, 2Pe.3:15, Ju 3; opposite to ἀπηώλεια, Php.1:28; αἰώνιος-., Heb.5:9; ὁ λόγος (τὸ εὐαγγέλιον) τῆς σ., Act.13:26, Eph.1:13; ὁδὸς σωτηρίας, Act.16:17; κέρας σωτηρίας, Luk...

σωτήριος, -ον (σωτήρ), [in LXX for יְשׁוּעָה, שֶׁלֶם, etc. ;] saving, bringing salvation, in NT always in spiritual sense (see: σώζω, σωτηρία): ἡ χάρις ἡ σ., Tit.2:1. Neut., -τὸ σ., as subst. (cl.), salvation: Luk.2:30; τ. σ. τ. θεοῦ, Luk.3:6, Act.28:28; περικεφαλαίαν τοῦ σ., Eph.6:17.

σωφρονέω, -ῶ (σώφρων), (a) to be of sound mind or in one's right mind: Mrk.5:15, Luk.8:35; opposite to ἐκστῆναι, 2Co.5:13 (Hdt.); (b) to be temperate, discreet, self-controlled (opposite to μαίνεσθαι, ὑβρίζειν, etc.; sch., Thuc., al.): Tit.2:6; opposite to ὑπερφρονεῖν, Rom.12:3; σ. καὶ νήφειν, 1Pe.4:7.
σωφρονίζω [in Aq.: Isa.38:16 ;] to make σώφρων, recall one to his senses, control (RV, train): with accusative of person(s), Tit.2:4.
σωφρονισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (σωφρονίζω), (a) an admonishing (F1J, Plut., al.); (b) self-control, self-discipline: 2Ti.1:7 (on the reflexive meaning here, see Ellic., in l).
σωφρόνως adv., [in LXX: Wis.9:11 ;] with sound mind, prudently, soberly: Tit.2:12.

σωφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Est.3:13, Wis.8:7, 2Ma.4:37, 4Ma.6:1-35 ;] (a) soundness of mind, good sense, sanity (opposite to μανία, Xen.): Act.26:25; (b) self-control, sobriety: 1Ti.2:9 (see Tr., Syn., § xx), 1Ti.2:15.
σώφρων, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.9:1-32 ;] (a) of sound mind, sane, sensible; (b) self-controlled, sober-minded: 1Ti.3:2, Tit.1:8 2:2, 5 (Arist.).
ταβέρνη (Lat. taberna), see: Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι. Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι (see: ταβέρνη), Three Taverns (Lit. Tres Tabernae) a halting place on the Appian Way: Act.28:15 (see DB, iv, 690).
Ταβειθά (Rec. Ταβιθά), ἡ (Aram. טַבְיְתָא or טְבִיתָא), Tabitha: Act.9:36, 40 (cf. Δορκάς).

τάγμα, -τος, τό (τάσσω), [in LXX chiefly for דֶּגֶל, also for חַי, רַגְלִי ;] that which has been arranged or placed in order; esp. as military term, a company, troop, division, rank: metaphorically, 1Co.15:23 (see ICC, in l).
τακτός, -ή, -όν (τάσσω), [in LXX: Job.12:5 (שַׁאֲנָן) ;] ordered, fixed, stated: ἡμέρα, Act.12:21,
to do hard labour, suffer hardship or distress: Jas.4:9.
In cl. occasionally trans., to weary, distress (so Psa.17:9, Isa.33:1).
hard work (Hippocr.).
hardship, suffering, distress: Rom.3:16 (LXX) pl., Jas.5:1 (see B1., § 32, 6; WM, 220; Swete, Mk., 153). (Hdt., Thuc., al.; and cf. MM, xxiv.)
ταλαίωρος, -ον [in LXX: Psa.137:8 (שָׁדַד), Isa.33:1 (ποιεῖν τ., שָׁדַד), Tob.13:10, Wis.3:11, al. ;] distressed, miserable, wretched: Rom.7:24, Rev.3:17.
ταλαντιαῖος, -α, -ον (τάλαντον), (a) worth a talent; (b) of a talent's weight: Rev.16:21.
a balance (Hom.).

that which is weighed, a talent; (a) a talent in weight (in Horn. always of gold); (b) a sum of money, whether gold or silver, equivalent to a talent in weight (see DB, iii, 418 ff.): Mat.18:24 25:15 ff.

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of the original form of

full.text: ταλειθά (Rec. ταλιθά), (Aram. טְלִיתָא, see Dalman, Gr., 150), talitha, i.e. maiden: Mrk.5:41.
a treasury (Thuc., al).
a store-chamber (Arist., Xen.; Deu.28:8): Luk.12:24.
an inner chamber (Xen., Hell., v, 4, 5 Isa.26:20, al.): Mat.6:6 24:26, Luk.12:3.

etymology: neuter contraction of a presumed derivative of

full.text: an arranging, hence, in military sense, disposition of an army, battle array (Thuc., al.).
arrangement, order (Plat., al): Luk.1:8.
due order: Col.2:5 (ICC, in l); κατὰ τάξιν, 1Co.14:40.
office, order (for exx., see MM, xxiv): Heb.5:6 (LXX), Heb.5:6 6:20 7:11, 17" (LXX) .

ταπεινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for שָׁפָל, עָנִי, etc. ;] low-lying; metaphorically, (a) lowly, of low degree, brought low: Jas.1:9, 2Co.7:6 (cf. Sir.25:23); οἱ τ., opposite to δυνάσται, Luk.1:52; τ. ταπεινοῖς (neut., R, txt.; masc., R, mg.; see ICC, in l), Rom.12:16; (b) lowly in spirit, humble, in cl. usually in a slighting sense (see Tr., Syn., § xlii; but see also Abbott, Essays, 81), in NT in an honourable sense: 2Co.10:1; before τ. καρδίᾳ, Mat.11:29 (cf. Psa.34:19); opposite to ὑπερήφανος, Jas....

ταπεινοφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ (ταπεινόφρων) lowliness of mind, humility: Act.20:19, Eph.4:2, Php.2:3, Col.3:12, 1Pe.5:5; of a false humility, Col.2:18, 23 (rare outside of NT, but found in bad sense in FlJ, BJ, iv, 9, 2; also in Epictet., see Tr., Syn., § xlii).

ταπεινόω, -ῶ (ταπεινός), [in LXX chiefly for עָנָה, also for שָׁפֵל, כָּנַע, etc. ;] to make low: ὄρος (βουνόν), Luk.3:5 (LXX). Metaphorical, to humble, abase: Mat.18:4 23:12, Luk.14:11 18:14, 2Co.11:7 12:21, Php.2:8; pass., Mat.23:12, Luk.14:11 18:14, Php.4:12; id. with mid. sense, Jas.4:10, 1Pe.5:6.
ταπείνωσις, -εως, ἡ (ταπεινόω), [in LXX chiefly for עֳנִי ;] abasement, humiliation, low estate: Luk.1:48, Act.8:33" (LXX), Php.3:21, Jas.1:10 (Plat., Arist., and later writers).

ταράσσω [in LXX for בָּהַל, רָגַז, etc., forty-six words in all ;] to disturb, trouble, stir up: primarily in physical sense (Hom., Eur.): τ. ὕδωρ, Jhn.1:5 [4], Jhn.1:7. Metaphorical, of the mind (Æsch., Plat., al.), to trouble, disquiet, perplex: Act.15:24, Gal.1:7 5:10; ἑαυτόν (troubled himself; Westc., in l), Jhn.11:33; of a crowd, to stir up, Act.17:8, 13; pass., Mat.2:3 14:26, Mrk.6:50, Luk.1:12 24:38, Jhn.12:27 (Psa.6:4) Psa.14:1, 7, 1Pe.3:14" (LXX) ; τ. πνεύματι, Jhn.13:21 (cf. δια-, ἐκ-τ...
ταραχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ταράσσω), [in LXX for חָלָה, מְהוּמָה, etc. ;] trouble, disturbance: τ. ὕδατος, Jhn.1:5 [4]. In pl. (as in cl.), tumults: Mrk.13:8, Rec.
τάραχος, -ου, ὁ (ταράσσω), [in LXX for מְהוּמָה, etc. ;] later form of ταραχή (Xen., al.; see Thackeray, Gr., 159): Act.12:18 19:23.
Ταρσεύς, -έως, ὁ (Ταρσός), of Tarsus: Act.9:11 21:39.
Ταρσός, -oῦ Tarsus, a city of Cilicia: Act.9:30 11:25 22:3.
ταρταρόω, -ῶ (Τάρταρος, a Greek name for the underworld, esp. the abode of the damned), to cast into hell: 2Pe.2:4 (see Mayor, in l).

τάσσω [in LXX chiefly for שׂוּם, also for צָוָה pi., נָתַן, etc. ;] primarily, in military sense, then generally, to draw up in order, arrange in place, assign, appoint, order: with dative (accusative) and inf. (Bl., § 72, 5), Act.15:2 18:2 (διατ-, WH) Act.22:10; ἑαυτούς, 1Co.16:15; pass., Mat.8:9 (T [WH], R, txt., om.), Luk.7:8, Rom.13:1; ὅσοι ἦσαν τεταγμένοι εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον (perh. in mid. sense; see EGT and Page, in l), Act.13:48; mid., to appoint for oneself or by one's own authority, Mat.28...

etymology: a prolonged form of a primary verb (which latter appears only in certain tenses);

full.text: ταῦρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שׁוֹר ;] a bull: Mat.22:4, Act.14:13, Heb.9:13 10:4.

as subst., this one, he; (a) absol.: Mat.3:17, Mrk.9:7, Luk.7:44, 45, Jhn.1:15, Act.2:15, al.; expressing contempt (cl.), Mat.13:55, 56, Mrk.6:2, 3, Jhn.6:42, al.; εἰς τοῦτο, Mrk.1:38, Rom.14:9; μετὰ τοῦτο (ταῦτα; V. Westc. on Jhn.5:1), Jhn.2:12 11:7, al.; (b) epanaleptic (referring to what precedes): Mat.5:19, Mrk.3:35, Luk.9:48, Jhn.6:46, Rom.7:10, al.; (with) proleptic (referring to what follows): before ἵνα (Bl., §69, 6), Luk.1:43, Jhn.3:19 (and freq.) 15:8, Rom.14:9, al.; before ὅτι, Luk.10:11, Jhn.9:3o, Act.24:14, Rom.2:3, al.; ὅπως, Rom.9:17; ἐάν, Jhn.13:35; (d) special idioms: τοῦτο μὲν . . . τ. δέ (cl), partly . . . partly. Heb.10:33; καὶ τοῦτο (τοῦτον, ταῦτα), and that (him) too, Rom.13:11, 1Co.2:2, Heb.11:12; τοῦτ᾽ ἐστιν, Mat.27:46.

As adj., with subst.; (a) with art. (α) before the art.: Mat.12:32, Mrk.9:29, Luk.7:44, Jhn.4:15, Rom.11:24, Rev.19:9, al.; (β) after the noun: Mat.3:9, Mrk.12:16, Luk.11:31, Jhn.4:13, Act.6:13, Rom.15:28, 1Co.1:20, Rev.2:24, al.; (b) with subst. anarth. (with predicative force; Bl., §49, 4): Luk.1:36 2:2 24:21, Jhn.2:11 4:54 21:14, 2Co.13:1.

etymology: nominative or accusative case neuter plural of (G3778)

full.text: thus, in the same way
hence, (to or with or by, etc.) these

etymology: dative case and accusative case feminine plural respectively of (G3778)

full.text: - (towards or of) this

etymology: dative case, accusative case and genitive case respectively of the feminine singular of (G3778)

full.text: ταφή, -ῆς, ἡ (θάπτω), [in LXX chiefly for קָבַר and cognate forms ;] burial: Mat.27:7 (Hdt., al.; for other meanings, see LS, see word, Deiss., BS, 355 f., MM xxiv).
a burial (Hom., al.).
τάχα (ταχύς), adv., [in LXX: Wis.13:6 14:19 ;] (a) (chiefly poët.), quickly, presently; (b) perhaps: Rom.5:7, Phm 15.

ταχέως (ταχύς) adv., [in LXX chiefly for מְהֵרָה and cognate forms ;] quickly, hastily: Luk.14:21 16:6, Jhn.11:31, 1Co.4:19, Php.2:19, 24, 2Ti.4:9; with suggestion of rashness, Gal.1:6, 2Th.2:2, 1Ti.5:22.
ταχινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Pro.1:16, Hab.1:6, Isa.59:7 (מָהַר), Wis.13:2, Sir.11:22 18:26 ;] poët. and late for ταχύς, swift: of swift approach, 2Pe.1:14 2:1.

τάχειον, see: ταχύ. ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον (T, Rec. τάχιον), = cl. θᾶσσον, θᾶττον (Att..), (see Bl., § 44, 3): Jhn.13:27 20:4, 1Ti.3:14 (T, Rec.), Heb.13:19, 23 Superl., τάχιστα: ὡς τ., as quickly as possible (BL, § 11, 3), Act.17:15.

etymology: neuter singular of the comparative of (G5036)

full.text: τάχιστα, see: ταχύ. ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον (T, Rec. τάχιον), = cl. θᾶσσον, θᾶττον (Att..), (see Bl., § 44, 3): Jhn.13:27 20:4, 1Ti.3:14 (T, Rec.), Heb.13:19, 23 Superl., τάχιστα: ὡς τ., as quickly as possible (BL, § 11, 3), Act.17:15.

etymology: neuter plural of the superlative of (G5036)

full.text: τάχος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for parts and derivatives of מָהַר ;] swiftness, speed. Adverbially, ἐν τ. (ταχέως), quickly, speedily, soon: Luk.18:8, Act.12:7 22:18 25:4, Rom.16:20, 1Ti.3:14, Rev.1:1 22:6.

ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον (T, Rec. τάχιον), = cl. θᾶσσον, θᾶττον (Att..), (see Bl., § 44, 3): Jhn.13:27 20:4, 1Ti.3:14 (T, Rec.), Heb.13:19, 23 Superl., τάχιστα: ὡς τ., as quickly as possible (BL, § 11, 3), Act.17:15.
ταχύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quick, swift, speedy: opposite to βραδύς, Ja 119.
τέ solitarium, and, denoting a closer affinity than καί between words and sentences which it connects (BL, §77, 8): Mat.28:12, Jhn.4:42, Act.2:33, 37 40 10:22 11:21, al.

Denoting a closer connection than simple καί, τὲ . . . καί, τὲ καί, τὲ . . . τέ (Act.26:16 Rom.14:8), as well . . . as also, both . . . and: Luk.12:45, Act.1:1 15:9, 21:30, al.; τὲ . . . δέ, and . . . and, Act.19:2; τὲ γάρ . . . ὁμοίως δὲ καί, Rom.1:26, 27.

etymology: a primary particle (enclitic) of connection or addition;

full.text: τεῖχος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for חוֹמָה ;] a wall, esp. that about a town: Act.9:25, 2Co.11:33, Heb.11:30, Rev.21:12-19
τεκμήριον, -ου, τό (τέκμαρ, a mark, sign), [in LXX: Wis.5:4 19:13, 3Ma.3:24 ;] a sure sign, a positive proof: Act.1:1-26 (for exx., see MM, xxiv). SYN.: ἔνδειγμα, q.v

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of

full.text: τεκνίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of τέκνον), a little child: as a term of endearment, in voc. pl., Jhn.13:33, Gal.4:19, 1Jn.2:1 Jn 2:12 Jn 2:28 Jn 3:7 Jn 3:13 Jn 4:4 Jn 5:21.
τεκνογονέω, -ῶ to beget or bear children: 1Ti.5:14 (Anthol.).
τεκνογονία, -ας, ἡ, child-bearing: 1Ti.2:15 (Arist.).

τέκνον, -ου, τό (τίκτω), [in LXX chiefly for בֵּן, also for יֶלֶד, etc. ;] that which is begotten, born (cf. Scottish bairn), a child of either sex: Mrk.13:12, Luk.1:7, Act.7:5; pl., Mat.7:11, Mrk.7:27, Luk.1:17, Eph.6:1, al.; τέκνα ἐπαγγελίας, Rom.9:8; τ. τῆς σαρκός, ib.; in a wider sense (as Heb. בָּנִים), of posterity, Mat.2:18, Luk.3:8, al.; specif., of a male child, Mat.21:28, Act.21:21, a,l.; in voc. as a form of kindly address from an elder to a junior or from a teacher to a disciple, Mat...
τεκνο-τροφέω, -ῶ to rear young (of bees, Arist.), to bring up children: 1Ti.5:10.
τέκτων, -ονος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for חָרָשׁ ;] an artificer in wood, stone or metal, but esp. a carpenter (see MM, xxiv): Mat.13:55, Mrk.6:3.

of persons, primarily of physical development, (a) full-grown, mature: Heb.5:14; ethically: Php.3:15; opposite to νήπιος (-άζειν), 1Co.2:6 14:20, Eph.4:13; τ. καὶ πεπληροφορημένοι, Col.4:12; τ. ἐν Χριστῷ, Col.1:28; (b) complete, perfect (expressing the simple idea of complete goodness, without reference either to maturity or to the philosophical idea of a τέλος; see Hort on Jas.1:4): Mat.5:48 19:21, Jas.1:4 3:2; of God, Mat.5:48.

Of things, complete, perfect: Rom.12:2; ἔργον, Jas.1:4; νόμος, Jas.1:25; δώρημα, Jas.1:17; ἀγάπη, 1Jn.4:18; τὸ τ., 1Co.13:10; compar., τελειοτέρα (σκηνή), Heb.9:11. (There is probably no reference in St. Paul's usage to the use of this term in the ancient mysteries; cf. ICC on Col.1:28; but see also Lft., in l, and Notes, 173 f.) SYN.: see: ὁλόκληρος (and cf. Rendall, He., 158 ff.)
τελειότης, -ητος, ἡ (τέλειος), [in LXX: Jdg.9:16, 19 (תָּמִים), Pro.11:3 (תֻּמָּה), Wis.6:15 12:17, Jer.2:2 ;] perfection, completeness: Col.3:14, Heb.6:1
bring to an end, finish, accomplish, fulfil: Jhn.4:34, Act.20:24; of time, Luk.2:43, Jhn.5:36 17:4; pass., ἡ γραφή, Jhn.19:28.
to bring to maturity or completeness, to complete, perfect;

subs.text: of things: Heb.7:19, Jas.2:22, 1Jn.2:5 Jn 4:12 Jn 4:17 :

full.text: τελείως (τέλειος), adv., [in LXX: Jdth.11:6, 2Ma.12:42, 3Ma.3:26 3Mac 7:22, R ;] completely, perfectly: 1Pe.1:13.
τελείωσις, -εως, ἡ (τελειόω), [in LXX chiefly for מִלֻּא, Exo.29:22, al. ;] fulfilment, completion, perfection: Luk.1:45, Heb.7:11.
τελειωτής, -οῦ, ὁ (τελειόω), a consummator, finisher: Heb.12:2 (nowhere else).
τελεσφορέω, -ῶ (τέλος, φέρω) [in LXX: 4Ma.13:20 ;] (a) of plants, to bring fruit to perfection (Theophr.): Luk.8:14 (b) of females, to bear perfect offspring (Artemid.; 4Mac, l.with).
trans., to complete, finish; esp. τ. τ. αἰῶνα, τ. βίον, to complete life, to die (Æsch., Hdt., al.).

Intrans., to come to an end, hence, to die (Hdt., al.): Mat.2:19 9:18 22:25, Mrk.9:48, Luk.7:2, Jhn.11:39, Act.2:29 7:15, Heb.11:22; Hebraistically, θανάτῳ τελευτάτω (מוּת, Exo.21:17), Mat.15:4, Mrk.7:10" (LXX) .
a finishing.
an end: τ. βίου (Hdt., al.): also without βίου, the end of life, death (Hdt., Plat., al.): Mat.2:15.
to bring to an end, complete, finish: τ. δρόμον, 2Ti.4:7; τ. λόγους, Mat.7:28 19:1 26:1; τ. παραβολάς, Mat.13:53; τ. πόλεις, Mat.10:23; pass., Rev.15:8 20:3, 5 20:7; with ptcp., Mat.11:1.
to execute, perform, complete, fulfil: Luk.2:39, Act.13:29, Rom.2:27, 2Co.12:9, Gal.5:16, Jas.2:8, Rev.11:7; pass., Luk.12:50 18:31 22:37, Jhn.19:28, 30, Rev.10:7 15:1 17:17.
to pay (frequently in cl.): Mat.17:24, Rom.13:6 (cf. ἀπο-, δια-, ἐκ-, ἐπι-, συν-τελέω).

end: most frequently of the termination or limit of an act or state (in NT also of the end of a period of time, cl. τελευτή), Luk.1:33, 1Co.10:11, 2Co.3:13, 1Pe.4:7; by meton., of one who makes an end, Rom.10:4; ἕως (ἄχρι, μέκρι) τέλους, 1Co.1:8, 2Co.1:13, Heb.3:14 6:11, Rev.2:26; εἰς τ., to or at the end, Mat.10:22, Mrk.13:13, Luk.18:5, Jhn.13:1 (or here, to the uttermost, see Westc., in l); τ. ἔχειν, Luk.22:37; adverbially, τὸ δὲ τέλος, finally, 1Pe.3:8; of the last in a series, Rev.21:6 22:13; of the issue, fate or destiny, Mat.26:58; with genitive of thing

subs.text: , Rom.6:21, al.; with genitive of person
, 2Co.11:13, al.; of the aim or purpose, 1Ti.1:5.

full.text: toll, custom, revenue: Rom.13:7; pl., as most usually, Mat.17:25
a farmer of taxes (Let. publicanus).

A subordinate of the former, who collected taxes or tolls in a particular district, a tax-gatherer (EV, publican): Mat.5:46 10:3, Luk.3:12 5:27, 29 7:29 18:10-11, 13; pl., τ. κ. ἁμαρτωλοί, Mat.9:10-11 11:19, Mrk.2:15-16, Luk.5:30 7:34 15:1; τ. κ. πόρναι, Mat.21:31-32; ὁ ἐθνικὸς κ. ὁ τ., Mat.18:17 (cf. DB, iv, 172; ext., 394 f.; MM, xxiv).
τελώνιον, -ου, τό (τελώνης), a custom house (so in MGr.), tollhouse, place of toll: Mat.9:9, Mrk.2:14, Luk.5:27.

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of (G5057)

full.text: τέρας, -ατος, τό [in LXX chiefly for מוֹפֵת ;] a wonder, marvel: in NT always pl., τ. κ. σημεῖα, Mat.24:24, al. (see: σημεῖον)
Τέρτιος, -ου, ὁ Tertius: Rom.16:22.
Τέρτυλλος, -ου, ό Tertullus: Act.2:1-2.
τεσσεράκοντα (Rec. τεσσαρ-, v, WH, App., 150; M, Pr., 45 f.; Thackeray, Gr., 62 f., 73 f.), οἱ, αἱ, τά, indecl., forty: Mat.4:2, Mrk.1:13, Luk.4:2, Jhn.2:20, Act.1:3, al
τεσσερακονταετίς (Τ, -έτης; Rec. τεσσαρ-, see supr.), -ές of forty years, forty years old: Act.7:23 13:18.

τέσσαρες (and Ion. and late -ερεν, and late accusative -εν; see WH, App., 150; M, Pr., 36, 45 f.), οἱ, αἱ, -αρα, τά, genitive, -ων, four: Mat.24:31, Mrk.2:3, Luk.2:37, Jhn.11:17 19:23, Act.10:11, Rev.4:4, 6, al
τεσσαρεσ-και-δέκατος, -η, -ον fourteenth: Act.27:27, 33.
τεταρταῖος, -α, -ον (τέταρτος), [in LXX: 2Ki.3:4 A (רְבִיעִי) ;] of or on the fourth day: τ. εἶναι (Hdt., τ. γενέσθαι), to be four days dead, Jhn.11:39.
τέταρτος, -η, -ον [in LXX chiefly for רְבִיעִי ;] fourth: Mat.14:25, Mrk.6:48, Act.10:30, Rev.4:7 6:7-8 8:12 16:8 21:19.
τετράγωνος, -ον (τετρα-, see supr., + γωνία), [in LXX for רָבַע and cogn. forms ;] square: Rev.21:16 (Hdt., Plat., al.).
τετράδιον, ου, τό a quarternion, a group of four: στρατιωτῶν, Act.12:4 (Philo).

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative of

full.text: τετρακισ-χίλιοι, -αι, -α four thousand: Mat.15:38 16:10, Mrk.8:9, 20, Act.21:38.
τετρακόσιοι, -αι, -α, four hundred: Act.5:36 7:6" (LXX) 13:20, Gal.3:17.
τετράμηνος, -ον [in LXX (neut.): Jdg.19:2 A Jdg.20:47 A (עַרְבָּעָה חֳדָשִׁים ) ;] of four months, four months; as subst., τ. (sc. ὥρα, but neut. in Rec.), Jhn.4:35 (Thuc., al.).
τετρα-πλόος, -η, -ον (-οῦς, -ῆ, -οῦν) fourfold: Luk.19:8.
τετρά-πους, -ουν [in LXX chiefly for בְּהֵמָה ;] four-footed: of beasts, neut. pl., Act.10:12 11:6, Rom.1:23.
τετρααρχέω (Rec. τετραρχ-; see WH, App., 145), -ῶ (τετραάρχης), to be tetrarch: with genitive, Luk.3:1 (FlJ, BJ, iii, 10, 7).

τετραάρχης (Rec. τετράρχης, see supr.), -ου, ὁ (τετρα- in corp. = τέτορα, Doric for τέσσαρα, + ἄρχω), a tetrarch, i.e. (a) prop., the governor of a fourth part of a region (Strab.); (b) any petty ruler (Plut.); in NT, of Herod Antipas: Mat.14:1, Luk.3:19 9:7, Act.13:1.
τεφρόω, -ῶ (τέφρα, ashes; Wis.2:3, al.), to burn to ashes: 2Pe.2:6.
τέχνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for חׇכְמָה, מַעֲשֶׂה, עֲבֹדָה ;] art, craft, trade: Act.17:29 18:3, Rev.18:22 (WH, R, mg., om.).
τεχνίτης, -ου, ὁ (τέχνη), [in LXX for חָרָשׁ, etc. ;] a craftsman, artificer: Act.19:24, 38, Rev.18:22; of God (Wis.13:1), Heb.11:10. SYN.: δημιουργός, which see, and cf. Tr., Syn., § cv
τήκω [in LXX for מָסַס ni., מָקַק ni., מוּג ni., etc. ;] trans., to melt, melt down; pass., to melt, melt away: 2Pe.3:12.
τηλαυγῶς adv. (τῆλε, afar, + αὐγή, radiance), poët. and in late prose, at a distance clearly: Mrk.8:25 (W, mg., δηλαυγῶς).

etymology: adverb from a compound of a derivative of (G5056) (G0827)

full.text: of persons,

subs.text: of such an age, so old;
so young.

full.text: Of things, so great: 2Co.1:10, Heb.2:3, Jas.3:4, Rev.16:18.

to watch over, guard, keep, preserve: Mat.27:36, Act.16:23, al.; ἑαυτόν, 2Co.11:9, 1Ti.5:22, Jas.1:27, Ju 21; before εἰς, Jhn.12:7, Act.25:21, 1Pe.1:4, 2Pe.2:4 2:9 3:7, Ju 6; ἐν, Jhn.17:11-12, Ju 21; ἐκ, Jhn.17:15, Rev.3:10; τ. πίστιν, 2Ti.4:7, Rev.14:12; τ. ἑνότητα τ. πνεύματος, Eph.4:3.

to watch, give heed to, observe: τ. σάββατον, Jhn.9:16; τ. ἐντολήν (-άς), Mat.19:17, Jhn.14:15 15:10, 1Ti.6:14, 1Jn.2:3-4 Jn 3:22 Jn 3:24 Jn 5:3, Rev.12:17 14:12; τ. λόγον (-ους), Jhn.8:51-52, 55 14:23-24 15:20 17:6, 1Jn.2:5, Rev.3:8, 10 22:7, 9; τ. νόμον, Act.15:5, Jas.2:10; τ. παράδοσιν, Mrk.7:9; τ. ἔργα, Rev.2:26; τ. γεγραμμένα, Rev.1:3 (cf. δια-, παρα-, συν-τηρέω) SYN.: φυλάσσω, implying custody and protection: τ. expresses the idea of watchful care and "may mark the result of which φ. is the means" (Thayer see word)
a watching, hence, imprisonment, ward (see Deiss., BS, 267): Act.4:3 5:18.
a keeping: τ. ἐντολῶν (cf. Sir, Wis, ll. with, and see Westc. on 1Jn.2:3.), 1Co.7:19.
Τιβεριάς, -άδος, ἡ (Τιβέριος), Tiberias, a city of Galilee: Jhn.6:23, Jhn.6:1 21:1 (cf Γεννησσαρέτ, Γαλιλαία).
Τιβέριος, -ου, ὁ, the Emperor Tiberius: Luk.3:1.
causative of κεῖμαι,

subs.text: to put down, lay down: of bending the knees, τ. γόνατα, to kneel, Mrk.15:19, Act.7:60, al.; of putting off garments, Jhn.13:4; of laying down life, τ. ψυχήν, Jhn.10:11, 15 17, 18 13:37, 38 15:13, 1Jn.3:16; of laying by money, παρ ̓ ἑαυτῷ, 1Co.16:2; of setting on food, Jhn.2:10; metaphorically, of setting forth an idea in symbolism, Mrk.4:30.

full.text: to set, fix, establish: ὑπόδειγμα, 2Pe.2:6.

to make, appoint: Mat.22:44, Mrk.12:36, Luk.20:43, Act.2:35, Rom.4:17, al. Mid., to make, set or appoint for oneself: Act.20:28, 1Co.12:28, 1Th.5:9, 1Ti.1:12, al.; before ἵνα, Jhn.15:16 (cf . ἀνα-, προσ-ανα-, ἀπο-, δια-, ἀντι-δια-, ἐκ-, ἐπι-, συν-επι-, κατα-, συν-κατα-, μετα-, παρα-, περι-, προ-, προσ-, συν-, ὑπο-τίθημι).

etymology: a prolonged form of a primary

full.text: τίκτω [in LXX chiefly for יָלַד ;] prop., of parents, to beget, bring forth, but esp. the latter, and so always in NT: absol., Luk.1:57 2:6, Jhn.16:21, Gal.4:27" (LXX) (see M, Pr., 127), Rev.12:2, 4; υἱόν, Mat.1:21, 23 1:25 Luk.1:31 2:7, Rev.12:5, 13; pass., Mat.2:2, Luk.2:11. Metaphorical, of the earth, βοτάνην, Heb.6:7 (Æsch., al.); of lust, ἁμαρτίαν, Jas.1:15.

etymology: a strengthened form of a primary

full.text: τίλλω [in LXX: Est.9:3, Isa.18:7, Dan LXX 7:4 (מָרַט) ;] to pluck, pluck off: στάχυας, Mat.12:1, Mrk.2:23, Luk.6:1.

etymology: perhaps akin to the alternate of (G0138) (G4951)

full.text: Τιμαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Aram. טִימַי (?), see Zorell, see word), Timæus: Mrk.10:46.
to fix the value, price: with accusative of person(s), of Christ, Mat.27:9 (LXX).

to honour: with accusative of person(s), Mat.15:4 (LXX), Mat.15:5, 8" (LXX) Mat.19:19 (LXX), Mrk.7:6, 10 10:19, Luk.18:28" (LXX), Jhn.5:23 8:49 12:26, Eph.6:2" (LXX), 1Ti.5:3, 1Pe.2:17; πολλαῖς τιμαῖς, Act.28:10.
a price paid or received: with genitive of person

subs.text: , Mat.27:9; with genitive of thing
, Act.5:2-3; pl., Act.4:34 19:19; τ. αἵματος, Mat.27:6; ἠγοράσθητε τιμῆς, 1Co.6:20 7:23; ὠνεῖσθαι τιμῆς ἀργυρίου, Act.7:16.

full.text: esteem, honour: Rom.12:10 13:7, 1Co.12:23, 1Ti.5:17 6:1, Heb.5:4, 1Pe.2:7 (R, txt., preciousness, cf. Hort, in l), 1Pe.3:7; τ. διδόναι, 1Co.12:24; ἔχειν, Jhn.4:44, Heb.3:3; τ. καὶ δόξα (δ. κ. τ.), Rom.2:7, 10, 1Ti.1:17, Heb.2:7" (LXX), Heb.2:9, 1Pe.1:7, 2Pe.1:17, Rev.4:9, 11 5:12-13 7:12 21:26; τ. καὶ κράτος, 1Ti.6:16; εἰς τ., Rom.9:21, 2Ti.2:20-21; ἐν τ., Col.2:23; 1Th.4:4; by meton., of marks of honour, Act.28:10.

τίμιος, -α, -ον (τιμή), [in LXX chiefly for יָקָר ;] valued, hence, (a) precious, costly, highly valued: primarily, of money value, λίθος, Rev.17:4 18:12, 16 21:19; pl., 1Co.3:12; compar., -ώτεφος, 1Pe.1:7, Rec.; superl., -ώτατος, Rev.18:12 21:11; in extended sense, καρπός, Jas.5:7; αἷμα, 1Pe.1:19; ἐπαγγέλματα, 2Pe.1:4; (b) held in honour, honoured, esteemed worthy: with dative, Act.5:34; ὁ γάμος, Heb.13:4; ψυχή, Act.20:24 (where τ. is pleonastic, see Page, in l).
τιμιότης, -ητος, ἡ (τίμιος), preciousness, worth: Rev.18:19 (cf. Hort on τιμή, 1Pe.2:7).
Τιμόθεος, -ου, ὁ Timothy: Act.17:14-15 18:5, Rom.16:21, al.
Τίμων, -ωνος, ὁ Timon: Act.6:5.
to help.
to avenge; mid., to avenge oneself on, punish (Hdt., Eur., al): act, in this sense, Act.22:5 26:11 (see MM, xxiv).
help, assistance.
vengeance, punishment: Heb.10:29. SYN.: κόλασις, which see, and cf. MM, xxiv
τίνω [in LXX: Pro.27:12 (τ. ζημίαν, for עָנַשׁ ni.), etc. ;] to pay: t. δίκην (cf. Pr, l.with), to pay penalty, 2Th.1:9 (see M, Th., in l).
one, a certain one: Luk.9:49, Jhn.11:1, Act.5:25, al.; pl., τίνες, certain, some: Luk.13:1, Act.15:1, Rom.3:8, al.

someone, anyone, something, anything: Mat.12:29, Mrk.9:30, Luk.8:46, Jhn.2:25, Act.17:25, Rom.5:7, al.; = indef., one (French on), Mrk.8:4, Jhn.2:25, Rom.8:24, al.; pl., τινες, some, Mrk.14:4, al. II. II. As adj.,
a certain: Mat.18:12, Luk.1:5 8:27, Act.3:2, al.; with proper names, Mrk.15:21, Luk.23:26, al.; with genitive partit., Luk.7:19, al.

1. masc., fem.: τίς; who, what?, Mat.3:7 26:68; Mrk.11:28, Luk.9:9, al. mult.; with genitive partit., Act.7:52, Heb.1:5, al; before ἐκ (= genitive partit.), Mat.6:27, Luk.14:28, Jhn.8:46; = ποῖος, Mrk.4:41 6:2, Luk.19:3, Act.17:19, al.; = πότερος (M, Pr., 77), Mat.21:31 27:17, Luk.22:27, al.; = ὅς or ὅστις (rare in cl.; cf. Bl., §50, 5; M, Pr., 93), Act.13:25.

Neut.: τί; what?, Mat.5:47 11:7, Mrk.10:3, al.; χάριν τίνος, 1Jn.3:12; διὰ τί, Mat.9:11, al.; εἰς τί, Mat.14:31, al.; elliptically, ἵνα τί (sc. γένηται), why, Mat.9:5, al.; τί οὖν, Rom.3:9 6:1, 15 1Co.14:15, al.; τί γάρ, Rom.3:3, Phi 1:18; τί ἐμοὶ (ὑμῖν) καὶ σοί, see: ἔγω. II. As adj.: who? what? which?, Mat.5:46, Luk.14:31, Jhn.2:18, al. III. As adv.: = διὰ τι (τί ὅτι), why, Mat.6:28, Mrk.4:40, Luk.6:46, Jhn.18:23, al.; in rhet. questions, = a negation, Mat.27:4, Jhn.21:22, 23 1Co.5:12 7:16, al. in exclamations (like Heb. מָה), how (2Ki.6:20, Psa.3:2, al.), Luk.12:49.
τίτλος, -ου, ὁ (Lat. titulus), [in Aq., Sm., Th.: Jer.21:4 ;] a title, inscription: Jhn.19:19-20 (in Christian Inscr., epitaph, see MM. xxiv).
St. Paul's disciple and companion: 2Co.2:13 7:6 7:13-14 8:6 8:16 8:23 12:18, Gal.2:1, 3, 2Ti.4:10, Tit.1:4, subscr., Rec. (Τῖτος).
One surnamed Justus: Act.18:7, RV (T, WH, Τίτιος).

etymology: of Latin origin but uncertain significance;

full.text: enclit. Particle, serving to express belief in an assertion, let me tell you, surely, verily , used to express an inference, then, consequently , (Homer); and in [variant datesTragica Adespota, to introduce a general sentiment.
to strengthen other Particles, γάρ τοι, ἤτοι, καίτοι, μέντοι, τοιγάρτοι, etc.: compare τἆρα, τἄν, μεντἄν.

etymology: probably for the dative case of (G3588)

full.text: τοι-γαρ-οῦν an inferential particle, [in LXX: Job.22:10 24:22, Sir.41:16, al. ;] wherefore then, so therefore: 1Th.4:8, Heb.12:1 (Hat., Plat., al.).

τοί-νυν an inferential particle, [in LXX: Isa.3:10 5:13 27:4, Wis.1:11 8:9, a1. ;] accordingly, therefore: as in cl., after the first word in a sentence, 1Co.9:26; accusative to later usage, at the beginning, Luk.20:25, Heb.13:13.

antecedent to οἷος, as ἀοιδοῦ τοιοῦδ᾽ οἷος ὅδ᾽ ἐστί of such a minstrel as is this one, (Odyssey by Homer); absolute, ἀλλ᾽ ὅδ᾽ ἐγὼ τοιόσδε here am I such as you see , (Odyssey by Homer):—;also, so great, so noble, so bad , τοιάδε λαίφη such clothes, i. e. so bad , (Odyssey by Homer); τοσόσδε καὶ τοιόσδε (Herdotus Historicus):—;also with a qualifying word, τοιόσδ᾽ ἠμὲν δέμας ἠδὲ καὶ ἔργα such both in form and works, (Odyssey by Homer):—;with the Art., ὁ τ. ἀνήρ (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc.; οἱ τοιοί...

etymology: (including the other inflections); from a derivative of 5104 and (G1161)

full.text: As subst., anarth: pl., Luk.9:9; with art., ὁ τ., such a one (Bl., § 47, 9; Ellic. on Gal.5:21): Act.22:22, 1Co.5:5, al.; pl., Mat.19:14, Mrk.10:14, al.; neut. pl., Act.19:25, Rom.1:32, al
τοῖχος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for קִיר ;] a wall, esp. of a house: figuratively, Act.23:3 (cf. τεῖχος).

τόκος, -ου, ὁ (τίκτω), [in LXX for נֶשֶׁךְ, etc. ;] (a) a bringing forth, birth; (b) offspring. Metaphorical, of the produce of money lent out, interest, usury (cf. Merch. of Venice, I, iii, "a breed of barren metal "): Mat.25:27, Luk.19:23 (Soph., Plat., al.).

τολμάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.15:12 (לָקַח), Est.1:18 7:5 (לֵב מָלֵא) Jdth.14:13, 2Ma.4:2, 3Ma.3:21 R, 4Ma.8:18 ;] to have courage, to venture, dare, be bold: absol, 2Co.11:21; before ἐπί, 2Co.10:2; with inf. (Bl., § 69, 4), Mat.22:46, Mrk.12:34, Luk.20:40, Jhn.21:12, Act.5:13 7:32, Rom.15:18, 1Co.6:1, 2Co.10:12, Php.1:14, Ju 9; to submit to (in cl. usually absol. in this sense), Rom.5:7 (see Field, Notes, 155); τολμήσας εἰσῆλθεν, took courage and went in (see Field, op. cit., 44), Mrk.15:43 (cf. ἀπο-...
τολμηρῶς adv., (τολμηρός, bold, daring), boldly: compar., -ότερως (T, Rec. -ότερον), Rom.15:15.

etymology: neuter of the compound of a derivative of the base of 5111 (as adverb);

full.text: τολμητής, -οῦ, ὁ (τολμάω), a bold, daring man: 2Pe.2:10 (Thuc.).
τομός, -ή, -όν (τέμνω), sharp: compar., -ώτερος, metaphorically, Heb.4:12.

etymology: comparative of a derivative of the primary

full.text: τόξον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for קֶשֶׁת ;] a bow: Rev.6:2.
τοπάζιον, -ου, τό (and τόπαζος, ὁ), [in LXX: Exo.28:17 35:17 (Exo.39:10), Job.28:19, Eze.28:13 (פִּטְדָה), Psa.119:127 (פָּז) ;] topaz: Rev.21:20 (see Swete, in l).

etymology: neuter of a presumed derivative (alternately) of

full.text: τόπος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for מָקוֹם ;] place: Luk.4:37 10:1, 32 Jhn.5:13 6:10, Act.12:17, 1Co.1:2, al.; τ. ἅγιος (cf. Isa.60:13), Mat.24:15; ἔρημος, Mat.14:13, al.; πεδινός, Luk.6:17; ἄνυδρος (pl.), Mat.12:43, Luk.11:24; κατὰ τόπος (BV, in divers places), Mat.24:7, Mrk.13:8; τραχεῖς τ., Act.27:29; τ. διθάλασσος (which see), ib. 41; ἑτοιμάζειν τ., Jhn.14:2, 3; ἔχειν, Rev.12:6; διδόναι, Luk.14:9; with genitive defin., τ. βασάνου, Luk.16:28; τ. καταπαύσεως, Act.7:49; κρανίου, Mat.27:33, Mrk.1...

τοσοῦτος, -αύτη, -οῦτο (Heb.7:22) and (elsewhere, as usually in Attic) -οῦτον, correlat. of ὅσος, of quantity, size, number, so great, so much, pl., so many: Mat.8:10 Luk.7:9, Heb.12:1, Rev.18:7, 16; of time, so long, χρόνος, Jhn.14:9, Heb.4:7; pl., Luk.15:29 (ἔτη), Jhn.12:37 21:11, 1Co.14:10; before ὥστε, Mat.15:33; absol.: pl., Jhn.6:9, Gal.3:4; of price, τοσούτου, Act.5:8; dative, τοσούτῳ κρείττων, Heb.1:4; τ. μᾶλλον ὅσῳ, Heb.10:25; καθ᾽ ὅσον . . . κατὰ τ., Heb.7:22.

τότε, demonstr. adv. of time, correlat. of ὅτε, then, at that time; (a) of concurrent events: Mat.2:17 3:5 and freq., Rom.6:21; before ptcp., Mat.2:16, Gal.4:8; opposite to νῦν, Gal.4:29, Heb.12:26; ὁ τ. κόσμος, 2Pe.3:6; (b) of consequent events, then, thereupon: Mat.2:7 3:5 4:1 and freq., Luk.11:26; τ. οὖν, Jhn.11:14 19:1, 16 20:8; εὐθέως τ., Act.17:14; ὅτε . . . τ., Mat.13:26 21:1, Jhn.12:16; ἀπὸ τ., Mat.4:17 16:21 26:16, Luk.16:16; (with) of things future: Mat.24:28, 40 25:1, 31ff.; opposite ...
- sometimes used for G5127; of this person
τοὐναντίον by crasis for τὸ ἐνανίον, neut. accusative, used adverbially, on the contrary, contrariwise: 2Co.2:7, Gal.2:7, 1Pe.3:9.
τοὔνομα by crasis for τὸ ὄνομα, accusative absol., by name: Mat.27:57.
that is that is to say, by this we mean

as subst., this one, he; (a) absol.: Mat.3:17, Mrk.9:7, Luk.7:44, 45, Jhn.1:15, Act.2:15, al.; expressing contempt (cl.), Mat.13:55, 56, Mrk.6:2, 3, Jhn.6:42, al.; εἰς τοῦτο, Mrk.1:38, Rom.14:9; μετὰ τοῦτο (ταῦτα; V. etymology: genitive case plural masculine or neuter of (G3778)

full.text: τράγος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עַתּוּד, also for צָפִיר, תַּיִשׁ ;] a he-goat: Heb.9:12-13, 19 10:4.

a table, dining-table: Mat.15:27, Mrk.7:28, Luk.16:21 19:23 22:21, 30; of the table of shewbread, Heb.9:2. By meton., of food provided (see DB, iv, 670 a): Act.16:34, Rom.11:9" (LXX) 1Co.10:21; διακονεῖν ταῖς τ., Act.6:2 (cf. Page, in l, but see infr.).
A money-changer's table, a bank (Dem., Arist., al.): Mat.21:12, Mrk.11:15, Luk.19:23, Jhn.2:15; so also accusative to Dr. Field (Notes, 113), Act.6:2 (but see supr.).
τραπεζείτης (Rec. -ζίτης, as in cl.), -ου, ὁ (τράπεζα), a money­changer, banker: Mat.25:27 (Dem., Plut., al.).
τραῦμα, -τος, τό [in LXX for פָּצַע, חָלַל, etc. ;] a wowed: Luk.10:34.
τραυματίζω (τραῦμα), [in LXX chiefly for חָלַל ;] to wound: Luk.20:12, Act.19:16.

τραχηλίζω (τράχηλος), prop., of wrestlers, to take by the throat, hence (Philo), to prostrate, overthrow. Metaphorical, but in what sense and from what age it is doubtful (see Westc., Rendall, on He, l.with; DB, iii, 625n), τραχηλισμένα, laid open (RV, Westc.; downcast, Rendall): Heb.4:13.

τράχηλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for צַוַּאר, also forעֹרֶף, etc. ;] the neck: Mat.18:6, Mrk.9:42, Luk.17:2; ἐπιπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τὸν τ., to embrace (Gen.46:29), Luk.15:20, Act.20:37. Metaphorical, ὑποθεῖναι τὸν ἑαυτοῦ τ. (for similar phrase in π., see Zorell, see word), Rom.16:4; ἐπιθεῖναι ζυγὸν ἐπὶ τὸν τ., Act.15:10.
τραχύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX: Isa.40:4 (רֶכֶס), etc. ;] rough: ὁδοί (Is, l.with), Luk.3:5 (LXX); τοποί (i.e. rocky), Act.27:29.

etymology: perhaps strengthened from the base of (G4486)

full.text: Τραχωνῖτις, -ιδος, ἡ (τραχύς) Trachonitis, a rough region S. of Damascus (in FlJ, sometimes ἡ Τ., sometimes ό Τράχων): ἡ Τ. χώρα, Luk.3:1.

τρεῖς, οἱ, αἱ, τρία, τά three: Mat.12:40, al.; μετὰ τ. ἡμέρας = τῇ τρίτῃ ἡ., Mrk.10:34, al. (cf. Field, Notes, 11 ff.) Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι (see: ταβέρνη), Three Taverns (Lit. Tres Tabernae) a halting place on the Appian Way: Act.28:15 (see DB, iv, 690).
τρέμω to tremble, esp. with fear: Mrk.5:33, Luk.8:47; with ptcp. (in cl more frequently with inf.), 2Pe.2:10.
to make to grow, bring up, rear (cl.; 1Ma.3:33 1Mac 11:39): Luk.4:16 (T, WH, mg., ἀνατ-)

to nourish, feed: Mat.6:26 25:37, Luk.12:24, Act.12:20, Rev.12:6, 14; of a mother, to give suck, Luk.23:29; of animals, to fatten (Jer.26:21 (Jer.46:21), figuratively, Jas.5:5 (cf. ἀνα-, ἐκ-, ἐν-τρέφω).

τρέχω [in LXX chiefly for רוּץ ;] to run: Mrk.5:6, Jhn.20:2, 4, 1Co.9:24 9:26; with inf., Mat.28:8; before ἐπί, Luk.24:12 [T [WH] R, mg., om.]; εἰς, Rev.9:9; ptcp., δραμών, with indic., Mat.27:48, Mrk.15:36, Luk.15:20. Metaphorical, from runners in a race, of swiftness or of effort to attain an end: Rom.9:16, Gal.2:2 5:7, Php.2:16; τ. ἀγῶνα (Hdt., Eur., al.), Heb.12:1; ὁ λόγος τ. κυρίου (cf. Psa.147:4), ἕως τάχους δραμεῖται ὁ λ. αὐτοῦ), 2Th.3:1 (cf. εἰς-, κατα-, περι-, προ-, προσ-, συν-, ἐπι-συν...
τριάκοντα, ἱο, αἱ, τά indecl. (τρεῖς), thirty: Mat.13:8, al.
τριακόσιοι, -αι, -α three hundred: Mrk.14:5, Jhn.12:5.
τρίβολος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Gen.3:18, Hos.10:8 (דַּרְדַּר), 2Ki.12:31 (חָרִיץ), Pro.22:5 (צֵן) ;] a thistle: Mat.7:16, Heb.6:8.
τρίβος, -ου, -ἡ (τρίβω), [in LXX for מְסִלָּה, נָתִיב, etc. ;] a beaten track, a path: Mat.3:3, Mrk.1:3, Luk.3:4" (LXX) .
τριετία, -ας, ἡ (τρεῖς, ἔτος), a period of three years: Act.20:31.
prop., of sounds by animals, to cry, chirp, etc.
Of other sounds, e.g., trans., τ. ὀδόντας, to gnash or grind the teeth: Mrk.9:18.

τρίμηνος, -ον (τρεῖς, μήν) [in LXX (neut.): Gen.38:24, 4Ki.23:31 B 4Ki.24:8, 2Ch.36:2 36:9 (חֹדֶשׁ שָׁלוֹשׁ) ;] of three months; as subst., τὸ τ. (= cl. ἡ τ.), a space of three months (Polyb., al.; LXX): Heb.11:23.
τρίστεγος, -ον (τρεῖς, στέγη) [in Sm.: Gen.6:17(16), Eze.42:6 ;] of three stories: τὸ τ. (sc. ὄκημα), the third story, Act.20:9 (Dion., FlJ, al.).
three thousand , (Iliad by Homer), etc.

τρίτος, -η, -ον the third: Mat.22:26, Mrk.12:21 15:25, Luk.24:21, Act.2:15, al.; τῇ τ. ἡμέρᾳ (i.e. the next day but one: Xen., al.; see Field, Notes, 11 ff.), Mat.16:21, Luk.24:46, al.; substantively, accusative masc., τρίτον, a third (servant), Luk.20:12; neut., τὸ τ., with genitive, the third part of: Rev.8:7-12 9:15, 18 12:4. As adv., τὸ τ., the third time: Mrk.14:41, Jhn.21:17; anarth., τρίτον, a third time, Luk.23:22, Jhn.21:14, 2Co.12:14 13:1; in enumerations, thirdly, 1Co.12:28; ἐκ τ., a ...
τρίχινος, -η, -ον (θρίξ), [in LXX: Zec.13:4 (שֵׂעָר), Exo.26:7 ;] of hair: στόκος, Rev.6:12 (Xen., Plat., al.).

τρόμος, -ου, ὁ (τρέμω), [in LXX for רַעַד, רַעַד, פַּחַד, etc. ;] trembling, quaking, esp. from fear: Mrk.16:8; φόβος κ. τ. (as in Gen.9:2, Exo.15:16, Deu.2:25, Isa.19:16, al.), 1Co.2:3, 2Co.7:15, Eph.6:5, Php.2:12.
τροπή, -ῆς, ἡ (τρέπω), [in LXX: Deu.33:14, Job.38:33, Wis.7:18, al. ;] a turning: esp. of the revolution of heavenly bodies, figuratively, Jas.1:17 (see: ἀποσκίασμα).

a way, manner, fashion: Mat.23:37, Luk.13:34, Act.1:11 7:28, 2Ti.3:8, Ju 7; καθ᾽ ὃν τ., Act.15:11 27:25 (ef. MM, xxv); κατὰ πάντα τ., Rom.3:2; κατὰ μηδένα τ. (4Ma.4:24, al.), 2Th.2:3; (ἐν) παντὶ τ., Php.1:18, 2Th.3:16
Of persons, manner of life, character (Hdt., Æsch., al.): Heb.13:5.
